yoruba: “Ní báyìí a ní àwọn ẹku olóṣù mẹ́rin tí kò ní dayabẹ́tìsì tẹ́lẹ̀tẹ́lẹ̀ tó ti ní ayabẹ́tìsì̀,” o ṣe àfikún.
english: "We now have 4-month-old mice that are non-diabetic that used to be diabetic," he added.

yoruba: Dókítà Ehud, akọ́ṣẹmọṣẹ́ ìmọ̀ ogun ní ilé ẹ̀kọ́ gíga Dalhousie ní Halifax, Nova Scotia àti alága ìpín ti ìṣègùn àti ìmọ̀ síẹ̀nsì ti Ẹgbé Ìtọ ṣúggà ti Ará Ìlú Canada sọ pé ìwádìí náà ṣì jẹ́ tuntun.
english: Dr. Ehud Ur, professor of medicine at Dalhousie University in Halifax, Nova Scotia and chair of the clinical and scientific division of the Canadian Diabetes Association cautioned that the research is still in its early days.

yoruba: Bí àwọn àkọ́ṣẹ́mọṣẹ́, ó ṣiyèméjì bóyá àrùn ìtọ ṣúgá lè ní ìwòsàn, tí ó sì mọ̀ pé kò wúlò fún àwọn tó bá ní àrùn ìtọ ṣúgá Type 1.
english: Like some other experts, he is skeptical about whether diabetes can be cured, noting that these findings have no relevance to people who already have Type 1 diabetes.

yoruba: Ní ọjọ́ Ajé, Sara Danius, akọ̀wé àgbà ti ẹgbẹ́ Nobel fún ìtàn àròsọ ní ilé ẹ̀ka Swedin, tí wọ́n kéde ní ètò kan lóri rẹ́díò lóri rẹ́díò Svergies ní Sweden, àwọn ẹgbk kò lè dé ọ̀dọ̀ BobDylan nípa bíborí ní 2016 Nobel Prize Literature, ó ti rẹ̀ wọ́n láti kàn si.
english: On Monday, Sara Danius, permanent secretary of the Nobel Committee for Literature at the Swedish Academy, publicly announced during a radio program on Sveriges Radio in Sweden the committee, unable to reach Bob Dylan directly about winning the 2016 Nobel Prize in Literature, had abandoned its efforts to reach him.

yoruba: Daniusi wipe, “Nisin a o se nkankan. Mo ti meeli ranse mo de ti pe si awon ajo sise timo timo re won de fun mi lesi to loyaya. Fun igba yi, o daniloju pe iyen ti to.”
english: Danius said, "Right now we are doing nothing. I have called and sent emails to his closest collaborator and received very friendly replies. For now, that is certainly enough."

yoruba: Tẹ́lẹ̀rí, olùdarí àgbà iléeṣẹ́ Ring, Jamie Siminoff ní iléeṣẹ́ náà bẹ̀rẹ̀ iṣẹ́ nígbà tí aago ẹnu ọ̀nà òhun kò dún sókè látinú ṣọ́ọ̀bù dé inú gáràjì.
english: Previously, Ring's CEO, Jamie Siminoff, remarked the company started when his doorbell wasn't audible from his shop in his garage.

yoruba: O ko ago ilekun WiFi, O wi.
english: He built a WiFi door bell, he said.

yoruba: Siminoff sọ pé ọjà lọ sókè lẹ́yìn tí òun hàn nínú eré Shark Tank lọ́dún 2013, ní ibi tí àwọn aláṣẹ eré ti ṣẹ́ pẹ̀ẹ́ lórí ìkówó lé eré ìbẹ̀rẹ̀.
english: Siminoff said sales boosted after his 2013 appearance in a Shark Tank episode where the show panel declined funding the startup.

yoruba: Ni eba ipari odun 2017, Siminoff fi ara han lori itakun mohun maworan ti awon ontaja QVC.
english: In late 2017, Siminoff appeared on shopping television channel QVC.

yoruba: Òrùká sì parí ęjọ́ pẹ̀lú ìlé Iṣẹ́ aláàbò tí wọ́n jọ ń figagbága- Ile Iṣẹ́ Nlá ADT.
english: Ring also settled a lawsuit with competing security company, the ADT Corporation.

yoruba: Nígbàtí àwárí òògùn kan fojú hàn láti dín ikú èbólà kù, títí di àsìkò yìí, kò tí i sí òògùn kan pàtó tí a sàfihàn pé ó lágbára láti tọ́jú ààrùn tó wà nílẹ̀.
english: While one experimental vaccine appears able to reduce Ebola mortality, up until now, no drugs have been clearly demonstrated suitable for treating existing infection.

yoruba: Àmúlùmálà apakòkòrò kan, ZMapp, ṣàfihàn ìlérí ní pápá, ṣùgbọ́n àwọn ẹ̀kọ́ ti tẹ́lẹ́ tóka pé o ní àǹfàní tó kéré láti dènà ikú.
english: One antibody cocktail, ZMapp, initially showed promise in the field, but formal studies indicated it had less benefit than sought in preventing death.

yoruba: Ní ìtọ́wò PALM ZMapp jẹ́ gẹ́gẹ́ bí ìṣàkóso o túmọ̀ si wípé àwọn onímọ̀ sayensi fi ṣe gbendeke wọ́n dẹ̀ fi wé àwọn ogun mẹ́ta ìyóku.
english: In the PALM trial, ZMapp served as a control, meaning scientists used it as a baseline and compared the three other treatments to it.

yoruba: Ẹgbẹ́ Ajìjàkadi ilẹ̀ Àmẹ́ríkà fọwọ́ sí lẹtà ìgbìmọ̀ Olympics, wọ́n sì gbà á wọlé pé Àpapọ̀-Ebi-Olympics nilò láti rí dájú pé wọ́n pèsè àyíká tí ó dára fún àwọn oníjàkadì/asáré-ìje wa gbogbo.
english: USA Gymnastics supports the United States Olympic Committee's letter and accepts the absolute need of the Olympic family to promote a safe environment for all of our athletes.

yoruba: A gba ọ̀rọ̀ USOC pé a lẹ̀ sin ànfàní àwọn eléré ìdárayá wa àti àwọn ẹgbe ìdárayá, àti eré ìdárayá wọn tí a bá tẹ̀síwájú pẹ̀lú ayipada tó ní túmọ̀ ní ilé ìṣe wa, ju yiyo ìwé aṣe kuro.
english: We agree with the USOC's statement that the interests of our athletes and clubs, and their sport, may be better served by moving forward with meaningful change within our organization, rather than decertification.

yoruba: Eréje Gymnastics ti ilẹ̀ USA sàtìlẹyìn fún ìwádìí olómìnira tí ó lè tan ìmọ́lẹ̀ lórí àsìlò ìpín ní àwọn tó móríye ìsẹ̀lẹ̀ Larry Nassar sàpéjúwe pẹ̀lú akitiyan. ní kò bá ti lọ láì rí fún ọjọ́ pípẹ́ àti gbígba àwọn àyípadà tó yẹ.
english: USA Gymnastics supports an independent investigation that may shine light on how abuse of the proportion described so courageously by the survivors of Larry Nassar could have gone undetected for so long and embraces any necessary and appropriate changes.

yoruba: Àwọn Asángun ilẹ̀ USA ati USOC ní àfojúsùn kanáà — èyí tó mún kí ààbò eré ìdárayá ìsangun àtawọn míran wà fáwọn eléréje láti le ma siṣẹ́ tọ àlá wọn ní àyíká tó láàbò, tó dára tó sì seégbé.
english: USA Gymnastics and the USOC have the same goal — making the sport of gymnastics, and others, as safe as possible for athletes to follow their dreams in a safe, positive and empowered environment.

yoruba: Ni gbogbo 1960S Bresinski sise fun Johannu F Kenedi gege bi onimoran re, o tun sise fun isakoso Lindoni B Jonsin.
english: Throughout 1960s, Brzezinski worked for John F. Kennedy as his advisor and then the Lyndon B. Johnson administration.

yoruba: Lákòókò ìsàlẹ́ṣà ọdún 1976, ó gba Carter nímọ̀ràn lórí ilẹ̀ òkèrè, lẹ́yìn èyí ó jẹ́ olùdámọ̀ràn ààbò àpapọ̀ (NSA) láti ọdún 1977 sí 1981, ẹni tó gbapò Henry Kissinger.
english: During the 1976 selections he advised Carter on foreign policy, then served as National Security Advisor (NSA) from 1977 to 1981, succeeding Henry Kissinger.

yoruba: Bi i NSA, ó ran Carter lọ́wọ́ ní yíyanjú Ètò Àgbáyé, bí i Camp David Accords ti ọdún 1978; mímú áyípadà bá àjọsepọ̀ ilẹ̀ US–China ní ọwọ ìparí 1970s; Ìjagbara àwọn ilẹ̀ Iran tí ó yorí sí rògbòdìyàn ìdógo àwọn Iran ní ọdún, 1979; àti yíyalu àwọn Soviet ní Afghanistan ní ọdún 1979.
english: As NSA, he assisted Carter in diplomatically handling world affairs, such as the Camp David Accords, 1978; normalizing US–China relations thought the late 1970s; the Iranian Revolution, which led to the Iran hostage crisis, 1979; and the Soviet invasion in Afghanistan, 1979.

yoruba: Fíìmú tó sàfihàn Ryan Gosling àti Emma Stone gba yíyàn nì gbogbo éka pàtàkì.
english: The movie, featuring Ryan Gosling and Emma Stone, received nominations in all major categories.

yoruba: Goslini àti Okuta yege fún ìbò Eléré Ọkùnrin àti Eléré Obinrin ní kọ̀kan wọn.
english: Gosling and Stone received nominations for Best Actor and Actress respectively.

yoruba: Awọn ìsọ̀rí mìíràn ni, Àwòrán tó dára jù, Olùdarí, Àyàwòrán, Aṣaralóge, Asàtúntò-Fíìmù, Aṣàtúntò ohùn, Apohùnpọ̀, àti Akọ̀tà.
english: The other nominations include Best Picture, Director, Cinematography, Costume Design, Film-editing, Original Score, Production Design, Sound Editing, Sound Mixing and Original Screenplay.

yoruba: Orin méjì látinú àgbéléwò, ìdánwò (Àwọn òmùgọ̀ tó ń lá àlá) àti ìlú àwọn ìràwọ̀ ni wọ́n mún fún orin ojúlówó tó dára jù. ilé ìsiṣẹ́ Lionsgate ló gba ìyànsípò mẹ́rìndínlọ́gbọ̀n — jú àwọn ilé ìsiṣẹ̀ mìíràn lọ.
english: Two songs from the movie, Audition (The Fools Who Dream) and City of Stars, received nominations for best original song. Lionsgate studio received 26 nominations — more than any other studio.

yoruba: Ní alẹ́ ọjọ́ àìkú, àrẹ oríll èdè Amẹ́ríkà Donald Trump, ní ọ̀rọ̀ rẹ̀ tí ó sọ nípasẹ̀ ọ̀gá oníròyìn, kéde pé àwọn ọmọ ogun US ma fi ìlú Syria sílẹ̀.
english: Late on Sunday, the United States President Donald Trump, in a statement delivered via the press secretary, announced US troops would be leaving Syria.

yoruba: Won se ikede naa leyin ti Trumpi ba Aare Toki Resep Tayipi Edogani lori ago.
english: The announcement was made after Trump had a phone conversation with Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdoğan.

yoruba: Turkey yíò pẹ̀lú àmójútó ìdábòbò àwọn ọlótè ISIS tí wọ́n mú, èyí tí àlàyé fi síta , àwọn orílẹ̀-èdè Yúróòpù tí kọ̀ jálè láti fi ipá gbé padà sí ìlú wọn.
english: Turkey would also take over guarding captured ISIS fighters which, the statement said, European nations have refused to repatriate.

yoruba: Èyì kò ṣàrídájú pé ó kéré jù àwọn dinosaurs ní ìyẹ́,ọ̀rọ̀ tó dájú tí ó ti tànká, ṣùgbọ́n ó pèsè ẹ̀kúnrẹ́rẹ́ àlàyé egungun ẹranko dinosaurs bíi àwọ́ àti títò lanà mẹt.
english: This not only confirms that at least some dinosaurs had feathers, a theory already widespread, but provides details fossils generally cannot, such as color and three-dimensional arrangement.

yoruba: Awon onimo sayensi wipe iye eranko naa je awo sesnut- burawuni lori pelu awo sisi tabi awo-karotenoidi labe.
english: . Scientists say this animal's plumage was chestnut-brown on top with a pale or carotenoid-colored underside.

yoruba: Wíwá náà tún fúni lóyẹ sí ìtànkálè ti àwọn ìyé nínú ẹyẹ.
english: The find also grants insight into the evolution of feathers in birds.

yoruba: Nítorí ìyẹ́ dánísọ̀̀ kò ní ọpá tó gbó káká, tí wọ́n ń pè ní rachis, ṣùgbọ́n ó ní àwọn àbùdá ìyé mìíràn – barbs àti barbules – àwọn aṣèwádìí sọ ohun tó jọ́ wípé rachis ṣeéṣe kí o jẹ́ àbùdá ìyẹ́ tí ó ti wà tipẹ́ síwájú àwọn ìyók.
english: Because the dinosaur feathers do not have a well-developed shaft, called a rachis, but do have other features of feathers — barbs and barbules — the researchers inferred the rachis was likely a later evolutionary development that these other features.

yoruba: Ohun tó dàbí ìyẹ dábàá pé kí wọ́n má lò nínu ọkọ̀ òfurufú ṣùgbọ́n fún títọ́ ara gbígbónà sọ́nà tàbí láti ṣàfihàn. Àwọn aṣèwádìí da lábàá pé, kódà èyí jẹ́ ìrù ọmọ dinosaur, àpẹrẹ rẹ ṣàfihàn ti àgbàlagbà.
english: The feathers' structure suggests that they were not used in flight but rather for temperature regulation or display. The researchers suggested that, even though this is the tail of a young dinosaur, the sample shows adult plumage and not a chick's down.

yoruba: Àwọn aṣàwárí dalábàá pé, bí ètí bá jẹ́ ìrù ọmọ dinosaur, àpẹrẹ rẹ̀ ṣàfihàn ti àgbàlagbà kìn ṣe ti ọmọdé.
english: The researchers suggested that, even though this is the tail of a young dinosaur, the sample shows adult plumage and not a chick's down.

yoruba: Àdó olóró tí wọ́n so mọ ọkọ̀ tí wọ́n wa lọ sí àgọ́ àwọn ọlọ́pàá ní Gaziantep, lórílẹ̀èdèTurkey, láàárọ̀ àná pa ọlọ́pàá méjì, tí ó sì ṣe àwọn tó tó ogún léṣe.
english: A car bomb detonated at police headquarters in Gaziantep, Turkey yesterday morning killed two police officers and injured more than twenty other people.

yoruba: Ọfíìsì Gómínà sọ pé mọ́kàndínlógún nínú àwọn tí ó fi ara pa jẹ́ ọlọ́pàá.
english: The governor's office said nineteen of the injured were police officers.

yoruba: Ọlọ́pa sọ pé àwọn funra sí ọmọ ogun Daesh (ISIL) fún ìṣẹ̀lẹ̀ ná.
english: Police said they suspect an alleged Daesh (ISIL) militant of responsibility for the attack.

yoruba: Wọn rí ìsàmúlò ìlànà kan náà lórí oòrùn bí i ti ìràwọ̀: Ìṣe gbogbo àwọn ìràwọ̀ ni a kíyèsí pé yíyí wọn àti ìse wọn nìkan ni ó ń darí wọn, kò sí nǹkan mìíràn.
english: They found the Sun operated on the same basic principles as other stars: The activity of all stars in the system was found to be driven by their luminosity, their rotation, and nothing else.

yoruba: A máà n lo luminositi ati iyipo papọ̀ láti fi mọ onka rosbi irawo, ti o ní Ṣe pẹ̀lú bí olasma Ṣe wàn s.
english: The luminosity and rotation are used together to determine a star's Rossby number, which is related to plasma flow.

yoruba: Bí nọ́ḿbà Rossby bá se kéré tó, bẹ́ẹ̀ náà ni iye ìràwọ̀ kò se ní lágbára sí ní ìtọ́ka sí ìdápadà sẹ́yìn agbérin.
english: The smaller the Rossby number, the less active the star with respect to magnetic reversals.

yoruba: Nínú ìrìnàjò rẹ´, Iwasaki bọ sínú wàhálà ní ọ̀pọ̀lọpọ ìgbà.
english: During his trip, Iwasaki ran into trouble on many occasions.

yoruba: Àwọn ajalèlókun ló jàálólè, wọ́n jalè ní Tibet nípa lílo ajá elégbọn, ó sá kúrò nínu ìgbáyàwó rẹ wọ́n sì mu ní India.
english: He was robbed by pirates, attacked in Tibet by a rabid dog, escaped marriage in Nepal and was arrested in India.

yoruba: Eto 802.11n sise pelu eto igbohun si afefe 2.4Ghz ati 5.0Ghz.
english: The 802.11n standard operates on both the 2.4Ghz and 5.0Ghz frequencies.

yoruba: Èyí yíò fún ní àǹfànni láti padà sẹ́yìn pẹ̀lú 802.11a, 802.11b àti 802.11g, èyí tó pèsè ibùdó o ní rádíò méjì.
english: This will allow it to be backwards compatible with 802.11a, 802.11b and 802.11g, provided that the base station has dual radios.

yoruba: Ìyára méjìlélẹ̀gbẹrin ó lé mọ́kànlá ni wọ́n má ń yára jùlọ pẹ̀lú àkọsílẹ̀ tíọ́rì tó gajù jẹ́ ẹgbẹ̀taMbit/s.
english: The speeds of 802.11n are substantially faster than that of its predecessors with a maximum theoretical throughput of 600Mbit/s.

yoruba: Dufaali to ti Ṣe ìgbéyàwó pẹ̀lú Ọmọ méjì o fí bẹ́ẹ̀ dá Mila, ti ìtàn náà tan mó, lọ́rùn.
english: Duvall, who is married with two adult children, did not leave a big impression on Miller, to whom the story was related.

yoruba: Nígbà tí a bèrè fún ìdásí, Miller sọ pé, “Mike ma ń sọ̀rọ̀ jù nípa kíkà... Mò ń gbáradìnítorínà mi kò gba ǹkan tí ó sọ.”
english: When asked for comment, Miller said, "Mike talks a lot during the hearing...I was getting ready so I wasn't really hearing what he was saying."

yoruba: “A ó tiraka láti gé ohun tí ó ń jáde carbon dioxide lóní ìwọ̀n GDP pẹ̀lú àmì tó farahàn bí yíò bá fi di 2020 láti ìpele 2005 gẹ́gẹ́ bí” Hu se sọ.
english: "We will endeavour to cut carbon dioxide emissions per unit of GDP by a notable margin by 2020 from the 2005 level," Hu said.

yoruba: Kò sàgbékalẹ̀ iye kankan fún ègé náà, sọ pé wọ́n yíò se é ní ìbámu àbájáde ọrọ̀ ajé ilẹ̀ China.
english: He did not set a figure for the cuts, saying they will be made based on China's economic output.

yoruba: Hu gba àwọn orílẹ̀èdè tó sẹ̀sẹ̀ ń dàgbà níyànjú láti “kọ ọ̀nà àtijọ́ tí wọ́n má ń ba àyíká jẹ́ saájú kí wọ́n tó tún un se bóbá yá.”
english: Hu encouraged developing countries "to avoid the old path of polluting first and cleaning up later."

yoruba: O fikun pé “kí wọ́n máṣe, síbẹ̀síbẹ̀, wọ́n ma sọ fún láti pọndandan rẹ kọjá ìpele ìdàgbàsóké, ojúṣe àti agbár.”
english: He added that "they should not, however, be asked to take on obligations that go beyond their development stage, responsibility and capabilities."

yoruba: Ẹgbẹ́ aṣèwádìí ilẹ̀ Iraq se àgbékalẹ̀ àbábọ̀ rẹ̀ ní nǹkan bí i aago méjìlá ọ̀sán òní.
english: The Iraq Study Group presented its report at 12.00 GMT today.

yoruba: Ó sèkìlọ̀ wípé kò sií ẹnikẹ́ni tó lè fọwọ́ sọ̀yà wípé ìgbésẹ̀kíìgbésẹ̀ tí ilẹ̀ Iraq bá gbé lásìkò yìí kò lè paná ìjà ẹgbẹ́jẹgbẹ́, làásìgbò tó ń peléke òun èyí tó lẹ̀ mórí lé rògbòdìyàn.
english: It warns No one can guarantee that any course of action in Iraq at this point will stop sectarian warfare, growing violence, or a slide toward chaos.

yoruba: Àbájádẹ bèrè pèlú ìsìpè fún ìjíròrò gbàngba àti ìgbédìdẹ ìpohùnpọ̀ ní orílè-èdè Améríkà nípa àwọn ípinnu tó ní sẹ pélù Ààrin Gúngún Ìlà-oòrùn.
english: The Report opens with plea for open debate and the formation of a consensus in the United States about the policy towards the Middle East.

yoruba: Ìjábọ̀ náà jẹ́ ǹkan tó le gan ní gbogbo ẹ̀yà ìlànà tuntun ti àwọn adarí sí ìlú Iraq ó sì sọ pé kí wọ́n yí ọ̀nà padà.
english: The Report is highly critical of almost every aspect of the present policy of the Executive towards Iraq and it urges an immediate change of direction.

yoruba: Ọ̀kan lára àwọn àlàkalẹ̀ méjìdínlọ́gọ́rin ni wípé wọ́n gbọ́dọ̀ gbé ìgbésẹ̀ ìlànà òsèlú kó tó di òpin odún yìí láti le gba ibodè Iraq tako ìdásí tó le, àti sísàtúngbékalẹ̀ àjọsepọ̀ tó dánmọ́rán pẹ̀láwọn alájọgbé.
english: First among its 78 recommendations is that a new diplomatic initiative should be taken before the end of this year to secure Iraq’s borders against hostile interventions and to re-establish diplomatic relations with its neighbors.

yoruba: Senato to wa lori oye ati Aya Aare Arabirin Kristina Fenandesi de Kisna kede ipe ohun fe wo idije aare ni irole anaa ni La Plata, ilu ti o je 50 kilometers (31 miles) si Bwenos Aresi.
english: Current senator and Argentine First Lady Cristina Fernandez de Kirchner announced her presidential candidacy yesterday evening in La Plata, a city 50 kilometers (31 miles) away from Buenos Aires.

yoruba: Arábìnrin Kirchner kéde èróngbà láti díje dunpò ààrẹ ilé ìseré ìtàgé Argentine, ibùdó kanáà tí ó lò fún ìpolongo rẹ̀ fún ìdìbò asòfin gẹ́gẹ́ bí ọmọ ẹgbẹ́ aṣojú ẹkùn Buenos Aires.
english: Mrs. Kirchner announced her intention to run for president at the Argentine Theatre, the same location she used to start her 2005 campaign for the Senate as member of the Buenos Aires province delegation.

yoruba: Ìjiyàn náà bẹ̀rẹ̀ láti àríyàn jiyàn lóri owó ìtura àtí àtúnkọ́ ilé ní ìgbà Ọrikénì, tí àwọn konsafetifu tí fi yẹ̀yẹ́ sọ lórúkọ “Ìṣòwò Olinsi Titun Buṣì”.
english: The debate was sparked by controversy over spending on relief and reconstruction in the wake Hurricane Katrina; which some fiscal conservatives have humorously labeled "Bush's New Orleans Deal."

yoruba: Ìbániwí tí kò le ti agbára àtúnkọ́lé tí fẹ́ gbé iṣẹ́ àtúnkọ́lé fún àwọn ọmọ ìlú Washington.
english: Liberal criticism of the reconstruction effort has focused on the awarding of reconstruction contracts to perceived Washington insiders.

yoruba: Ó kọjá èyan milionu mẹ́rin tó lọsí Romu fún ìsìnkú náà.
english: Over four million people went to Rome to attend the funeral.

yoruba: Iye àwọn ènìyàn tí ó péjọ pọ̀ tóbẹ́ẹ̀ gẹ́ tí ó fi jẹ́ pé kò ṣe é ṣe fún gbogbo ènìyàn láti rí àyè wọlé fún ìsìnkú ní ilé ìjọsìn St. Peter.
english: The number of people present was so large that it was not possible for everybody to gain access to the funeral in St. Peter's Square.

yoruba: Ọnírúurú àwọn ẹ̀rọ amóhùnmáwòrán tójú wọn tóbi ní a sàfilọ́lẹ̀ káàkiri agbègbè ní ilẹ̀ Roomu láti lè jẹ́ kàwọn ènìyàn wo ayẹyẹ.
english: Several large television screens were installed in various places in Rome to let the people watch the ceremony.

yoruba: Ni ọpọ awọn ilu omiran ni Italy ati ni iyoku agbaye, papa julọ ni Poland, iru agbekalẹ bayi wa, eyi ti opolopo eniyan ri.
english: In many other cities of Italy and in the rest of the world, particularly in Poland, similar setups were made, which were viewed by a great number of people.

yoruba: Àwọn akọ̀tàn ti se àríwísí àwọn ètò ìmúlò àtẹ̀yìnwá ọ́fíìsì àjọ àpapọ̀ òtẹlẹ̀múyé fún lílo ìsúná rè lòrì àwọn ìsẹ̀lẹ̀ tó rọrùn láti yanjú, ní pàtàkì àwọn ọ̀ràn jíjí ọkọ̀ áyọkẹ́lẹ́ pẹ̀lú èróngbá láti se ìgbélárugẹ fún ódiwọ̀n àsẹyọrì rẹ.
english: Historians have criticized past FBI policies for focusing resources on cases which are easy to solve, especially stolen car cases, with the intent of boosting the agency's success rate.

yoruba: Àpérò bẹ̀rl sí ní kó owó sí obcenity initiative ní fiscal n\i ọdún 2005 ó sì so pé àwọn FBI nílái fi aṣojú mẹ́wàá sílẹ̀ fún fíìmù àgbàlagbà.
english: Congress began funding the obscenity initiative in fiscal 2005 and specified that the FBI must devote 10 agents to adult pornography.

yoruba: Robin Uthappa wọlé pẹ̀lú góòlù tó ga jùlọ bí i àádọ́rin pẹ̀lú bọ́ọ̀lù mọ́kànlélógì nípa gbígbá mẹ́rin mọ́kànlá àti mẹ́fà méjì.
english: Robin Uthappa made the innings highest score, 70 runs in just 41 balls by hitting 11 fours and 2 sixes.

yoruba: Middle order batsmen, Sachin Tendulkar and Rahul Dravid, ṣe dáadáa tí wọ́n sì ní ọgọ́rùn ún àjọsepọ̀.
english: Middle order batsmen, Sachin Tendulkar and Rahul Dravid, performed well and made a hundred-run partnership.

yoruba: Ṣùgban lẹ́yìn tí wọ́n sọ captain wicket nù India ṣe 36 runs tí wọ́n sì sọ wicket 7 nù láti parí rẹ̀.
english: But, after losing the captain's wicket India only made 36 runs loosing 7 wickets to end the innings.

yoruba: Olori oorilrede U.S to n je George W. Bush de si ilu Singapore ni aro ojo ikerindinlogun ninu osu kokanla, eyi to fi bere awoyika ilu Asia olose kan.
english: U.S. President George W. Bush arrived in Singapore the morning of November 16, beginning a week-long tour of Asia.

yoruba: Igbákejì Olórí Singapo Wongi Kani Sengi ló kíi o dẹ̀ sọ̀rọ̀ ní ba isòwò àti ọ̀rọ̀ ìhàlẹ̀ pẹ̀lú Olórí Singapo Lee Hsien Loongi.
english: He was greeted by Singapore's Deputy Prime Minister Wong Kan Seng and discussed trade and terrorism issues with the Singapore Prime Minister Lee Hsien Loong.

yoruba: Lẹ́yin àdánu ọ̀sẹ̀ kan nínú ètò ìdìbò ìdajì sáà, Bush sọ fún àkójọpọ̀ àwọn èro rẹ̀ nípa sísàmúngbòrò kátàkárà ní ilẹ̀ Asia.
english: After a week of losses in the midterm election, Bush told an audience about the expansion of trade in Asia.

yoruba: Adari ijọba Stephen Harper ti gba lati fi iwe erongba ijọba lori “Clean Air Act” ranṣẹ si ijoko awon ọmọ ẹgbẹ fun agbeyẹwo, ki oto di kika fun igba elekeji lọyin ipade ọjọ iṣẹgun oni iṣẹju mẹẹdọgbọn pẹlu adari NDP Jack Layton ni PMO.
english: Prime Minister Stephen Harper has agreed to send the government's 'Clean Air Act' to an all-party committee for review, before its second reading, after Tuesday's 25 minute meeting with NDP leader Jack Layton at the PMO.

yoruba: Laytoni ti bèèrè fún àyípadà lórí òfin ìhùwàsí àyíká lákòókò ìpàdé pẹ̀lú PM, bèèrè pé kí wọ́n se àtúnkọ àbádòfin ìhùwà ẹgbẹ́ sí àyíká.
english: Layton had asked for changes to the conservatives' environmental bill during the meeting with the PM, asking for a "thorough and complete rewriting" of the Conservative party's environmental bill.

yoruba: Láti ìgbà tí ìjọba àpapọ̀ ti gba ìsúná ilé ìwòsàn Mersey tí ó wà ní Devonport, ní Tasmania, ìjọba ìbílẹ̀ àti àwọn Aṣojú ìjọbá àpapọ̀ ti bu ẹnu àtẹ́ lu ìgbésẹ̀ yìí gẹ́gẹ́ bí ètè tí ó ní í ṣe pẹlú ètò ìdìbò tó ń bọ̀ ní oṣù kọkànlá.
english: Ever since the Federal Government stepped in to take over funding of the Mersey hospital in Devonport, Tasmania, the state government and some federal MPs have criticised this act as a stunt in the prelude to the federal election to be called by November.

yoruba: Ṣùgbọ́n Mínísítà àgbà, John Howard ti sọ wípé àbá náà wà fún àbò àwọn ohun èlò ilé ìwòsàn náà, kí ìjọba Tasmanian má ba à fi wọ́lẹ̀, ní gbigbé milíọ́nù márùndínlógójì dọ́là sílẹ̀.
english: But Prime Minister John Howard has said the act was only to safeguard the facilities of the hospital from being downgraded by the Tasmanian government, in giving an extra AUD$45 million.

yoruba: Gẹ́gẹ́ bí ìròyìn tuntun ṣe wí, ìpelé odò takasí pé wọ́n fa tsunami ni. Àwọn iṣe tsunami ti wọ́n ṣàkọsílẹ̀ ní tòsí Pago Pago àti Niue.
english: According to the latest bulletin, sea level readings indicated a tsunami was generated. There was some definite tsunami activity recorded near Pago Pago and Niue.

yoruba: Wọn ò jésì ìpalára tàbí ìjàmba púpọ̀jù ní Tonga, ṣùgbọ́n iná ṣẹ́jú fún ìgbà kan, tó mú kí àwọn aláṣẹ Tonga má rí ìkilọ̀ ìjì súnámì tí PWC gbéjáde.
english: No major damage or injuries have been reported in Tonga, but power was temporarily lost, which reportedly prevented Tongan authorities from receiving the tsunami warning issued by the PTWC.

yoruba: Àwọn ilé ìwé mẹ́rìnlà ní Hawaii tó wà ní tàbí tó wà ní ẹ̀gbẹ́ etí òkun ti jẹ́ títìpa ní ọjọ́rú pẹ̀lú bí wọ́n ṣe yọ ìkìlọ̀ kúrò.
english: Fourteen schools in Hawaii located on or near coastlines were closed all of Wednesday despite the warnings being lifted.

yoruba: Ààrẹ orilé-ẹ̀dẹ̀ Améríkà George W. Bush gba ìkẹ́de náà wọlé.
english: U.S. President George W. Bush welcomed the announcement.

yoruba: Ẹni tó sọ̀rọ̀ fún Búsì Godoni Johanudro pé ilérí Koria Ariwa ní “ìgbésẹ̀ nlá sọ́na àlébá láti yọ irin ogún nukilia kúrò ní irekusu Koria.
english: Bush spokesman Gordon Johndroe called North Korea's pledge "a major step towards the goal of achieving the verifiable denuclearization of the Korean peninsula."

yoruba: Ìjì lílé kẹwàá ti sáà Atlantic Hurricane, Subtropical Storm Jerry, ni wọ́n sẹ̀dà ní ọ̀sà Atlantic lónìí.
english: The tenth named storm of the Atlantic Hurricane season, Subtropical Storm Jerry, formed in the Atlantic Ocean today.

yoruba: Ibùdó Ìjì líli Orílẹ̀ èdè (NHC) sọ pé ní àsìkò yìí Jerry kò lè ṣe ǹkan kan ní orí ilẹ̀.
english: The National Hurricane Center (NHC) says that at this point Jerry poses no threat to land.

yoruba: Àwọn ẹgbẹ́ ológun ilẹ̀ Amẹ́ríkà tí ẹ̀ka onímọ̀ ẹ̀rọ, ṣe ìṣirò pé tí òjọ̀ bá rọ ìwọ̀n íǹṣì mẹ́fà péré, yóò dí ojú ihò ewé tó ti bàjẹ́ tẹ́lẹ.
english: The U.S. Corps of Engineers estimated that 6 inches of rainfall could breach the previously damaged levees.

yoruba: Ìpín Kẹ̀sán, tó ní ìrírí ẹ̀kúnwọ́ tó ga tó ìwọ̀n bàtà ogún nínú ìjì líle Katrina, lọ́wọ́ lọ́wọ́ ti máa ní gíga tó ègbé ikùn bí ìdarí omi tó sún mọ́tí di bíbò mọ́lẹ. .
english: The Ninth Ward, which saw flooding as high as 20 feet during Hurricane Katrina, is currently in waist-high water as the nearby levee was overtopped.

yoruba: Omi ń sàn lórí levee ní apá kan níwọ̀n fífẹ̀ ẹsẹ̀ ọgọ́rùn ún.
english: Water is spilling over the levee in a section 100 feet wide.

yoruba: Alákòóso tó wọ́pọ̀ Adam Cuerden ti fi ìpinlẹẹ̀mí ọkàn hàn lórí àwọn ìparẹ́ tó wáyé lákòókò tó ń sọ̀rọ̀ ní Wikinews lóṣù tó kọjá.
english: Commons Administrator Adam Cuerden expressed his frustration over the deletions when he spoke to Wikinews last month.

yoruba: “Ó [Wales] parọ́ fún wa láti ìbẹ̀rẹ̀. Àkọ́kọ́ nípa síseré àfi bí èyí wà fú idí òfin. Ìkejì, nípa dídíbọ́n pé òun gbọ́ wa títí di bí ó se pa iṣẹ́ ọnà rẹ́.”
english: "He [Wales] basically lied to us from the start. First, by acting as if this was for legal reasons. Second, by pretending he was listening to us, right up to his art deletion."

yoruba: Ìkórira agbègbè náà fa agbára láti kọ ìlànà tó ní ṣe pẹ̀lú ìbálòpọ́ fún ojú òpó tó gba àlejò tó mílíọ́nù mídíà gbangba.
english: The community irritation led to current efforts to draft a policy regarding sexual content for the site which hosts millions of openly-licensed media.

yoruba: Isé sísẹ náà lọ́pọ̀lọpọ̀ jẹ fún ẹ̀kọ́, sùgbón kíkọ́ ètò náà jẹ láti sẹ àfiwẹ́ra àkíyèsí tó di sísẹ nípa Sagirrarius lójú òrun.
english: The work done was mostly theoretical, but the program was written to simulate observations made of the Sagittarius galaxy.

yoruba: Awon agbara iji omi lo le ko ipa ti egbe naa n wa laarin ohun dudu galaksi ati nkan dudu Ona Milik.
english: The effect the team was looking for would be caused by tidal forces between the galaxy's dark matter and the Milky Way's dark matter.

yoruba: Gẹ́lẹ́ bí òṣùpá ṣe ń ṣe pẹlú ayé, tí yóò sì jẹ́ kí ó gún, bẹ́ẹ̀ ni Milky Way ṣe máa ń ṣe pẹ̀lú Sagittarius galaxy.
english: Just like the moon exerts a pull on the earth, causing tides, so does the Milky Way exert a force on the Sagittarius galaxy.

yoruba: O ṣeéṣe fún àwọn onímọ̀ sáyẹnsì latí pinu pé ohun dúdú ma ń ran ohun dúdú mín bí ohun désé ṣe ma n ràn án.
english: The scientists were able to conclude that the dark matter affect other dark matter in the same way regular matter does.

yoruba: Àlàyé yìí sọ pé àwọn ǹkan dúdú tó wà ní àyíkà galaxy ló wà ní àyíká galaxy gẹ́gẹ́ bí halo, àwọn ǹkan kékèké ló gbedúró.
english: This theory says that most dark matter around a galaxy is located around a galaxy in a kind of halo, and is made of lots of small particles.

yoruba: Iroyin lati ori amohunmaworan fihan pe efin n jade lati inu koriko wa.
english: Television reports show white smoke coming from the plant.

yoruba: Àwọn aláṣẹ abẹ́lé ti ń sèkìlọ̀ fáwọn òsìṣẹ́ lágbègbè iléeṣẹ́ láti dúró sílé, pa ẹ̀rọ amúnlétutù, kí wọ́n má sì mú omi ẹ̀rọ.
english: Local authorities are warning residents in the vicinity of the plant to stay indoors, turn off air-conditioners and not to drink tap water.

yoruba: Ní ìbámu pẹ̀lú àjọ ohun àmúsagbàra ti Japan, kẹ́míkà tóní ìpalára àti iodine tí di àwárí ní ilé́ isẹ́ náà.
english: According to Japan's nuclear agency, radioactive caesium and iodine has been identified at the plant.

yoruba: Awon alase ma n daba pe eyi toka si pe awon kolo to n di epo uraniumu ni ni oju ibe le ti be ati pe o n jo.
english: Authorities speculate that this indicates that containers holding uranium fuel at the site may have ruptured and are leaking.

yoruba: Dr. Tony Moll sàwárí Extremely Drug Resistant Tuberculosis (XDR-TB) ní KwaZulu-Natal lẹ́kùn South African.
english: Dr. Tony Moll discovered the Extremely Drug Resistant Tuberculosis (XDR-TB) in the South African region KwaZulu-Natal.

yoruba: Ninu iforoworo kan, o wi pe irufe titun naa o n “danilamu pupo o de n fa ijaya nitoripe osuwon iku ti o le sokun fa, o ga.”
english: In an interview, he said the new variant was "very highly troubling and alarming because of the very high fatality rate."

yoruba: Dokita Moli ro wipe awon alaisan kan leti fara ko kokoro naa ni ile-iwosan,ati wipe meji lara won je osise eleto ile-iwosan naa.
english: Some patients might have contracted the bug in the hospital, Dr. Moll thinks, and at least two were hospital health workers.

yoruba: Bí yíò bá fi di ọdún kan sí àkókò yìí, ènìyàn kan tó ní ààrùn lè kó o ran ẹni mẹ́wàá sí márùndínlógún mííràn tó bá ní ìfarakínra.
english: In one year's time, an infected person may infect 10 to 15 close contacts.

yoruba: Àmọ́ṣa, ìda XDR-TB nínú gbogbo àwọn èèyà tó ní ikọ́ àwúgbẹ sì kéré níye: ẹgbẹ̀rún mẹ́fà nínú èèyàn ọ̀jì lé lọ́ọ̀dúnrún ó dín mẹ́wàá ni ó ní lákòókò kan nílẹ̀ South Africa.
english: However, the percentage of XDR-TB in the entire group of people with tuberculosis still seems to be low; 6,000 of the total 330,000 people infected at any particular moment in South Africa.

yoruba: Sátẹ̀láìtì náà, méjèjì tó wọ́n ju 1000 pounds lọ,tí wọ́n s;i rin ìrìn àjò tó tó máàlì 17,500 ní wákàtí,kọlu máàlì 491 lórí ilẹ̀.
english: The satellites, both of which weighed in excess of 1,000 pounds, and traveling at approximately 17,500 miles per hour, collided 491 miles above the Earth.

yoruba: Àwọn onímò Sayensi wípé búbúgbàmà ti ìkọlù náà jẹ títóbi gan.
english: Scientists say the explosion caused by the collision was massive.

yoruba: Won si n sagbeyewo bii ijamba naa se tobi to ati ipa ti o ma ko lori Aye.
english: They are still trying to determine just how large the crash was and how the Earth will be affected.

yoruba: Àṣẹ ọgbọ́n ti United States tí ẹ̀ka olùgbèjà U.S wà àwọn ǹkan tó kù.
english: The United States Strategic Command of the U.S. Department of Defense office is tracking the debris.

yoruba: Èsì yíya àwòrán yíò jáde sórí ìtàkùn ayélujára o ní gbogbogbò.
english: The result of plotting analysis will be posted to a public website.

yoruba: Àwọn agbófinro ni Ohio sọ pé, Oníṣègùn tó ṣiṣẹ́ ni ilé ìwòsàn àwọn ọmọdé tí ó wà ní Pittsburgh, ni Pennsylvania ni wọn yóò fi ẹ̀sùn ìpànìyàn kàn lẹ́yìn tí wọn bá òkú ìyá rẹ̀ lẹ́yìn ọkọ̀ rẹ̀ ní ọjọ́rú.
english: A doctor who worked at Children's Hospital of Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania will be charged with aggravated murder after her mother was found dead in the trunk of her car Wednesday, authorities in Ohio say.

yoruba: Dr. Malar Balasubramanian, ẹni ọdún mọ́kàndínlọ́gbọ̀n ní wọ́n rí ní Blue Ash, Ohio, agbègbè tó dàbí máìlì márùndínlógún sí àríwá Cincinnati, tó sùn sí ẹ̀gbẹ́ ojú ọ̀nà nínú aṣọ T-shirt àti àwọ̀tẹ́lẹ̀ nípò tó sàfihàn pé ó ń lo òògùn.
english: Dr. Malar Balasubramanian, 29, was found in Blue Ash, Ohio, a suburb approximately 15 miles north of Cincinnati lying on the ground beside the road in a T-shirt and underwear in an apparently heavily medicated state.

yoruba: Ó darí àwọn agbófinró sí ọkọ̀ dúdú rẹ̀ èyí tó wa bí i ìwọ̀n ẹsẹ̀ ẹ̀ẹ́dẹ́gbẹ̀ta sí i.
english: She directed officers to her black Oldsmobile Intrigue which was 500 feet away.

yoruba: Níbè, wọn rí ará Saroja Balasubramanian, métàlẹ́làáadọ́ta, tí a bò pèlú àwọn aṣo ìbora ẹ̀jẹ.
english: There, they found the body of Saroja Balasubramanian, 53, covered with blood-stained blankets.

yoruba: Àwọn ọlọ́pàá sọ pé ó dàbí ẹni wípé ara náà ti wà níbẹ̀ fún bí i ọjọ́ kan.
english: Police said that the body appeared to have been there for about a day.

yoruba: Ìgbà àkọ́kọ́ tí wọ́n ma rí àrùn náà ní àkókò yìí ni ní ìparí oṣù kéje.
english: The first cases of the disease this season were reported in late July.

yoruba: Àwọn elede lo ma n gbé àrùn náà, a wá ti ara ẹ̀fọn fọ́nká sí ọmọ èyan.
english: The disease is carried by pigs, which then migrates to humans through mosquitos.

yoruba: Àjàkálẹ̀ ti mún kí ìjọba ilẹ̀ India gbé ìgbésẹ̀ láti gbé ìgbésẹ̀ fi darí àwọn tó ń mún ẹlẹ́dẹ̀ láwọn agbègbè típalára wà, pín ẹgbẹ̀lẹ́gbẹ̀ kọ́tìnì ẹ̀fọn àti òògùn fínfín ẹ̀fọn.
english: The outbreak has prompted the Indian government to undertake such measures as deployment of pig catchers in seriously affected areas, distributing thousands of mosquito curtains and spraying pesticides.

yoruba: Àwọn ìjọba ti ṣe lérí pé ọ̀pọ̀lọpọ̀ abẹ́rẹ́ àjẹsára encephalitis, èyí tó ma rànwálọ́wọ́ láti gbáradì fún àwọn àjọ ìlera fún ọdún tó ń bọ̀.
english: Several million vials of encephalitis vaccine have also been promised by the government, which will help prepare health agencies for next year.

yoruba: Wọn dínwọ̀ ètò láti jíṣẹ́ ògùn maporo sí agbègbè tó kópa jù nítorí àìsowo àti pe wọn ò fiṣe nkan pàtàkì láàrín àwọn àrùn.
english: Plans for vaccines to be delivered to the historically most affected areas this year were delayed due to lack of funds and low prioritisation relative to other diseases.

yoruba: Ní ọdún 1956, Slania kó lọ sí Sweden, níbití ó ti bẹ̀rẹ̀ iṣẹ́ fún ilé iṣẹ́ ìfìwéránṣẹ́ Sweden lẹ́yìn ọdún mẹ́ta tó sì di olórí ayàwòrán.
english: In 1956 Słania moved to Sweden, where three years later he began work for the Swedish Post Office and became their chief engraver.

yoruba: O se staampi oye to ju 1,000 fun Swidini ati awon ilu 28 omiran.
english: He produced over 1,000 stamps for Sweden and 28 other countries.

yoruba: Iṣẹ́ rẹ̀ ní gbajúmọ̀ àti tó jẹ́ pé ó wà lára àwọn “òrúkọ ìdílé” díẹ̀ lára àwọn filatelisti. Àwọn kan ma n ṣa iṣẹ́ rẹ̀ nìkan.
english: His work is of such recognized quality and detail that he is one of the very few "household names" among philatelists. Some specialize in collecting his work alone.

yoruba: Òǹtẹ̀ ọgọ́rùń rẹ̀ tóbi gan “Great Deeds by Swedish Kings” nípasẹ́ David Klocker Ehrenstrahi ní ọdún 2000, tí a kọ sílẹ̀ nínú ìwé Guiness of World Records.
english: His 1,000th stamp was the magnificent "Great Deeds by Swedish Kings" by David Klöcker Ehrenstrahl in 2000, which is listed in the Guinness Book of World Records.

yoruba: Ó tún lọ́wọ́ nínú yíya àwòrán owó fún ọ̀pọ̀lọpọ̀ orílẹ̀èdè, àpẹrẹ iṣẹ́ rẹ̀ lọ́wọ́lọ́wọ́ ni àwòrán olóòtú àgbà níwájú owó Canada tuntun bíi dọ́là márùn ún àti ọgọ́rùn ún dọ́là.
english: He was also engaged in engraving banknotes for many countries, recent examples of his work including the Prime Ministerial portraits on the front of the new Canadian $5 and $100 bills.

yoruba: Lẹ́yìn tí ìjàǹbá náà ṣẹlẹ̀, wọ́n gbé Gibson lọ sí ilé ìwòsàn ṣùgbọn kò pẹ́ lẹ́yìn rẹ̀ ni ó kú.
english: After the accident occurred, Gibson was transported to a hospital but died shortly afterwards.

yoruba: Awako to je omo odun 64, o sese ni ijamba oko naa.
english: The truck driver, who is aged 64, was not injured in the crash.

yoruba: Wọn yọ ọkọ naa gangan kuro loju ibi ti ijamba naa ti ṣẹlẹ ni bii ago 1200 GMT lọjọ na.
english: The vehicle itself was taken away from the scene of the accident at approximately 1200 GMT on the same day.

yoruba: Perry ṣe àlàyé wípé òhun o ‘padà sí Texas láti ṣe ayèwò àbájáde ifikùnlukùn ẹgbẹ́ lálé òní, pinnu bóyá ìtẹ̀síwájù wà fún mi nínú ìdíje yìí.” Ṣùgbọ́n lẹ́yìn náà o sọ wípé òhun o tesíwájú nínú ìdíje láti figagbága nínú idibò abẹ́lẹ́ ọjọ́ kọkànlélógún oṣù kìíní tí gúúsù Carolina.
english: Perry stated that he would "return to Texas to assess the results of tonight's caucus, determine whether there is a path forward for myself in this race", but later said that he would remain in the race and compete in the January 21 South Carolina primary.

yoruba: Bachmann, tó borí ìdìbò Ames Straw nínú oọù kẹjọ ti pinnu láti parí ìpolongo rẹ̀.
english: Bachmann, who won the Ames Straw Poll in August, decided to end her campaign.

yoruba: Wọ́n ti gbé ayàwòrán lọ sí ilé ìtọ́jú UCLA Ronald Reagan, níbi tí ó ti padà jáde láyé.
english: The photographer was transported to Ronald Reagan UCLA Medical Center, where he subsequently died.

yoruba: Wọ́n fi tó ni léti pé o kú ni nǹkan bí ọmọ ogún ọdún, Birber sọ pé “ mi ò sí ní ibẹ̀ bẹ́ẹ̀ ni ìṣẹ̀lẹ̀ ńla-ibi yìí kò ṣe ojú mi kòró, àdúrà mi ńbẹ pẹ̀lú àwọn ẹbí tí olóògbé fi sí ayé.
english: He was reportedly aged in his 20s. In a statement, Bieber said "[w]hile I was not present nor directly involved with this tragic accident, my thoughts and prayers are with the family of the victim."

yoruba: Ìtàkùn Ìròyìn ìdárayá tí a mọ̀ sí TMZ jẹ́ kó yé pé àayàwòrán náà dá ọkọ̀ òun dúró lẹ́gbẹ̀ẹ́ kejì Sepulveda Boulevard, ó sì gbìyànjú láti ya àwòránibi ìdánidúró àwọn ọlọ́pàá kí ó tó sọdá sí òdì kejì tí ó sì tẹ̀síwájú, ní èyí tí ó mú kí àwọn ọlọ́pàá òpópónà California tí wọn n ṣe ètò sún kẹrẹ̀ fà kẹrẹ ọkọ dáa dúró kí o padà ní ẹ̀ẹ̀méj.
english: Entertainment news website TMZ understands the photographer stopped his vehicle on the other side of Sepulveda Boulevard and attempted to take pictures of the police stop before crossing the road and continuing, prompting the California Highway Patrol police officer conducting the traffic stop to order him back across, twice.

yoruba: Pelu ohun ti awon agbofinro so, o sese ki awako oko to kolu ayaworan naa ma fi oju bale ejo.
english: According to police, the driver of the vehicle that hit the photographer is unlikely to face criminal charges.

yoruba: Pẹ̀lú gbigba àwọn àmì ẹ̀yẹ méjìdínlógún lójojúmọ́, ọ̀pọ̀ àwọn orílẹ̀-èdè ti kùnà láti wà lóri tábìlì àmì ẹ̀yẹ.
english: With only eighteen medals available a day, a number of countries have failed to make the medal podium.

yoruba: Wọ́n sàfimọ́ àwọn ará ilẹ̀ Netherlands pẹ̀lú Anna Jochemsen tó parí pẹ̀lú ipò kẹsàn án nínú àwọn obìnrin tó ń dúró ní kílàsì super-G lánàá àti Katja Saarinen ti ìlú Finland tó parí pẹ̀lú ipò kẹwàá nínú ètò kanáà.
english: They include the Netherlands, with Anna Jochemsen finishing ninth in the women's standing class in the Super-G yesterday, and Finland with Katja Saarinen finishing tenth in the same event.

yoruba: Mitchell Gourley ti ilẹ̀ Australia parí pẹ̀lú ipò kọkànlá nínú àwọn ọkùnrin tó wà nínú ìdíje Super-G. Czech tí Oldrich Jelinek sì parí pẹ̀lú ipò kẹrìndínlógún ìdíje super-G.
english: Australia's Mitchell Gourley finished eleventh in the men's standing Super-G. Czech competitor Oldrich Jelinek finished sixteenth in the men's sitting Super-G.

yoruba: Arly Velasquez ti ilẹ̀ Mexico parí nípò karùndínlógún ìjókòó àwọn ọkùnrin Super-G. Adam Hall ti ilẹ̀ New Zealand parí pẹ̀lú ipò kẹsàn án níjòòkó àwọn ọkùnrin Super-G.
english: Arly Velasquez of Mexico finished fifteenth in the men's sitting Super-G. New Zealand's Adam Hall finished ninth in the men's standing Super-G.

yoruba: Ọkùnrin afọ́jú a gùnkè ilẹ̀ Poland Maciej Krezel àti guide Anna Ogarzynska parí pẹ̀lú ipò kẹtàlá nínú ìdíje Super-G. Jong Seork Park ti ilẹ̀ South Korea parí pẹ̀lú ipò mẹ́rìnlélógún nígun àwọn ọkùnrin super-G.
english: Poland's men's visually impaired skier Maciej Krezel and guide Anna Ogarzynska finished thirteenth in the Super-G. South Korea's Jong Seork Park finished twenty-fourth in the men's sitting Super-G.

yoruba: Àwọn asètò àlááfíà àjọ UN, tí wọ́n dé ìlú Haiti lẹ́yìn ìsẹ̀lẹ̀ ìjì líle ní ọdún 2010, ni wọ́n ti dá lẹ́bi fún títan ààrùn tó bẹ̀rẹ̀ ní etí bèbè àgọ́ àwọn ọmọ ológun ká.
english: UN peacekeepers, whom arrived in Haiti after the 2010 earthquake, are being blamed for the spread of the disease which started near the troop's encampment.

yoruba: Gẹ́gẹ́ bí ìwé ìpéjọ́, ìdọ̀tí tí àjọ UN kó jáde ní ọ̀nà tí kó boójú mú fún ìlera, ṣe okùnfà àwọn kòkòrò àrùn tó wọ ibùdó odò Artbonite, ìkan lára èyí tó tóbi jù ní Haiti .
english: According to the lawsuit, waste from the UN camp was not properly sanitized, causing bacteria to enter the tributary of the Artibonite River, one of Haiti's largest.

yoruba: Kí àwọn ológun tó dé, ìlú Haiti kò ì tí kojú ìsòro tí ó jọ́ mọ́ ààrùn yìí láti ọdun 1800.
english: Prior to the arrival of troops, Haiti had not encountered problems related to the disease since the 1800s.

yoruba: Ilẹ́-ẹ̀kọ́ fún òdodo àti ìjọba tiwantiwan ti Haiti ti tọ́ka sí àwọn ìwádìí olómìnira tí ó dá àbá pẹ́ ikọ́ ọmọ ogun ti Nepal fún àlàáfíà ní UN pẹ̀lú àìmọ̀ kó àrùn náà wọ Haiti.
english: The Haitian Institute for Justice and Democracy has referenced independent studies that suggest the Nepalese UN peacekeeping battalion unknowingly brought the disease to Haiti.

yoruba: Danielle Lantagne, tí oh jẹ́ ògbóntarìgì fún UN lórí àìsàn, sọ pé àwọn onílàjà ni ó ṣe e ṣe kí wọn fa àjàkálẹ̀ ná.
english: Danielle Lantagne, a UN expert on the disease, stated the outbreak was likely caused by the peacekeepers.

yoruba: Hamilton fi idi re mule wipe ile iwosan ile eko giga Howard gba alaisan na wole ni ara pipe.
english: Hamilton confirmed Howard University Hospital admitted the patient in stable condition.

yoruba: Aláìsàn náà ti lọ sí ilẹ̀ Nigeria níbiti wọ́n ti ní àwọn ìṣẹ̀lẹ̀ kòkòrò Èbólà kan ti wáyé.
english: The patient had been to Nigeria, where some cases of the Ebola virus have occurred.

yoruba: Ile iwosan naa ti tẹle gbogbo ilana lati dena kokoro, wọn ya awọn ti wọn ni kokoro kuro lati ma ba ko ran awọn to ṣẹku.
english: The hospital has followed protocol for infection control, including separating the patient from others to prevent possible infection of others.

yoruba: Látẹ̀yìnwá, Simpsons Simon ti sisẹ́ lórí ọ́pọ́lọpọ́ àwọn àfihàn ní onírúurú ipò.
english: Before The Simpsons Simon had worked on several shows in various positions.

yoruba: Ni odun 1980s o kópa ninu awon ere bii Taxi, Cheers ati Ere Traci Ulman.
english: During the 1980s he worked on shows such as Taxi, Cheers, and The Tracy Ullman Show.

yoruba: Ní 1989 ó rànwọ́lọ́wọ́ láti ṣe ìdásílẹ̀ The Simpson with Brooks àti Groening, ló jk aṣojú fún gbígba àwọn ẹgbẹ́ àkọ́kọ́ tó ko ètò náà.
english: In 1989 he helped create The Simpsons with Brooks and Groening, and was responsible for hiring the show's first writing team.

yoruba: Pẹ̀lú pé ó kúrò nínú eré náà ni ọdún 1993 orúkọ rẹ̀ sí wà gẹ́gẹ́ bí onígbọ̀wọ́ àgbà, ó sìn ń gba mílíọ̀nù mẹ́wàá dọ́là ní sáà gbogbo nínú ọlạ́.
english: Despite leaving the show in 1993 he kept the title of executive producer, and continued to receive tens of millions of dollars every season in royalties.

yoruba: Sáájú, ilé-iṣẹ́ oníròyìn ilẹ̀ China, Xinhua jábọ̀ ọkọ̀ òfuurufú tí a fẹ́ fi ipá gbà.
english: Earlier the Chinese news agency Xinhua reported a plane to be hijacked.

yoruba: Awon iroyin to gbeyin nigba naa wipe oko oju ofurufu naa gba ihale bombu won de daa pada lo si Afganistani, o de si Kandaha.
english: Later reports then stated the plane received a bomb threat and was diverted back to Afghanistan, landing in Kandahar.

yoruba: Ìròyìn àárọ̀ ní wọ́n dá ọkọ̀ ojú òfúrufú lọ sí Afghanistan lẹ́yìn tí wọ́n dùn wọ́n ní ìbalẹ̀ pàjáwìrì ní Ürümqi.
english: The early reports say the plane was diverted back to Afghanistan after being denied an emergency landing in Ürümqi.

yoruba: Ìjàm̀bá ojú òfúrufú ló wọ́pọ̀ ní ilẹ̀ Iran, èyí tó ní ìtàn pípẹ́ fún àìse àbòjútó tó péye fún ìlò ará ìlú àti ológun.
english: Air accidents are common in Iran, which has an aging fleet that is poorly maintained both for civil and military operations.

yoruba: Òfin ìfìyàjẹni ilẹ̀ òkèrè ti fi lélẹ̀ pẹ kò gbọdọ̀ sí ríra ọkọ̀ ojú òfurufú tuntun.
english: International sanctions have meant that new aircraft cannot be purchased.

yoruba: Níbẹ̀rẹ̀ ọsẹ̀ yìí, ọkọ̀ bàálù abàsíbi ó wùú àwọn ọlọ́pàá sekúpa èèyàn mẹ́ta , nígbàtí àwọn mẹ́tàa min farapa .
english: Earlier this week, a police helicopter crash killed three people and wounded three more.

yoruba: Lóṣù tó kọjá, ilẹ̀ Iran fojú winná àjálù ojú afẹ́fẹ́ tó burú jù lọ́dún nígbàtí ọkọ̀ ojú òfúrufú tó ń lọ sí ilẹ̀ Armenia já lulẹ̀, èyí tó sekúpa ènìyàn méjìdínláàdọ́sàn án tó wà nínú rẹ̀.
english: Last month Iran saw its worst air disaster in years when an airliner heading to Armenia crashed, killing the 168 on board.

yoruba: Ni osu kan naa oko ofurufu miin sa koja oju ona ni Masaadi o de kolu ogiri, awon eyan metadinlogun lo ku.
english: The same month saw another airliner overrun a runway at Mashhad and strike a wall, killing seventeen.

yoruba: Aerosmith ti fagilé ìpàdé orin tó kù ní ìrìn àjò.
english: Aerosmith have cancelled their remaining concerts on their tour.

yoruba: Ẹgbé olórin alàriwo ohùn ti jìtá náà tí sétó làti rin Orílẹ̀-èdè Améríkà àti Canada já títí di Ọjọ́ Kẹrìndínlógún Osù Kẹsàn.
english: The rock band was due to tour the United States and Canada until September 16.

yoruba: Wọ́n ti wọ́gilé ìrìnafẹ́ lẹ́yìn tí asájú orin Steven Tyler nífarapa lẹ́yin tó subú lórí ìtàgé lákòókò tó ń seré lọ́jọ́ karùn ún osù kẹjọ.
english: They have cancelled the tour after lead singer Steven Tyler was injured after he fell off stage while performing on August 5.

yoruba: Mure pàdánù eré kiní ní eré ìdíje titiipa lẹ́yìn tí àwọn okùnrin náà di gbogbo sefi ní sẹ́ti náà.
english: Murray lost the first set in a tie break after both men held each and every serve in the set.

english: Del Potro had the early advantage in the second set, but this too required a tie break after reaching 6-6.

yoruba: Potro gba itoju si ejika re nibayi o si gbiyanju lati pada si ori papa.
english: Potro received treatment to his shoulder at this point but managed to return to the game.

yoruba: Eto naa bere ni dede ago mejo abo ale ti ile naa (15.00 UTC).
english: The program started at 8:30 p.m. local time (15.00 UTC).

english: Famous singers across the country presented bhajans, or devotional songs, to Shri Shyam's feet.

english: Singer Sanju Sharma started the evening, followed by Jai Shankar Choudhary. esented the chhappan bhog bhajan as well. Singer, Raju Khandelwal was accompanying him.

yoruba: Lẹ́yìn náà, Lakkha Singh ni ó gba ìdarí kíkọ orin bhajans.
english: Then, Lakkha Singh took the lead in singing the bhajans.

yoruba: Àwọn Chhappan Bhog méjìdínláàdọ́fà (ni Hinduism, mẹ́rìndílọ́gọ́ta ọ̀tọ̀tọ̀ ohun tó ṣe é jẹ, bíi súùtì èso, ẹ̀pà, ounjẹ abbl. Tí wọ́n gbé fún àwọn òrìṣà) ni wọ́n gbé fún Baba Shyam.
english: 108 plates of Chhappan Bhog (in Hinduism, 56 different edible items, like, sweets, fruits, nuts, dishes etc. which are offered to deity) were served to Baba Shyam.

yoruba: Lakkha Singh ti se ìfàkalẹ̀ chhappan bhog bhajan pẹ̀lú. Olórin Raju Khandelwal ló ń kọ́ọ̀wọ́rìn pẹ̀lú rẹ̀.
english: Lakkha Singh presented the chhappan bhog bhajan as well. Singer, Raju Khandelwal was accompanying him.

yoruba: Nínú àgbékalẹ̀ gbòógì àfihàn géèmù ilẹ̀ Tokyo tọjọ́ àlàmísì, ààrẹ ilẹ̀ Nintendo, Satoru Iwata se àfihàn ìsàkóso àwòrán fún iléẹṣẹ́ tuntun Nintendo Revolution.
english: At Thursday's keynote presentation of the Tokyo Game Show, Nintendo president Satoru Iwata unveiled the controller design for the company's new Nintendo Revolution console.

yoruba: Ó jọ ohun ìdarí ẹ̀rọ amóhùnmáwòrán, ohun tí ẹ ó fi tukọ̀ lo sẹ́ńsọ̀ méjì tí wọ́n gbé sí ẹ̀gbẹ́tẹ̀ifisàn ẹni tí ó ń lòó láti yígun ipo rẹ̀ ni ọnà mẹ́ta ọ̀tọ̀ọ̀tọ̀.
english: Resembling a television remote, the controller uses two sensors placed near the user's television to triangulate its position in three-dimensional space.

yoruba: Èyí máa gba àwọn eléré ìje láàyè láti darí àwọn ìrìnkákiri nínú eré fídíò nípa gbígbé èrọ náà ká nípasẹ̀ afẹ́fẹ. .
english: This will allow players to control actions and movements in video games by moving the device through the air.

yoruba: Jiankalo Fisisela o lè ṣàkóso moto mọ́,ó dẹ̀ mú u kí èrè sísá náà parí lẹ́sẹ̀kẹsẹ̀ tí wọ́n bẹ̀rẹ̀.
english: Giancarlo Fisichella lost control of his car and ended the race very soon after the start.

yoruba: Ọmọ egbẹ́ rẹ̀ Fenando Alonso ló darí fún púpọ̀ nínú ere ìdíjé, ṣùgbọ́n ó parí lẹ́yìn ìdádúro re, bóyá nítorí pé wọn ò fí ojúẹsẹ̀ṣ mọ́tò há dada.
english: His teammate Fernando Alonso was in the lead for most of the race, but ended it right after his pit-stop, probably because a badly tucked right front wheel.

yoruba: Michael Schumacher parí eréje rẹ̀ láìpẹ́ tí Alonso parí nítori ìjàm̀bá ìrọ́kúnlé nínú ọ̀pọ̀ ìjà làkòókò eréje.
english: Michael Schumacher ended his race not long after Alonso, because of the suspension damage in the numerous battles during the race.

yoruba: “Ó lẹ́wà lọ́pọ́, ó tún kọrin dáradára,” ó sọ́ nípa àkọọ́lẹ̀ ìpàdé àwọn oníròyìn.
english: "She’s very cute and sings quite well, too," he said according to a transcript of the news conference.

yoruba: “Gbogbo ìgbà tí a bá se ìgbáradì lóri rẹ̀ ni ara mi ma ń dìde látinú ọkàn mi wá.”
english: "I was moved every time we did a rehearsal on this, from the bottom of my heart."

yoruba: Nígbàtí yíò fi tó ìṣẹ́jú mẹ́ta ìsàfilọ́lẹ̀, ọ̀kan lára èrò ayàwòrán tó wà nínú ọkọ̀ ti sàfihàn ọ̀pọ̀lọpọ̀ ẹyọ fóòmù ìdáàbòòbò tó já kúrò lára ilé ìképosí.
english: Around 3 minutes into the launch, an on-board camera showed numerous pieces of insulation foam break away from the fuel tank.

yoruba: Síbẹ̀síbẹ̀, kò sí ẹ̀ròngbà wípé wọn mú ìdibàjẹ́ bá agbẹ́rù náà.
english: However, they are not thought to have caused any damage to the shuttle.

yoruba: Aládarí ọkọ̀ alálọbọ̀ ti NASA, N.Wayne Hale Jr. sọ ọ́ di mímọ̀ pé fóòmùn ti jábọ́ “lẹ́yìn ìgbà tí ó se kókó fún wa”.
english: NASA's shuttle program chief N. Wayne Hale Jr. said the foam had fallen "after the time we are concerned about."

yoruba: Ìṣẹ́jú márùn ún síwájú idán, ìjì bẹ̀rẹ̀ sí ni jà, bí ìṣẹ́jú kan si, ìjì náà dé orí àádọ́rin kìlómítà láàrín wákàtí kan…léyìn náà ni òjò bẹ̀rẹ̀ sí ni rọ̀ to bẹ́ẹ̀ ti dídún rẹ̀ lára dàbí abẹ́rẹ́, bẹ́ẹ̀ ni yìnyín ń bọ́ láti ojú ọ̀run, gbogbo ẹnìyàn bẹ̀rẹ̀ sí ní wá rìrì, wọ́n ń bẹ̀rù, wọ́n ń pariwo, wọ́n sì ń sá gba orí ara wọn.
english: Five minutes into the display a wind starts rolling in, about a minute later, the wind is reaching 70km/h... then the rain comes, but so hard and so large that it slaps your skin like a needle, then hail fell from the sky, people panicking and screaming and running over each other.

yoruba: Mo pàdánù àbúrò mi obìnrin àti ọ̀rẹ́ rẹ̀, ní ojú ọ̀nà nígbàtí mò ń lọ mo rí àwọn aláàbbọ̀ ara méjì lórí àga, àwọn ènìyàn kàn ti ara wọ́n,” Armand Versace sọ bẹ́ẹ̀.
english: I lost my sister and her friend, and on my way there were two disabled people in wheelchairs, people just jumping over and pushing them," Armand Versace said.

yoruba: NHK ròyìn pe Ilé ìgbìn agbára nukilia Kaṣiwasaki Kariwa ni Nigata kí ń ṣiṣẹ́ dédé tẹ̀lé.
english: NHK also reported that the Kashiwazaki Kariwa nuclear power plant in Niigata prefecture was operating normally.

yoruba: Hokuriku electric power co. jábọ̀ pé kò sí ipa kankan látara ilẹ̀ rírì àti pé àwọn ìsesí kìnní àti ìkejì sí ilé iṣẹ̀ tó ń pèsè agbára apanirun Shika ni wọ́n ti gbé tìpa.
english: Hokuriku Electric Power Co. reported no effects from the earthquake and that the Number 1 and 2 reactors at its Shika nuclear power plant were shut down.

yoruba: A gba pé àwọm ilé tó tó 9400 ní agbègbè náà wà láìsí omo àti pé àwọn tó tó 100 wà láìsí iná.
english: It is reported that some 9400 homes in the region are without water and approximately 100 without electricity.

yoruba: Àwọn ọ̀nà kán ti bàjẹ́ wón to dá reluwé dúró níbi tó kàn Papa Ọkọ̀ Ofurufu Noto ní Iṣikawa wà ní títì.
english: Some roads have been damaged, railway service interrupted in the affected areas, and the Noto Airport in Ishikawa prefecture remains closed.

yoruba: Àdó olóró kan dún gbàmù ní ọfíìsi ̀gbogbogbo gómìnà.
english: One bomb exploded outside the governor general's office.

yoruba: Boomu mẹ́ta bẹ̀ ní ẹ̀gbẹ́ ilé ìjọba laarin wákàtí méjì.
english: Three more bombs exploded near government buildings in a period of two hours.

yoruba: Awon iroyin miin wipe bii eyan mejo lo ku, iroyin osise jeri pe o to 30 to sise; sugbon a o tii mo awon onka ipari.
english: Some reports put the official death toll at eight, and official reports confirm that up to 30 were injured; but final numbers are not yet known.

yoruba: Cyanuric àti melamine ni a rí nínu ìtọ̀ àwọn ohun ọ̀sìn inu ilé tó ti kú lẹ́yìn tí wọ́n jẹ òúnjẹ́ wọ́n tó ti dọ̀tí.
english: Both cyanuric acid and melamine were found in urine samples from pets that died after consuming contaminated pet food.

yoruba: Àwọn ìsopọ̀ méjì náà siṣẹ́pọ̀ pẹ̀lú ara wọn láti di àwọn kìrísítà tí ó lè dí iṣẹ́ kídìnrín lọ́wọ́ gẹ́gẹ́ bí àwọn olùsàyẹ̀wò ilẹ́ẹ̀kọ́ fásitì ti wí.
english: The two compounds react with one another to form crystals that may block kidney function, researchers at the university said.

yoruba: Àwọn oníiwàdí ri wíp wọ́n se kírísítà pẹ̀lú ìtọo olóńgbò pẹ̀lú àdàlù melamine àticyanuric acid.
english: The researchers observed crystals formed in cat urine by the addition of melamine and cyanuric acid.

yoruba: Ẹ̀kún àwọn kristali wọ̀nyí bá ti ìtọ̀ àwọn ẹran ọ̀sìn to kóo nígbàtí a fi wọn wéra pẹ̀lú infrared spectroscopy (FTIR.
english: The composition of these crystals matches those found in the urine of affected pets when compared by infrared spectroscopy (FTIR).

yoruba: Mi kò mọ̀ bóyá ó yé ọ tàbí kò yé ọ, sùgbọ́n ọ̀pọ̀ àwọn ẹrù ni ó dé láti ààrin gbùngbùn America wọ orílèèdè yìí lọ́ọ̀fẹ́.
english: I don't know if you realize it or not, but most of the goods from Central America came into this country duty-free.

yoruba: Bee kede re, mejo ninu idamewa awon oja wa ni o gba owo ori nipase tarifi ni ilu Amerika aarin, aa wo e san.
english: Yet eighty percent of our goods were taxed through tariffs in Central American countries. we treat you.

yoruba: Ìyẹn ò ní ìtunmọ̀ sí mi; dájúdájú kò dára.
english: That didn't seem to make sense to me; it certainly wasn't fair.

yoruba: Ohun gbogbo tí mo má ń sọ fáwọn ènìyàn ni wípé kí o tọ́jú wa bí a se ń tọ́jú rẹ.
english: All I say to people is you treat us the way we treat you.

yoruba: Gomina Kalifonia Arnold Schwarzenegger fowosi ofin titun ti o pa leewo lati ya tabi ta ere fidio to ni ijakadi, si awon omo kekere..
english: California Governor Arnold Schwarzenegger signed into law a bill that bans the sale or rental of violent video games to minors.

yoruba: Àbá náà gba kí wọ́n sa àmì “ọmọ ọdún méjìdínlógún” sí ara eré fọ́ńrán amóhùnmáwòrán ̀ kan tí wọ́n tà ní ìpínlẹ̀ California, kí wọ́n sì ta ọjà wọn fún àwọn ọmọ tí wọn kò ì bàlágà ṣùgbọ́n tí ìjìyà owó ìtanràn tó tó ẹgbẹ̀rún kan dọ́là wà fún.
english: The bill requires violent video games sold in the state of California to be labeled with a decal reading "18" and makes their sale to a minor punishable by a fine of $1000 per offense.

yoruba: Àwọn adarí ìbánirojó Gbogbgbò, Kier Stammer QC, fi ọ̀rọ̀ kan sílẹ̀ ní àárs yìí tí ó ń kéde ìbánirojọ́ ti Huhne àti Pryce.
english: The Director of Public Prosecutions, Kier Starmer QC, gave a statement this morning announcing the prosecution of both Huhne and Pryce.

yoruba: Hune ti fiwe sile, won de ma fi Ed Dafe MP ropo e ni Igbimo. Nomani Lab MP ni a n reti pe o ma gba ise Minista Isowo ti Dafe ma fi sile.
english: Huhne has resigned and he will be replaced in the Cabinet by Ed Davey MP. Norman Lamb MP is expected to take the Business Minister job Davey is vacating.

yoruba: A ṣètò fún Huhne àti Pryce láti farahàn níwájú ilé ẹjọ́ májísírètì Westminster ní ọjọ́o kẹrìndínlógún oṣùu kejì.
english: Huhne and Pryce are scheduled to appear at the Westminster Magistrates Court on February 16.

yoruba: Àwọn tí ó kú ní Nikolasi Aldini, márùndínlọ́gbọ̀n, àti Sakari Kudbaaki, mọ́kànlélógún, Kudbaaki ni ó ń wa ọkọ̀.
english: The fatalities were Nicholas Alden, 25, and Zachary Cuddeback, 21. Cuddeback had been the driver.

yoruba: Edgar Veguilla gba ọgbẹ́ ní apá ati àgbọ̀ǹ nígbà tí Kristoffer Scheider wà ní ipò tó nílò àtúnsẹ isé abẹ fún ojú rè.
english: Edgar Veguilla received arm and jaw wounds while Kristoffer Schneider was left requiring reconstructive surgery for his face.

yoruba: Irinogun Uka rugbá nígbàtí wọ́n kọjú ẹ̀ sórí okùnrin ikarun. Ṣinaida wà nínú ìnira lọ́wọ́, ojú rẹ̀ kan ti fọ́, apá agbári rẹ̀ ti sọnù ati pé apá ojú rẹ̀ kan kikida titaniumu ni wọn fi tún un kọ́.
english: Uka's weapon failed whilst pointed at a fifth man's head. Schneider has ongoing pain, blindness in one eye, a missing section of skull and a face rebuilt from titanium.

yoruba: Ṣínaida fi ìhá-fídíò jẹ́rí láti ibi ọ̀dọ̀ USAF kan nínú orílèdé rẹ̀.
english: Schneider testified via videolink from a USAF base in his homeland.

yoruba: Kọjá ìṣẹ̀lẹ̀ ọjọ́rú, Carpanedo kópa nínú ìdíje orísìí méjì níbi ìfigagbága olúbori.
english: Beyond Wednesday's event, Carpanedo competed in two individual races at the Championships.

yoruba: Esi re akoko ni Slalom, ni ibi ti o ti gba esi wipe ko pari idije. Merindinlogoji ninu awon olukopa merindinlogofa ni o ni esi kanna ninu idije naa.
english: Her first was the Slalom, where she earned a Did Not Finish in her first run. 36 of the 116 competitors had the same result in that race.

yoruba: Ó parí ní ipò kẹ́wà ní ẹgbẹ́ ìjóko obinrin ní eré ikeji rẹ̀ Slalomi nla, pẹ̀lú àkọ́kọ́ ìsáré àpapọ̀ ìṣẹ́jú 4:41:30, 2:11:60 èyí tí kò sáré tó onípò kíní Klodia Loeṣi ara Ostria àti ìṣẹ́jú 1:09:02 tí kò yá tó onípò kẹ́san Giyongi Dani ará Ongari.
english: Her other race, the Giant Slalom, saw her finish in tenth in the women's sitting group with a combined run time of 4:41.30, 2:11.60 minutes slower than first place finisher Austrian Claudia Loesch and 1:09.02 minutes slower than the ninth place finisher Gyöngyi Dani of Hungary.

yoruba: Àwọn afòlókè mẹ́rin nínú ẹgbẹ́ àwọn obìnrin kùnà láti parí àwọn iṣẹ́ wọn, àti márùndínláàdọ́ta nínú mẹ́tàdínlọ́gọ́fà àwọn olùfò nínú ikọ̀ Giant Slalom ló kùnà láti rípò nínú ìdíje.
english: Four skiers in the women's sitting group failed to finish their runs, and 45 of the 117 total skiers in the Giant Slalom failed to rank in the race.

yoruba: Awon Olopa Madhya Pradesi ri komputa foonu agbeko ti won ji gba pada.
english: The Madhya Pradesh Police recovered the stolen laptop and mobile phone.

yoruba: Ìgbákẹjì Ayèwò Gbogbogbò D K Arya sṣ wípé, “ A ti fi pánpé mú àwọn márùn tó fi ipá ba´ obìnrin Swiss náà lòpọ̀ a si ti gba èrọ ìbánisọ̀rọ̀ àti kọ̀mpútà rẹ padà.
english: Deputy Inspector General D K Arya said, "We have arrested five persons who raped the Swiss woman and recovered her mobile and laptop".

yoruba: Àwọn tí wọ́n fi ẹ̀sùn kan ní orúkọ wọ́n jẹ́ Baba Kanjar, Bhutha Kanjar, Rampro Kanjar, Gaza Kanjar àti Vishnu Kanjar.
english: The accused are named as Baba Kanjar, Bhutha Kanjar, Rampro Kanjar, Gaza Kanjar and Vishnu Kanjar.

yoruba: Ọ̀ga Olópa wípé àwọn boju ẹlẹ́wọ̀n wá sí ilé ẹjọ́.
english: Police superintendent Chandra Shekhar Solanki said the accused appeared in court with covered faces.

yoruba: Biotilejepe àwọn èyàn wà nílé náà nígbàtí mọ́́tò kọlù, kò sí kan nínú wọn tó ṣiṣe.
english: Although three people were inside the house when the car impacted it, none of them were hurt.

yoruba: Sugbon, awako sese sori gidi.
english: However, the driver sustained serious injuries to the head.

yoruba: Wọn ti ọpọpọna ti ijamba naa ti ṣẹlẹ ni titipa fun gba diẹ nigbati awọn oṣiṣẹ pajawiri gbiyanju lati yọ awakọ naa kuro ninu ọkọ Audi TT pupa.
english: The road where the crash happened was temporarily closed while emergency services freed the driver from the red Audi TT.

yoruba: Ọkùnrin náà kọ́kọ́ wà ní ilé ìwòsàn James Paget ní ìlú ńlá Great Yarmouth.
english: He was initially hospitalised in the James Paget Hospital in Great Yarmouth.

yoruba: Wọ́n padà gbé lọ sí ilé ìwòsàn Addenbrooke tí ó wà ní Cambridge.
english: He was subsequently relocated to Addenbrooke's Hospital in Cambridge.

yoruba: Adékọ̀yà ti wà ní ilé ẹjọ́ Edinburg pẹ́lù ẹ̀sùn pé ó pa ọmọ rẹ̀.
english: Adekoya has since been in Edinburgh Sheriff Court charged with murdering her son.

yoruba: Ó wà ní àhámọ́ títí dí àkókò ìgbẹ́jọ́ àti ìdájọ́, àmọ́ ó se é se kí wọ́n tọwọ́ bọ ẹ̀rí nítorí pé wọ́n ti gbé àwòrán rẹ̀ kiri jù.
english: She is in custody pending indictment and trial, but any eyewitness evidence may be tainted because her image has been widely published.

yoruba: Èyí lọ̀pọ̀ ohun tí wọ́n má ń se ní US àmọ́ ìdàjọ́ ilẹ̀ Scottish siṣẹ́ yàtọ̀ àti wípé ilé ẹjọ́ má ń ka àwọn àwòrán tí wọ́n bá gbé síta bí ohun tó lè fa ẹlẹ́yàmẹ̀yà.
english: This is common practice elsewhere in the UK but Scottish justice works differently and courts have viewed publication of photos as potentially prejudicial.

yoruba: Omowe Pamela Fegusini tí Ilé Ẹ̀kọ́ Gíga Dondi wipe erékéré ní awon oníròyìn tó n ya àwòrán àwọn tí a fẹ̀sùn kàn, n ṣe.”
english: Professor Pamela Ferguson of the University of Dundee notes "journalists do seem to be walking a dangerous line if publishing photos etc of suspects."

yoruba: Crown Office, ọ́fíísì tó wà nídìí ìbániṣẹjọ́, ti ṣàfihàn rẹ̀ fún àwọn oníròyìn pé òhun kò ní fèsì kankan mọ títí dìgbà ìdájọ́.
english: Crown Office, which is in overall charge of prosecutions, has indicated to journalists that no further comment will be made at least until indictment.

yoruba: Àwọn àkọọ́lẹ̀ gẹ́gẹ́ bí ó ti se jẹ jáde, ni ó ma tọ́ka sí ìfànfà ẹnubodè, léyìí tí ilẹ̀ Palestine fẹ́ níbàámun pẹ̀lú bí ibodè se wà sáájú oogun Mideast lọ́dún 1967.
english: The document, according to the leak, will refer to the borders dispute, which Palestine wants based on the borders before the 1967 Mideast War.

yoruba: Ara awon akori ti eto naa dale lori ni oro ojo iwaju ile olominira ti Jerrusalemu ti o se koko si orile ede mejeeji ati ti oro petele Jordani.
english: Other topics covered reportedly include the future state of Jerusalem which is sacred to both nations and the Jordan Valley issue.

yoruba: Isreli bere fun isoju omo ogun ni petele naa fun odun mewa ni kete ti won pa ti kowesile nigba naa PA gba pe ohun ma fi isoju naa le fun odun marun nikan.
english: Israel demands an ongoing military presence in the valley for ten years once an agreement is signed while the PA agrees to leave such presence only for five years.

yoruba: Àwọn tó yìnbọ ní ìtọ́wò àgbóguntí kòkòrò ní láti ní alábòjútó, bí ṣọ́ ìtọ́wò náà àti bí ó ṣe ní ipa tọ́.
english: Shooters in the supplementary pest control trial were to be closely supervised by rangers, as the trial was monitored and its effectiveness evaluated.

yoruba: Ní àjọsepọ̀ pẹ̀lú NPWS àti ẹgbẹ́ Sporting Shooters Association of Australia (NSW) Inc, wọ́n gba àwọn olùfínúfíndọ̀ tó pegedé lábẹ́ ètò ìdọdẹ ẹgbẹ́ Sporting Shooters Association.
english: In a partnership of NPWS and the Sporting Shooters Association of Australia (NSW) Inc, qualified volunteers were recruited, under the Sporting Shooters Association's hunting program.

yoruba: Nípasẹ̀ ọ̀rọ̀ Mik Oflini, Olùdarí Eré Ṣíṣe Pak Conserfaṣọ́nì àti Ẹ́rítájì pẹ̀lú NPWS; àwọn check mẹ́rẹ̀rin tí wón yàn fún ìjade ìbọn yínyìn àkọ́kọ́, rí àmọ̀ràn ìdáàbòbò àti ìtọ́nisọ́nà tí ó péye gbà.
english: According to Mick O'Flynn, the Acting Director Park Conservation and Heritage with the NPWS, the four shooters selected for the first shooting operation received comprehensive safety and training instruction.

yoruba: Martelly búra ninú ìgbìmọ̀ elétò ìdìbò tuntun ẹlẹ́ni mẹ́sàn-án ni àn.
english: Martelly swore in a new Provisional Electoral Council (CEP) of nine members yesterday.

yoruba: Odún Kaàrún CEP Martelly nìyí,ni ọdún mẹ́rin síi.
english: It is Martelly's fifth CEP in four years.

yoruba: Ní oṣù tó kọjá, ìgbìmọ̀ àrẹ kan filọ̀ pe kí CEP tó wà nípò tẹ́lẹ̀, fi iṣẹ́ lẹ̀ gẹ́gẹ́ bí ara àwọn igbese láti mú kí ilu ṣe ìbò titun.
english: Last month a presidential commission recommended the prior CEP's resignation as part of a package of measures to move the country towards new elections.

yoruba: Comisooni naa je esi Mateli si itako ijoba to wopo to si bere ni Owara.
english: The commission was Martelly's response to widespread anti-regime protests that started in October.

yoruba: Àì lè ṣe ìdìbò ló má ń fa àwọn ìfẹ̀hónú tó le hàn ní ọ̀pọ̀ ìgbà, àwọn kan ní 2011.
english: The sometimes-violent protests were triggered by failure to hold elections, some due since 2011.

yoruba: Láàrin àwọn Ípọọ̀dù ọgọ́ta tó ń se bálabàla ní wọ́m ní ó ń gbóná lala, tó fa iná mẹ́fà tí àwọn èèyàn mẹ́rìn in sì farapa yánayàna.
english: Around 60 cases of malfunctioning iPods overheating have been reported, causing a total of six fires and leaving four people with minor burns.

yoruba: Ilé-ìṣe òjisẹ ajé, ìṣòwò àti ilé ìṣe sọ wípé o ti di mímọ wípé ìjàmbá mẹ́tàdínlógbọn lo ti ṣẹlẹ̀ nípasẹ̀ àwọn èrò náà.
english: Japan's Ministry of Economy, Trade and Industry (METI) said that it had been aware of 27 accidents related to the devices.

yoruba: Lọ́sẹ̀ tó kọjá, METI kéde pé iléeṣẹ́ Apple ti fi ìṣẹ̀lẹ̀ ìgbónájù mẹ́rìnlélọ́gbọ̀n tó òhun léti, èyí tí iléeṣẹ́ pè ní “àìkójú-òsùwọ̀n.”
english: Last week, METI announced that Apple had informed it of 34 additional overheating incidents, which the company called "non-serious."

yoruba: Ilé iṣẹ́ náà dáhùn nípa pípè fún sísún ìjábọ̀ iṣẹ́ Apple síwájú ní “èyí tó seni láàánú jọjọ.”
english: The ministry responded by calling Apple's postponement of the report "truly regrettable."

yoruba: Ìṣẹlẹ ilẹ̀ ríru náà kọlu Mariana ní dédé agogo méje kọjá ìṣẹ́jú mọ́kàndínlógún àárọ̀, tí ó sì jẹ́ (09:19 p.m. GMT ọjọ́ ẹtì) ní àsìkò tiwa.
english: The eathquake struck Mariana at 07:19 a.m. local time (09:19 p.m. GMT Friday).

yoruba: Ọ́fìsì tó ń rísí ìsàkósó ìṣẹ̀lẹ̀ pàjáwìrì fún ẹkùn Northern Marianas ti sọ ọ́ di mímọ̀ wípé kò sí àkọsílẹ̀ ìbàjẹ́ kankan ní orílẹ̀èdè náà.
english: The Northern Marianas emergency management office said that there were no damages reported in the nation.

yoruba: Bákanáà Ibùdó Ìkìls Pacific Tsunami sọ pé kò sí ìtọ́kasí Tsunami.
english: Also the Pacific Tsunami Warning Center said that there was no Tsunami indication.

yoruba: Ọlọ́pàá Filipino ní ìgbà kan rí ti fi àwọn arìnrìnàjò afẹ́ láti Hong Kong sí àhámọ́ nípa fífi agídí gba ọkọ̀ bọ́ọ̀sì wọn lọ́wọ́ wọn ni Manila, tí ó jẹ́ olú ìlú Philippines.
english: A former Filipino policeman has kept Hong Kong tourists hostage by hijacking their bus in Manila, the capital of the Philippines.

yoruba: Roland Mendoza yin ìbọn rẹ M16re mọ́ àwọn arìnrìnàjò.
english: Rolando Mendoza fired his M16 rifle at the tourists.

yoruba: Ọ̀pọ̀lọpọ̀ àwọn tí a mú ní ìdógó ni wọ́n ti gbà kalẹ̀, ṣùgbọ́n bíi mẹ́fà ni wọ́n sàfimúnlẹ̀ pé wọ́n ti kú.
english: Several hostages have been rescued and least six have been confirmed dead so far.

yoruba: Àwọn mẹ́fà tí a gbé pamọ́ pẹ̀lú àwọn ọmọdé àti àgbàlagbà ni wọ́n ti fi sílẹ̀ àti àwọn Filipino tó jẹ́ ayàwòrán.
english: Six hostages, including the children and elderly, were released early, as were the Filipino photographers.

yoruba: Àwọn olùyàwòrán padà gba ipò obìnrin arúgbó kan nítorí ó lọ́ sí ilé ìtúnraṣe. Wọ́n yín Mendoza níbọn pa.
english: The photographers later took the place of an aged lady as she needed the lavatory. Mendoza was gunned down.

yoruba: Ń se ni Liggins tọpasẹ̀ baba rẹ, lọ kẹ́ẹ̀kọ́ ìmọ̀ ìsègùn òyìnbó.
english: Liggins followed in his father’s footsteps and entered a career in medicine.

yoruba: Ó kọ́sẹ́ bí i oníṣẹ́ abẹ aláboyún, bẹ̀rẹ̀ iṣẹ́ ni ilé ìwòsàn National Women ní Auckland lọ́dún 1959.
english: He trained as an obstetrician and began to work at the Auckland's National Women's Hospital in 1959.

yoruba: Nígbà tí ó ń siṣẹ́ ní ilé ìwòsàn, Liggins bẹ̀rẹ̀ ìwáàdí láwọn àkòko tí kò ní iṣẹ́ lọ́wọ́ lórí ìrọbí àìtọ́jọ́.
english: While he was working at the hospital Liggins began to investigate premature labor during his spare time.

yoruba: Ìwáàdí rẹ̀ sàfihàn wípé bí wọ́n bá lo òmóònù, yíò mún kí ẹ̀dọ̀fọ́ró ọmọoyún tètè dàgbà.
english: His research showed that if a hormone was administered it would speed up the baby's foetal lung maturation.

yoruba: Xinhua sọ ọ́ di mímọ̀ wípé àwọn asèwádìí ìjọba ti gba àpótí dúdú àkọọ́lẹ̀ ogun méjì padà lọ́jọ́ọ̀rú.
english: Xinhua reported that government investigators recovered two 'black box' flight recorders on Wednesday.

yoruba: Àwọn ẹlẹ́gbé oníìjàkadì náà gbóríyìn fún Luna.
english: Fellow wrestlers also paid tribute to Luna.

english: Tommy Dreamer said "Luna was the first Queen of Extreme. My first manager. Luna passed away on the night of two moons. Pretty unique just like her. Strong woman."

english: Dustin "Goldust" Runnels commented that "Luna was as freaky as me...maybe even more...love her and will miss her...hopefully she's in a better place."

yoruba: Nínú àwọn ènìyàn egbèje tí wọ́n rí saájú ètò ìdìbò àpapọ̀ ọdún 2010, àwọn tí ó takoìdi orílẹ̀ olómìnira ilẹ̀ Australia láti ọdún 2008 fi ìdá mẹ́jọ pọ̀ si.
english: Out of 1,400 people polled prior to the 2010 federal election, those who oppose Australia becoming a republic grew by 8 per cent since 2008.

english: Caretaker Prime Minister Julia Gillard claimed during the campaign of the 2010 federal election that she believed Australia should become a republic at the end of Queen Elizabeth II's reign.

yoruba: Ìdá mẹ́rìnlélọ́gbọ̀n àwọn tó wà níbi ìdìbò ló ní ìwòye yìí, pé kí Queen Elizabeth II jẹ́ orí adé ilẹ̀ Australia tó kẹ́yìn.
english: 34 per cent of those in the poll share this view, wanting Queen Elizabeth II to be Australia's last monarch.

yoruba: Ní apá kan ní idìbo ìdámẹ́wa 29 ló wà àwọn ìwádí gbagbo wipe Ọstrelia mato di ripobiliki n gẹ́lẹ́ ti o ṢeéṢe idamewa 31 wipe kí Ostrelia ma di ripobiliki rara.
english: At the extremes of the poll, 29 per cent of those surveyed believe Australia should become a republic as soon as possible, while 31 per cent believe Australia should never become a republic.

yoruba: Asiwaju oniwúra ti Olimpiki ló yẹ kó wẹ̀ ní eré 100m àti 200m igbasile àti eré ikọlẹ mẹ́ta ní Ìdíje Olimpiki, ṣùgbọ́n torípé ó fohùn lẹ̀ wọn ṣàríyanjiyàn bó ya ara rẹ̀ yá gá.
english: The Olympic gold medalist was due to swim in the 100m and 200m freestyle and in three relays at the Commonwealth Games, but due to his complaints his fitness has been in doubt.

yoruba: Kò tí ì le lo àwọn òògùn tó nílò láti borí ìnira rẹ̀ níbí wọ́n se ti fòfin dèwọ́n nínú ìdíje.
english: He has been unable to take the drugs needed to overcome his pain as they are banned from the Games.

yoruba: Curtis Cooper, onímọ̀ ìsirò àti ọ̀jọ̀gbọ́n ìmọ̀ ìjìnlẹ̀ ẹ̀rọ ayárabíàṣà nílé ẹ̀kọ́ gíga fásitì Central Missouri ti se àwárí prime number tó pọ̀ jùlọ tí a mọ̀ ní ọjọ́ karùndínlọ́gbọ̀n osù kínni.
english: Curtis Cooper, a mathematician and computer science professor at the University of Central Missouri, has discovered the largest known prime number to date on January 25.

yoruba: Ọ̀pọ̀lọpọ̀ ló sé àfimúnlẹ̀ àwárí lílo orísìírísìí ohun èlò bí yó bá fi di Osù Kejì àti wípé wọ́n kéde rẹ̀ lọ́jọ́ ìṣẹ́gun.
english: Several people verified the discovery using different hardware and software by the beginning of February and it was announced on Tuesday.

yoruba: O ṣeéṣe kí kọ́mẹ́tì jọ́nà tí omí ma ń fi dé ilé ayé pẹ̀lù nkan ọ̀gáníkì tó ní protini nínú tó ṣì n gbe èémí lẹ́yìn.
english: Comets may possibly have been a source of water delivery to the earth along with organic matter that can form proteins and support life.

yoruba: Àwọn onímọ̀ ìjìnlẹ̀ lóye bí ilé àyé ṣe wáyé, pàápàá bí ilé orí ilẹ̀ ṣe dáyé, nìgbàtí ǹkan bí yìnyín àti eruku kọlu Orí ilẹ̀ ní ọ̀pọ̀lọpọ̀ ìgbà sẹ́yìn.
english: Scientists hope to understand how planets form, especially how the Earth formed, since comets collided with the Earth long ago.

yoruba: Cuomo, ọmọ ọdún mẹtàléláàdọ́ta, bẹ̀rẹ̀ iṣẹ gẹ́gẹ́ bíi gómínà ní ìbẹ̀rẹ̀ ọdún yìí, bákan ni ó sì sọ àbá kí ọkùnrin máa fẹ́ ọkùnrin àti kí obìnrin máa fẹ́ obìnrin di àṣẹ.
english: Cuomo, 53, began his governorship earlier this year and signed a bill last month legalizing same-sex marriage.

yoruba: O ṣàfiwé àwọn àhesọ gẹ́gẹ́ bí “ìsọkúsọ òṣèlù àti ọ̀rọ̀ ọ̀dẹ̀”.
english: He referred to the rumors as "political chatter and silliness".

yoruba: Àsọtẹ́lẹ̀ ti wà nípa ọkùnrin náà pé yíò díje dunpò ààrẹ lọ́dún 2016.
english: He is speculated to make a run for president in 2016.

yoruba: NextGen jẹ́ ọ̀nà tí FAA sọ pé wọ́n ma fàyègba ọks òfurufú láti fo ní ibi ti kò jìnà wan sì ma tọ̀jú gálanù epo.
english: NextGen is a system the FAA claims would allow aircraft to fly shorter routes and save millions of gallons of fuel each year and cut carbon emissions.

yoruba: O ṣe àmúlò ìmọ̀ ẹ̀rọ tí orísun rè jẹ́ sátáláìtì, èyí tí o yàtò sí àfiwé ìmọ̀ ẹ̀rọ orílẹ̀ àtijó láti fi àyè gba àwọn adarí láti tọ́ka sí ọkọ̀ òfuurufú ní pàtó àti láti fún àwọn awako ní àwọn iròyìn tó ṣe déédéé .
english: It uses satellite-based technology as opposed to older ground-radar-based technology to allow air traffic controllers to pinpoint aircraft with greater precision and give pilots more accurate information.

yoruba: Kò sí àlékún owó ìrìnnà, àwọn ọkọ̀ ojú irin abẹ́ ilẹ̀ kò ní dúró ní Wembley, àwọn ibi ìgbọ́kọ̀sí kò sí nílẹ̀ lábẹ́ ilẹ̀.
english: No extra transport is being put on and overground trains will not stop at Wembley, and car parking and park-and-ride facilities are unavailable at the ground.

yoruba: Ìbẹ̀rùbojo àísi ohun ìrìnnà mú kí o sẹésẹ pe ìdíjẹ náà kò ní lẹ wáyẹ́ ní ojútáyẹ́ làísí àwọn alátìlẹyìn níbẹ̀.
english: Fears of lack of transportation raised the possibility that the game would be forced to play behind closed doors without the team's supporters.

yoruba: Igbejade iwadi kan ni Ojobo ninu iwe iroyin imo ijinle jabo lori iparapo eya eye tuntun kan lori erekusu Ecuadorean Galapagos.
english: A study published on Thursday in the journal Science reported on formation of a new bird species on the Ecuadorean Galápagos Islands.

yoruba: Àwọn aṣèwádìí láti ilé ẹ̀kọ́ gíga Princeton ní United States àti Ilé ẹ̀ka gíga Uppsala ní sweden jábọ̀ ìròyìn ẹ̀yà tuntun ní ó wà ní ìran méjì, ṣùgbọ́n ètò yìí ma ń gba àkókò, látàrí dídàgbà láàrín endemic Darwin finch, Geospiza forted, àti ìṣílọ cactus finch, Geospiza conirostris.
english: Researchers from Princeton University in the United States and Uppsala University in Sweden reported the new species evolved in just two generations, though this process had been believed to take much longer, due to breeding between an endemic Darwin finch, Geospiza fortes, and the immigrant cactus finch, Geospiza conirostris.

yoruba: A lè kan wúra sí àpere púpọ̀, a lè yíi sí àpere kékèké.
english: Gold may be worked into all sorts of shapes. It can be rolled into tiny shapes.

yoruba: Wọ́n lè fàá sínu wáyà tó tínrín, tí a lè yí tí a tún lè dì a lè gbá ǹkan le lórí tàbí kí a we sínu aṣọ.
english: It can be pulled into thin wire, which can be twisted and plaited. It can be hammered or rolled into sheets.

yoruba: O ṣeeṣe ni tinrin, ti a o si fi bo irin miran. o si ṣeeṣe ni ọna ti o jẹ wipe yio ṣe lo lati fi ṣe ẹṣo si awon aworan alafowoya ninu awọn iwe ti a n pe ni “Illuminated manuscript.”
english: It can be made very thin, and stuck onto other metal. It can be made so thin that it was sometimes used to decorate the hand-painted pictures in books called "illuminated manuscripts".

yoruba: Eléyìí ni à ń pè ní pH apòògùn. O lè se asàmì pẹ̀lú lílo ohun ẹlẹ́rìndòdò pupa ti kábèjì.
english: This is called a chemical's pH. You can make an indicator using red cabbage juice.

yoruba: Omi kabeeji maa paro awo eyi dalelori oye asidi tabi besiki (alkalaini) to wa ninu kemika naa.
english: The cabbage juice changes color depending on how acidic or basic (alkaline) the chemical is.

yoruba: Átọka ìwọ̀n áásìdì sàfihàn nípasè iyẹ Hydrogen ( H nínú pH) mólẹ́kù nínú kẹ́míkàli tí a sẹ àyẹ̀wò rẹ.
english: The pH level is indicated by the amount of Hydrogen (the H in pH) ions in the tested chemical.

yoruba: Hydrogen ions jẹ́ protons tó ní electron tí wọ́n bọ́ lára wọn (nítoríHydrogen atoms ní proton kan àti electron kan).
english: Hydrogen ions are protons that had their electrons stripped off them (since Hydrogen atoms consist of one proton and one electron).

yoruba: Po àwọn eruku gbígbẹ̀ méjì pọ̀ kí o wa mún ọ̀wọ́ tútù tó mọ́ fi fún wọn pọ̀ di bọ́ọ̀lù.
english: Swirl the two dry powders together and then, with clean wet hands, squeeze them into a ball.

yoruba: Ọ̀rinrin ọwọ́ rẹ yíò dáhùn kíákíá sì ìpele tó wà níta, èyí tí yíò rí bákan tàbí se àkójọ ìkaraun.
english: The moisture on your hands will react with the outer layers, which will feel funny and form a sort of shell.

yoruba: Àwọn ìlú Harappa àti Mohenjo-daro fẹẹrẹ ní ilé ìgbònsẹ̀ oní kọ̀lọ̀fìn omi ní gbogbo ilé, ti o so mọ ìlànà kógbè-kógbè ẹlẹgẹ́.
english: The cities of Harappa and Mohenjo-daro had a flush toilet in almost every house, attached to a sophisticated sewage system.

yoruba: A ti rí ṣálángá tó ṣẹkù nínú àwọn ilé ní agbègbè Minoan ti Crete àti Santorini ní Greece.
english: Remains of sewage systems have been found in the houses of the Minoan cities of Crete and Santorini in Greece.

yoruba: Ile igbonse wa ni Ijipti, Pesia ati Saina atijo. Ni Roomu, ile igbonse maa n saba wa lara ibi iwe gbogboogbo ni ibi ti okunrin ati obinrin ma n jo wapo.
english: There were also toilets in ancient Egypt, Persia and China. In Roman civilization, toilets were sometimes part of public bath houses where men and women were together in mixed company.

yoruba: Ti o ba pe eniti o jina si o pelu egberun maili, ero ayelujara lo n lo.
english: When you call someone who is thousands of miles away, you are using a satellite.

yoruba: Sátálàìtì tó wà nínú òfúrufú ń gba ìpè ó sì tún sàfihàn rẹ̀ pádà sílẹ̀ lẹ́sẹ̀kẹsẹ̀.
english: The satellite in space gets the call and then reflects it back down, almost instantly.

yoruba: Wọ́n ran satalaiti ló sí spéèsì pẹ̀lú rokeeti. Àwọn onimo sayensi ma n lo teliskoopi ni sleesi nitori pe afẹ́fẹ́ ma n kóba ina wa ati nkan to ba ri.
english: The satellite was sent into space by a rocket. Scientists use telescopes in space because the Earth’s atmosphere distorts some of our light and view.

yoruba: O pẹ fún rọkẹtì nla tó ga jù ìwọ̀n ẹsẹ́ ọgọ́rùn kan lọ láti gbẹ satelaiti tábì àwọn ẹ̀rọ́ ìwòyẹ sínú òfuruufú.
english: It takes a giant rocket over a 100 feet high to put a satellite or telescope in space.

yoruba: Wíìlì ti yí àyé padà ní ọ̀nà púpọ̀. Nkan tó tóbi jùlọ tí wíìlì ṣe fún wa ni pé o mú ìrìn àjò yá àti rọrùn fún wa.
english: The wheel has changed the world in incredible ways. The biggest thing that the wheel has done for us is given us much easier and faster transportation.

yoruba: Ó ti mún ọkọ̀ ojú irin, ọkọ̀ ilẹ̀ àtàwọn ohun èlò ìrìnnà míràn wá.
english: It has brought us the train, the car, and many other transportation devices.

yoruba: Àwọn olóógbò kéèké tó má ń jẹ àwọn àpajẹ kéèkèé bẹ̀rẹ̀ láti orí ehiri lọ sí ìgalà àti àgbọ̀nrín.
english: Under them are more medium sized cats that eat medium sized prey ranging from rabbits to antelopes and deer.

yoruba: NÍgbẹ̀yìn, ọ̀pọ̀lọpọ̀ àwọn olóógbò kéèké (nínú èyí tí olóógbó igbó wà) ó má ń jẹ ọ̀pọ̀lọpọ̀ àpajẹ bíi kòkòrò, èkúté, aláǹgbá àti ẹyẹ.
english: Finally, there are many small cats (including loose pet cats) that eat the far more numerous small prey like insects, rodents, lizards, and birds.

yoruba: Àṣírí àṣeyọrí wọn ní ìmọ̀ nípa níṣì, iṣẹ́ àkànṣe tí ológbò kookan lè ṣe láyì díje pẹ̀lú àwọn míì.
english: The secret to their success is the concept of the niche, a special job each cat holds that keeps it from competing with others.

yoruba: Kìnìún ni ẹranko ológbò tó lajú jù, tí o máa n wà ní agbo ńlá tí a sì máa ń pè ní olólà ijù.
english: Lions are the most social cats, living in large groups called prides.

yoruba: Àkójọpọ̀ kínìú jẹ́ akọ métà, pẹ̀lú ọgbọ̀n abo àti àwọn ọmọ wọn.
