english: Prides are made up of one to three related adult males, along with as many as thirty females and cubs.

yoruba: Àwọn obìnrin wọn ma ń súma ara wọn, nítorí wọ́n jẹ́ ẹbí tó tóbi tóní àwọn ọmọ obìnri.
english: The females are usually closely related to each other, being a large family of sisters and daughters.

yoruba: Àwọn àkópọ̀ kìnìhún máà wùwà bí àpẹ́jọpọ̀ ìkookò tàbí ajá, ìyanilẹ́nu ló jẹ́ wípẹ́ awon ẹranko bi kìnìhún (àmọ́ tí kì í se àwọn olóńgbò tó tóbi) ní ìhùwàsí, pẹ̀lú pé wọ́n léwu fún àwọn ẹranko ìjẹ..
english: Lion prides act much like packs of wolves or dogs, animals surprisingly similar to lions (but not other big cats) in behavior, and also very deadly to their prey.

yoruba: Eléré ìje tó dá ń tọ́, ẹkùn lè gùn ún (ó lè má tọ̀ọ́ dáadáa)lúwẹ̀ẹ́, fo ibi tó jínna, bẹ́ẹ̀ ló lè fa ohun tí èèyàn tó sángun lè fà fún ìgbà márùn ún.
english: A well rounded athlete, the tiger can climb (though not well), swim, leap great distances and pull with five times the force of a strong human.

yoruba: Ẹkùn náà wà nínu ẹgbk kanáà (Genus Panthera) gẹ́gk bí kìnìú, àmọ̀tékù, àti olóngbò ńlá. Àwọn olọ́ngbò mẹrin wọ̀nyí nìkan ló lè dún.
english: The tiger is in the same group (Genus Panthera) as lions, leopards, and jaguars. These four cats are the only ones who can roar.

yoruba: Kikun ekun ko dabi ti kinikun to kun, sugbon o dabi gbolohun aso, oro onigbe.
english: The tiger's roar is not like the full-voiced roar of a lion, but more like a sentence of snarly, shouted words.

yoruba: Àwọn olóńgbò jẹranjẹran féràn láti ma jẹ ẹranko kekeke. Wọn a mú àwọn ọ̀bọ, ejò, ẹ̀kútẹ́ àti ẹyẹ tí ọwọ́ wọn bá tẹ̀. Bí i gbogbo ẹranko ti olóńgbo jẹranjẹran fi n ṣe ọdẹ rẹ ni o kẹ́rẹ́ juu lọ.
english: Ocelots like to eat small animals. They will catch monkeys, snakes, rodents and birds if they can. Almost all of the animals that the ocelot hunts are far smaller than it is.

yoruba: Àwọn onímọ̀ sáyẹnsì gbàgbọ́ pé àwọn Osiloti ma ń fí oorun wá àti tẹ̀lé eranko tí wọn fẹ́ jẹ.
english: Scientists think that ocelots follow and find animals to eat (prey) by smell, sniffing for where they've been on the ground.

yoruba: Wọ́n ríran dada ní òkùnkùn pẹ̀lú iràn òkùnkùn àti pe wọn ma n yọ́ rìn náà. Àwọn Osiloti ma n pa àwọ̀ da láti bá àyíká rẹ̀ rẹ́ kó ba lè bà léàwọn eranko tí wọ́n ma jẹ.
english: They can see very well in the dark with night vision, and move very stealthily, too. Ocelots hunt their prey by blending in with their surroundings then pouncing on their prey.

yoruba: Nígbà tí ẹgbẹ́ kékeré nǹkan abẹ̀mí (tó kéré níye) bá yẹra kúrò lọ́dọ̀ àwọn olùgbé àkọ́kọ́ tíwọ́n ti wá (bí i kí wọ́n kúrò lórí òkè àbí odò tàbí tí wọ́n bá kó lọ sí erékùsù tuntun kí wọ́n má lè padà mọ́) wọ́n yíò ri wípé wọ́n bára wọn ní àyíká tó yàtọ̀ ju ibi tí wọ́n wà tẹ́lẹ̀ lọ.
english: When a small group of living things (a small population) gets separated from the main population that they came from (like if they move over a mountain range or a river, or if they move to a new island so that they can't easily move back) they will often find themselves in a different environment than they were in before.

yoruba: Agbègbè tuntun ní ọ̀pọ̀lọpọ̀ ohun àlùmọ́nì àti àwọn orogún òwò lóríṣìíríṣìí, fún ìdí èyí iye ènìyàn tuntun yóò nílò oríṣìíríṣìí nǹkan láti ní okun tí wọ́n fi lè kọjú àwọn tí wọ́n jọ ń sorogún.
english: This new environment has different resources and different competitors, so the new population will need different features or adaptations to be a strong competitor than what they had needed before.

yoruba: Ojúlówó iye kò yípadà rárá, wọ́n túbọ̀ nílò láti ṣe àmúlò ẹ̀da bíí ti àtijọ́.
english: The original population hasn't changed at all, they still need the same adaptations as before.

yoruba: Bí àkókò ṣe ń lọ, bí àwọn olùgbé titun ṣe n yípadà sí àyíká wọn, wọn ò ní fibẹ́ẹ̀ jọ àwọn olùgbé tókù.
english: Over time, as the new population begins to adapt to their new environment, they start to look less and less like the other population.

yoruba: Láìpẹ́, léyìn egberun tàbi mílíọ́nù ọdún, iye àwọn èèyàn náà ò ní jọra tó jẹ́ pé a ò lè pe wọ́n ní ęgbẹ́ alàyè kán náà.
english: Eventually, after thousands or even millions of years, the two populations will look so different that they can't be called the same species.

yoruba: A pè ìṣètò yìí ní ẹ̀yà, èyí tó túms sí pé ẹ̀yà tuntun. Ẹ̀yà jẹ́ àtubọ̀tán tí a kò lè yẹra fún ó sì jẹ́ ǹkan pàtàkì fún ìtànkálẹ̀.
english: We call this process speciation, which just means the formation of new species. Speciation is an unavoidable consequence and a very important part of evolution.

yoruba: Awon ewe lo n se eemi ti awon omo eyan maa n mi sinu, won si ma n gba eemi ti awon eyan ma n mi si ta (eyi to je, .
english: Plants make oxygen which humans breathe, and they take in carbon-dioxide which humans exhale (that is, breathe out).

yoruba: Fotosintesisi ni ọ̀nà tí àwọn ewé ma n gbà gi ṣe ounje wọn láti orun. Wọ́n dẹ̀ ma n pèsè àbò.
english: Plants make their food from the sun by photosynthesis. They also provide shade.

yoruba: A kọ́ àwọn ilé wa pẹ̀lú ewé a sì ṣe àwọn aṣọ wá nínu ewé náà. Púpọ̀ nínú àwọn óúnjẹ́ tí à ń jẹ jẹ́ ewé. Láìsí ewé, àwọn ẹranko kò lè yè.
english: We make our houses from plants and make clothes from plants. Most foods that we eat are plants. Without plants, animals could not survive.

yoruba: Mosasaurus je apanirun ti o le ju nigba aye re, ko beru nkankan leyin awon mosasaurs miran.
english: Mosasaurus was the apex predator of its time, so it feared nothing, except other mosasaurs.

yoruba: Erigi re to gun kun fun ju 70 eyin to mu, pelu imin ni aja enu re, o tumo si wipe nkan kikan to ba baa pade o le lo lai farapa.
english: Its long jaws were studded with more than 70 razor-sharp teeth, along with an extra set in the roof of its mouth, meaning that there was no escape for anything that crossed its path.

yoruba: Kòdánilójú, sùgbọ̀n ó lè ti ní ahọ́n amúga. Lára oúnjẹ rẹ̀ ni ìjàpà, ẹja ńlá, àwọn mosasaurs àti pé ó lè jẹ́ àwọn ẹranko tó ń jẹ́ ènìyàn.
english: We don't know for sure, but it may have had a forked tongue. Its diet included turtles, large fish, other mosasaurs, and it may even have been a cannibal.

yoruba: Ó tún ma ń se àkọlù sí ohunkóhun tó bá wọnú omi; kódà bó jẹ́ dínósọ̀ bí i bí i T. rex kò sì lè kápá rẹ̀.
english: It also attacked anything that entered the water; even a giant dinosaur such as T. rex would be no match for it.

yoruba: Níwọ̀n ìgbà ti àwọn oúnjẹ wọn kò sàjòjì sí wa, àwọnn arà Romu náà ní àwọn àsè tó sàjòjì tàbí tí kò wọ́pọ̀, bi akọ ẹlẹ́dẹ̀, àkùkọ, ìgbín, àti ẹ̀dà eku tí a ń pè ní dormouse.
english: While most of their food would be familiar to us, Romans did have their share of strange or unusual feast items, including wild boar, peacock, snails, and a type of rodent called a dormouse

yoruba: Ìyàtọ́ míràn nipé nígbàtí àwọn aláìní àtawọn obìnrin bá ń jẹun tiwọn lórí ìjókòó, àwọn olówó ma ń nífẹ̀ẹ́ sí àsè àpapọ̀ níbití wọn ó ti rọ̀gbọ̀kún lẹ́gbẹ̀ẹ́ méjìjì bí wọ́n bá se ń jẹun.
english: Another difference was that while the poor people and the woman ate their food while sitting in chairs, the rich men liked to have banquets together where they would lounge on their sides while they ate their meals.

yoruba: Àwọn ounje tó wá sí ile Yuropi láti Amerika àbí Asia ní senturi tó gbẹ̀yìn, o lè wà lára oúnjẹ́ ayé àtijọ́ Romu ò lè.
english: Ancient Roman meals couldn't have included foods that came to Europe from America or from Asia in later centuries.

yoruba: Fún àpẹrẹ wọn ò ní àgbàdo, tomato, ànàmọ́, kòkó, kò dẹ̀ sí ará Roomu àtijọ́ kan kan tó tọ́ pẹ́pẹ́yẹ wò rí.
english: For instance, they didn't have corn, nor tomatoes, nor potatoes, nor cocoa, and no ancient Roman ever tasted a turkey.

yoruba: Awon ara Bailoni ko tempili gbendeke fun awon orisa kookan won ti won ri gege bi ibugbe orisa naa.
english: The Babylonians built each of their gods a primary temple that was considered the home of the god.

yoruba: Àwọn èèyàn yíò mún ẹbọ wá fún òrìṣà àwọn àlúfà yí ó sì gbìyànjú à ti bá àìní àwọn ọlọ́run pàdé látara ayẹyẹ àti ọdún.
english: People would bring sacrifices to the gods and the priests would try to attend to the needs of the gods through ceremonies and festivals.

yoruba: Tẹ́m̀pìlì kọ̀ọ̀kan ló ní ojúde àgbàlà tẹ́ḿpìlì àti inú ibi ìyàsímímọ́ tí àlùfa nìkan lè wọ̀.
english: Each temple had an open temple courtyard and then an inner sanctuary that only the priests could enter.

yoruba: Nígbà míràn àwọn ilé ìṣọ́ pyramid, tí à ń pè ní ziggurats, ní wọ́n kọ́ láti jẹ́ ara ilé àdúrà.
english: Sometimes special pyramid shaped towers, called ziggurats, were built to be a part of the temples.

yoruba: Oke tente ile naa je ibi ijosin pataki fun olorun.
english: The top of the tower was special sanctuary for the god.

yoruba: Nínú ojú ọjọ́ tó gbóná ti Middle East, ilé náà kò se pàtàkì.
english: In the warm climate of the Middle East, the house was not so important.

yoruba: Púpọ̀ nínu ayé àwọn ẹbí Ibru ṣẹlẹ̀ ní afẹ́fẹ́ ìta.
english: Most of the life of the Hebrew family happened in the open air.

yoruba: Àwọn obìrin ló n s’oúnjẹ ní ọgbà, Àwọn ṣọ́bù kàn jẹ́ igbá lásán tón ranjú wo títí. Òkúta ní wọ́n fi ń kọ́ ilé.
english: Women did the cooking in the yard; stores were just open counters looking into the street. Stone was used for building houses.

yoruba: Ko fi be si igbo to tobi ni ile Canaan, nitori naa igi won gan.
english: There were no large forests in the land of Canaan, so wood was extremely expensive.

yoruba: A tẹdo agbegbe Greenland gẹgẹ bi ilu kereje. Ninu iṣẹlẹ Norse, wọn so wipe wọn le Erik Alawo Pupa kuro ni Iceland fun esun ipaniyan, nigbati o n rin irin ajo siwaju si ọna iwo oorun, o ri agbegbe Greenland o si sọ lorukọ.
english: Greenland was settled sparsely. In the Norse sagas they say that Erik the Red was exiled from Iceland for murder, and when travelling further west, found Greenland and named it Greenland.

yoruba: Sùgbọ́n láì nání àwárí rẹ̀, ẹ̀yà Eskimo ti ń gbébẹ̀ nígbà náà.
english: But regardless of his discovery, Eskimo tribes were already living there at the time.

yoruba: Bí ó tilẹ̀jẹ́ wípé ìlú kọ̀kan jẹ́ ará 'Scandinavian', ìyàtọ̀ púpọ̀ wà láàrín àwọn èèyàn, ọba, àṣà,ìṣe àti ìtàn àwn ìlú Denmark, Sweden, Norway àti Iceland.
english: Though each country was 'Scandinavian', there were many differences between the people, kings, customs and history of Denmark, Sweden, Norway and Iceland.

yoruba: Bí o bá ti wo èré àgbéléwò National Treasure, wà á rò wípé àtọ́ka wúra wà ní kíkọ lẹ́yin ìkéde òmìnira ni.
english: If you have watched the movie National Treasure, you may think a treasure map was written on the back of the Declaration of Independence.

yoruba: Àmọ́ṣá, èyí kò tọ̀nà. lóòótọ́ ohùn kan wà tí wọ́n kọ sí ẹ̀yìn àkọọ́lẹ̀, kìíse ìtọ́nisọ́nà wúra.
english: However, that is not true. Although there is something written on the back of the document, it is not a treasure map.

yoruba: Oro ti ako si eyin Ikede Ominira ni “Ojulowo Ikede ti Ominira eyi ti a se ni 4th July 1776”. Oro naa han ni isale iwe naa, ni adorikodo.
english: Written on the back of the Declaration of Independence were the words "Original Declaration of Independence dated 4th July 1776". The text appears on the bottom of the document, upside down.

yoruba: Nígbàtí kò sí ẹni tí ó mọ pàtó ẹni tí ó kọ ọ́, ohun tí a mọ̀ ni pé ní ìbẹ̀rẹ̀ pẹ̀pẹ̀ ayé rẹ̀, ìwé àkọọ́lẹ̀ aláwọ ẹran tí ó tóbi (ó wọn tó 29¾ inṣi sí 24½ inṣì) ní wọ́n yí pamọ́.
english: While no one knows for certain who wrote it, it is known that early in its life, the large parchment document (it measures 29¾ inches by 24½ inches) was rolled up for storage.

yoruba: Torina o seese pe won fi akosile naa se lebeeli lasan.
english: So, it is likely that the notation was added simply as a label.

yoruba: Ìfibalè ọjọ́ nla náà àti àwọn ogun tó tèle e lọ fún apá Àríwá ìlú Faransé ní òmìnira, súgbọn apá Gúúsù ò tìí di òmìnira.
english: The D-Day landings and the following battles had freed the north of France, but the south still wasn't free.

yoruba: “Vichy” French ló ṣe adarí rẹ̀. Àwọn ará French niwọ́n tó fẹ́ àláfíà pẹ̀lú àwọn ará ìlú Germany ní 1940 tí wọ́n sì ṣiṣẹ́ pẹ̀lú àwọn tó wá dabẹ̀ rú tí wọ́n kò pè dípò kí wọ́n bá wọn jà lọ.
english: It was ruled by the "Vichy" French. These were French people who had made peace with the Germans in 1940 and worked with the invaders instead of fighting them.

yoruba: Ní ọjọ́ karùndínlógún oṣù kẹjọ ọdún 1940, Àwọn tó jẹ́ ọ̀rẹ́ wọnú ìlú southern France, wọ́n sí pé ìkọlù náà ní “Operation Dragon”.
english: On 15 August 1940, the Allies invaded southern France, the invasion was called "Operation Dragoon".

yoruba: Ní ọ̀sẹ̀ méjì péré ikọ̀ ológun ilẹ̀ Americans àti Free France ti dóòlà ẹkùn gúúsù ilẹ̀ France, wọ́n sì ń padà sí ilẹ̀ Germany.
english: In just two weeks the Americans and Free French forces had liberated southern France and were turning towards Germany.

yoruba: Ọ̀làjú jẹ́ àṣà tí a pín nípasẹ̀ ẹgbẹ́ tó tóbi tí wọ́n ṣiṣẹ́ tí wọ́n sì ń gbé papọ̀, ẹgbẹ́ kan.
english: A civilization is a singular culture shared by a significant large group of people who live and work co-operatively, a society.

yoruba: Ọ̀rọ̀ tí à ń pè ní ọ̀làjú wá látí inú èdè ìlú Latin, èyí tó túmọ̀ sí ọ̀làjú ilẹ̀ Latin, civitas túmọ̀ sí ìlú tàbí ìpínlẹp àti pé wọ́n tún fi í se àlàyé bí àwùjọ se tóbi tó.
english: The word civilization comes from the Latin civilis, meaning civil, related to the Latin civis, meaning citizen, and civitas, meaning city or city-state, and that also somehow defines the size of the society.

yoruba: Àwọn ìlú-ìpinle jẹ àwọn ìsáájú fún àwọn orílè-èdè. Àsà tí ọ̀làjù túnmò sí ìfìoyẹ lẹ́lẹ̀ kọjá ọ̀pọ̀lọpọ̀ ìran, nípasẹ̀ àsà asájú àti ìtànkálẹ̀ ìtẹ̀lẹ́ra.
english: City-states are the precursors of nations. A civilizational culture implies the passing on of knowledge across several generations, a lingering cultural footprint and fair dissemination.

yoruba: Àwọn àṣà kéèké ma ń pòórá lá ì fi ìtàn kankan sílẹ̀ bí ẹ̀rí, wọn kì í si ri í lò gẹ́gẹ́ bí ohun ìlàjú tó yẹ.
english: Minor cultures often vanish without leaving relevant historic evidence and fail to be recognized as proper civilizations.

yoruba: Lákòókò ogun àláyìípadà, àwọn ìpínlẹ̀ mẹ́tàlá sèdàsílẹ̀ ìjọba tí kò lágbára—pẹ̀lú níní ìgbìmọ̀ asòfin bí i ẹka kan lábẹ́ àkọọ́lẹ̀ gbogbogbo.
english: During the Revolutionary War, the thirteen states first formed a weak central government—with the Congress being its only component—under the Articles of Confederation.

yoruba: Àwọn aṣòfin kò ní agbára láti kan owó orí nípá àti pé kò sí ìgbìmọ̀ aláṣẹ tàbí ìdàjọ́, ó wà lọ́wọ́ àwọn aláṣẹ́ ìpínlẹ̀ tí wọ́n kì í sábàá fọwọ́ṣowọ́pọ̀ láti se àgbéró àwọn òfin gbogbo.
english: Congress lacked any power to impose taxes, and, because there was no national executive or judiciary, it relied on state authorities, who were often uncooperative, to enforce all its acts.

yoruba: Kò ní áṣẹ kankan láti mún òfin owó orí àti ìlànà ìdíyelé láàrin ìpínlẹ̀.
english: It also had no authority to override tax laws and tariffs between states.

yoruba: Àkọọ́lẹ̀ tìí bèèrè fún àjùmọgbà ìjẹho látọ̀dọ̀ àwọn ìpínlẹ̀ kí wọ́n tó lè sàtúnse sí i, àmọ́ àwọn ìpínlẹ̀ fọwọ́ yẹpẹrẹ mún ààrin gbungbun ìjọba débi wípé àwọn asojú kì í sí níjoòkó.
english: The Articles required unanimous consent from all the states before they could be amended and states took the central government so lightly that their representatives were often absent.

yoruba: Egbe agbaboolu ilu Itili pelu ti Jamani ni egbe agbaboolu keji to se aseyori ju laye, awon de lo gbe igba oroke ni Ere Idije FIFA Koopu Agbaye ni 2006.
english: Italy's national football, along with German national football team is the second most successful team in the world and were the FIFA World Cup champions in 2006.

yoruba: Àwọn eré ìdárayá tó gbajúmọ̀ ní bọ́ọ̀lù àfẹsẹ̀gbá, bọ́ọ̀lú aláwọ̀n, volley ball, water polo àti eré sísá pẹ̀lú mọ́tò F1.
english: Popular sports include football, basketball, volleyball, water-polo, fencing, rugby, cycling, ice hockey, roller hockey and F1 motor racing.

yoruba: Èré ìdárayá ìgbà òtútù ní wọ́n sábàá ma ń se ní ẹkùn àríwá pẹ̀lú àwọn ará ilẹ̀ Italy tó ma ń figagbága nínú ìdíje àgbáyé àti ètò Olympi.
english: Winter sports are most popular in the Northern regions, with Italians competing in international games and Olympic events.

yoruba: Ilẹ̀ Japan ní erékùsù tó tó ẹgbẹ̀rún méje (èyí tó tóbi jù ní Honshu), sọ ilẹ̀ Japan di erékùsù tó tóbi jù sìkeje ní àgbáyé!
english: Japans holds nearly 7,000 islands (the biggest being Honshu), making Japan the 7th largest island in the world!

yoruba: Torí àkókọpọ̀ irékusú tí Japaani ní a máa n sába pe ní “akipelago” nípasẹ̀ ìmọ̀ àyíká.
english: Due to the cluster/group of islands Japan has, Japan is often referred to, on a geographical stance, as an "archipelago"

yoruba: Ìbẹ̀rẹ̀ Taiwan ní 15th century níbi tí àwọn awakọ̀ ojú omi Europe gbà kọjà ṣàkọsílẹ̀ orúkọ island na gẹ́gẹ́ bí IIha Formosa, tàbí island tó rẹwà.
english: Taiwan beginning start way back in 15th century where European sailors passing by record the island’s name as Ilha Formosa, or beautiful island.

yoruba: Ní ọdún 1624, ilé-iṣẹ́ Dutch East ní ilẹ̀ India se àgbékalẹ̀ ibùdó ní gúúsù ìwọ̀ oòrùn Taiwan, èyí tó mún ìyàtọ̀ bá iṣẹ́ ọ̀gbìn àgbàdo tí àwọn ọmọ onílẹ̀ ń se, tí ó sì tún fààyè sílẹ̀ fún gbígba àwọn òsìṣẹ́ ilẹ̀ China láti síṣẹ́ lórí okò ìrẹsì àti ṣúgà.
english: In 1624,Dutch East India Company establishes a base in southwestern Taiwan, initiating a transformation in aboriginal grain production practices and employing Chinese laborers to work on its rice and sugar plantations.

yoruba: Ní ọdún 1683, àwọn ọmọ ogun ìran Qing (1644-1912) gba àkóso agbègbèTaiwan western àti northern coastal wọ́n sì sọ Taiwan dí ìlú ti Qing Empire ní 1885.
english: In 1683, Qing dynasty (1644-1912) forces take control of Taiwan’s western and northern coastal areas and declared Taiwan as a province of the Qing Empire in 1885.

yoruba: Lọ́dún 1895, lẹ́yìn ìparun ogun Sino àti Japan (1894-1895), ìjọba Qing buwọ́lu àjùmọgbà àláfíà Shimonoseki, nínú èyí tí ó ti gbé agbára kọja Taiwan fún Japan, èyí tó ń darí erékùsù títí dí odún 1945.
english: In 1895, after defeat in the First Sino-Japanese War (1894-1895), the Qing government signs the Treaty of Shimonoseki, by which it cedes sovereignty over Taiwan to Japan, which rules the island until 1945.

yoruba: Machu Picchu ní ẹ̀yà mẹ́ta, tó jẹ́ Intihuatana, Te Temple of the Sun, àti Room of the Three Windows.
english: Machu Picchu consist of three main structures, namely Intihuatana, the Temple of the Sun, and the Room of the Three Windows.

yoruba: Wọ́n ti se àtúnkọ́ ọ̀pọ̀lọpọ̀ àwọn ilé tó wà létí ilé ńlá, lójúnà àti fún àwọn onírìn afẹ́ ní òye bí wọ́n se rí gẹ́lẹ́.
english: Most of the buildings on the edges of the complex have been rebuilt in order to give tourists a better idea of how they originally appeared.

yoruba: Ní 1976, ìda ọgbọ̀n ti Machu Picchu tí ní ìdápadà ó sì tẹ̀síwájú títí di òní.
english: By 1976, thirty percent of Machu Picchu had been restored and restoration continues till today.

yoruba: Fún àpẹrẹ, ìlànà àwòrán tó pọ̀jù láyé ní 35mm, èyí tó jẹ́ ìwọ̀n fíìmùn tó pọ̀jù níbí fíìmù àtijọ́ se ń lọ.
english: For example, the most common still image photography format in the world is 35mm, which was the dominant film size at the close of the analog film era.

yoruba: Wan ṣì ma ń gbe jáde lónìí,ṣùgbọ́n ní pàtàkì abala ìpín rẹ ti jẹ́ ti ìgúnrégé sensor àwòrán kémẹ́rà onídígítà.
english: It is still produced today, but more importantly its aspect ratio has been inherited by digital camera image sensor formats.

yoruba: Ìwọ̀n ní ààrùndínlógójì mm jẹ, èyí tó dojúrú bákan, ìwọ̀n èèríndínlógójì mm, ní fífè ní mẹ̀rìnlẹ́lógún mm ní gíga.
english: The 35mm format is actually, somewhat confusingly, 36mm in width by 24mm in height.

yoruba: Abala ìpín fọ́màtì yìí (pínpín pẹ̀lú méjìlá láti lè ní odidi nọ́ḿbà onípínpín) ni a ma ní ó jẹ́ 3:2.
english: The aspect ratio of this format (dividing by twelve to obtain the simplest whole-number ratio) is therefore said to be 3:2.

yoruba: Ọ̀pọ̀lọpọ̀ àwọn ìgúnrégé (ìgúnrégé ebí APS, fún àpẹrẹ) dọ́gba sí tàbí súmọ ìpín náà.
english: Many common formats (APS family of formats, for example) are equal to or closely approximate this aspect ratio.

yoruba: Òfin ìkẹta tí wọ́n ma ń bẹnu àtẹ́ lù jùlọ ní ó jẹ́ àlàkalẹ̀ láti se àgbékalẹ̀ ìyàtọ̀ nígbà tí wọ́n ń sàrídájú ìlànà àwòrán.
english: The much-abused and often-ridiculed rule of thirds is a simple guideline creating dynamism while keeping a measure of order in an image.

yoruba: Ó fi lẹdẹ́ pé ipò tó múnádóko jùlọ fún èyí tó sẹ kókó ni ìkóríta àwọn ìlà tó pín àwòrán náà sí ìdá mẹ́ta ní ìnàró àti ní ìdùbúlè.
english: It states that the most effective place for the main subject is at the intersection of lines dividing the image into thirds vertically and horizontally (see example).

yoruba: Ni akoko yi ni itan abalaye Yuropi, Ijo Katoliki, to ti di olow ati alagbara, wa labe imojuto fini fini.
english: During this period of European history, the Catholic Church, which had become rich and powerful, came under scrutiny.

yoruba: Fún ẹgbẹgbẹ̀rùn ọdún sẹ́yìn ẹ̀sì krìstíẹ́nì tí so àwọn orílẹ̀ èdè Europe papọ̀ pẹ̀lú èdè àti àṣa wọn tó yàtọ̀.
english: For over a thousand years the Christian religion had bound European states together despite differences in language and customs. I

yoruba: Gbogbo ènìyàn bẹ̀rẹ̀ láti orí ọba lọ sí orí àwọn ará ìlú ni agbara gbogbogbò yíò sèpalára fún.
english: Its all-pervading power affected everyone from king to commoner.

yoruba: Ọ̀kan lára òpó ẹ̀sìn kìrìsìtíẹ́nì ni pé, ọrọ̀ gbọ́dọ̀ jẹ́ lílò láti fi mún ìyà àti ìṣẹ́ tán nílẹ̀ àti wípé àwọn owó ìjọ wà fún ìdí pàtàki náà.
english: One of the main Christian tenets is that wealth should be used to alleviate suffering and poverty and that the monetary funds of the church are there specifically for that reason.

yoruba: Àṣe nlá ìjọ ti wà ní Rómù ó lé ní ęgbẹ̀rún ọdún, ìkójọpọ̀ agbára àti owó yí tí jẹ́kí àwọn èyàn ma ṣèyemẹ́jì pé bóyá a gbọ́ràn sí òfin yí.
english: The central authority of the church had been in Rome for over a thousand years and this concentration of power and money led many to question whether this tenet was being met.

yoruba: Láìpẹ́ tí ìfúngunmọ́ni bẹ̀rẹ̀, Ilẹ̀ Britian bẹ̀rẹ̀ síní fi ikọ̀ ológun ojú omi dá ilẹ̀ Germany dúró.
english: Soon after the outbreak of hostilities, Britain initiated a naval blockade of Germany.

yoruba: Ọgbọ́n náà dára, yíyọ àwọn ǹkan èlò tí ológun àti àwọn ènìyàn, ṣùgbọ́n ìdènà yìí kò tọ̀nà fún òfin orílẹ̀èdè tí wọ́n fọwọ́sí tí àwọn tó wà ní òkè òkun fọwọ́sí fún ọ̀pọ̀lọpọ̀ ọdún sẹ́yìn.
english: The strategy proved effective, cutting off vital military and civilian supplies, although this blockade violated generally accepted international law codified by several international agreements of the past two centuries.

yoruba: Britain ma ń wa omi òkun tí àwọn ọka ojú omi ma ń gbà kọjá láti má jẹ́ kí èyíkèyí okọ̀ ojú omi wo gbogbo abala òkun náà, tó ma fa ewu fún àwọn ọkọ̀ ojú omi tí kò mọ ǹkankan.
english: Britain mined international waters to prevent any ships from entering entire sections of ocean, causing danger to even neutral ships.

yoruba: Torí pé èsì sí ìgbésẹ̀ náà ò pọ̀, Jamani retí irú èsì bẹ́ẹ̀ sí ìjàkadì ọkọ̀ abẹ́ omi t’aláìhámọ.
english: Since there was limited response to this tactic, Germany expected a similar response to its unrestricted submarine warfare.

yoruba: Ní ìgbà 1920, àwọn ìwà àwọn ènìyàn àti orílẹ̀ èdè tó borí ní alòdì sí ogn àti ìdáwà.
english: During the 1920s, the prevailing attitudes of most citizens and nations was that of pacifism and isolation.

yoruba: Lẹ́yìn wíwo ìbẹ̀rù àti oríṣìí aburú tí ogun àgbáyé àkọ̀kọ́ fa, gbogbo àgbáyé ti ń gbìyànjú láti yẹra fún irú ìṣẹlẹ̀ aburu bẹ́ẹ̀ ni ọjọ́ iwáj.
english: After seeing the horrors and atrocities of war during World War I, nations desired to avoid such a situation again in the future.

yoruba: Tesla kó lọ sí ilẹ̀ America ní ọdún 1884, láti lọ gba iṣẹ́ pẹ̀lú ilé-iṣẹ́ Edison tó wà ní New York Cit.
english: In 1884, Tesla moved to the United States of America to accept a job with the Edison Company in New York City.

yoruba: O de si orile ede US, pelu senti 4, to je owo to ni laye lorun, pelu iwe ewi, pelu iwe akosile oniduro lati odo Charles Batchelor (oga re ni ise ti o se gbeyin) si Thomas Edison.
english: He arrived in the US with 4 cents to his name, a book of poetry, and a letter of recommendation from Charles Batchelor (his manager in his previous job) to Thomas Edison.

yoruba: Ìlú China àdáyébá ní ọ̀nà ọ̀tọ̀ láti sàfihàn àwọn àkókò ìgbà; ọ̀kọ̀ọ̀kan ìlú China tàbí ìdílé tó wà ní ẹka agbára jẹ́ ìjọba tó dá yàtọ̀.
english: Ancient China had a unique way of showing different time periods; each stage of China or each family that was in power was a distinctive dynasty.

yoruba: Bákan náà, láàárin àkókò ilẹ̀ ọba kan, ọjọ́ orí tí kìí dúró llójú kan, tí ó pín ìgbèríko.Eyí tí a mọ̀ ju nínú irúfẹ́ àkokò yìí ni ìbẹ̀ẹ̀rẹ̀ ìjọba mẹ́ta tí ó wáyé fún ọgọ́ta ọdún láàárin ogún ọba Han àti Ji.
english: Also between each dynasty was an unstable age of divided provinces. The best-known of these periods was the Three Kingdoms epoch taking place for 60 years between the Han and the Jin Dynasty.

yoruba: Lákòókò ìgbà ogun líle tó wáyé láàrin àwọn ọlọ́lá tó ń jà fún ipò.
english: During these periods fierce warfare took place between many nobles fighting for the throne.

yoruba: Ìjọba ilẹ̀ mẹ́ta jẹ́ ọ̀kan lára àwọn àkókò ẹlẹ́jẹ̀ nínú itàn àdáyébá ilẹ̀ China, ẹgbẹ̀lẹ́gbẹ̀ àwọn èèyàn ló kú nínú ìjà láti gba ìjókòó ààfin ńlá Xi’an.
english: The Three Kingdoms was one of the bloodiest eras in Ancient China’s history thousands of people died fighting to sit in the highest seat in the grand palace at Xi’an.

yoruba: Ọ̀pọ̀ àwọn àbájádẹ tí òsèlú ti àwújọ wa, bíi lílo ètò métíríkì, ìsípòpadà làti ìsẹ ìjọba ipá sí ìjọba àwa ara wa, jíjé ọmọ orílè-èdè àti ìgbàgbọ pé orílè-èdè jé ti tàwọn ènìyàn rẹ láìsẹ ti àdánìkànjọpọ́n aládarí kan ṣoṣo.
english: There are a lot of social and political effects such as the use of metric system, a shift from absolutism to republicanism, nationalism and the belief the country belongs to the people not to one sole ruler.

yoruba: Síwáájú si lẹ́yìn ìsọ̀tẹ̀ náà àyè láti sisé si´ sílè fún gbogbo àwọn òlùbèwò okùnrin èyí tó fi àyè gba àwọn to ní ìtalára àti pẹgẹdẹ́ jùlọ láti sẹ àsèyor.
english: Also after the Revolution occupations were open to all male applicants allowing the most ambitious and successful to succeed.

yoruba: Bákan na ni fún àwọn ajagun nítorí pẹ kàkà kí wọn fi kíláàsi sẹ ìwọ̀n ipò ajagun, wọn fi agbára sẹ e.
english: Same goes for the military because instead of army rankings being based on class they were now based on cailaber.

yoruba: Ogun àyípadà ìjọba ní ilẹ̀ Faransé ní ó jẹ́ kóríyá fáwọn òsìṣẹ́ ilẹ̀ míràn tí wọ́n ń fúngun mọ́ láti bẹ̀rẹ̀ àyípadà ti wọn.
english: The French Revolution also inspired many other repressed working class people of other country's to began their own revolutions.

yoruba: Àwọn ohun tó kọjá ayé yìí jẹ Muhammad lógún. Ó má ń lọ síbi ihò kan tí ó di ohun tí wọ́n mọ̀ sí “iHira” lórí òkè “Noor” (ìmọ́lẹ̀) fún àsàrò.
english: Muhammad was deeply interested in matters beyond this mundane life. He used to frequent a cave that became known as “Hira‘” on the Mountain of “Noor” (light) for contemplation.

yoruba: ó dé ara rẹ̀ pamọ́, ó sì yè, èyí fún wa ní àwòrán ìfa ọkàn nípa ti ẹ̀mi Mohammad.
english: he cave itself, which survived the times, gives a very vivid image of Muhammad’s spiritual inclinations.

yoruba: Ìsinmi lórí ọ̀kan lára àwọn òkè àríwá Mecca, ihò àpáta náà ni a yàsọ́tọ̀ sí ìyókù lágbàáyé.
english: Resting on the top of one of the mountains north of Mecca, the cave is completely isolated from the rest of the world.

yoruba: Kódà kò rọrùn láti wárí rárá tí èyàn bá tiẹ̀ mọ̀ pé ó wà. Tóba ti wà ní òkúta àpámọ́, o ma dáwà pátá pátá.
english: In fact, it is not easy to find at all even if one knew it existed. Once inside the cave, it is a total isolation.

yoruba: Kòsí ǹkan tí a lè rí ju ojú ọ̀run tó rẹwà tó sì mọ́lẹ̀ lókè àti ọ̀pọ̀lọpọ̀ àwọn orí òkè tó yiká. Díẹ̀ lára àgbáyé yìí ní a lè rí tàbí gba láti inú ihò.
english: Nothing can be seen other than the clear, beautiful sky above and the many surrounding mountains. Very little of this world can be seen or heard from inside the cave.

yoruba: Pírámìdì ńlá tó wà ní Giza ni ọ̀kan péré nínú àrà méje tó sì ń fẹsẹ̀ múnlẹ̀ lónìí.
english: The Great Pyramid at Giza is the only one of the seven wonders that is still standing today.

yoruba: Awọn ara Íjíbì́tì ni wọ́n kọ́ ọ ni ṣẹ́ńtúri kẹta sáájú ìbí Jesu, ibi ìsìnkú pàtàkì yìí jẹ́ ọ̀kan lára ọ̀pọ̀lọpọ̀ ibi ìsìnkú tí wọ́n gbé kalẹ̀ láti yẹ́ òkú Pharaoh sí.
english: Built by the Egyptians in the third century BCE, the Great Pyramid is one of many large pyramid structures built to honor dead Pharaoh.

yoruba: Giza Plateau àbí “Giza Necropolis” ní òkè Egypt àwọn tí ó ti kú ní àkópọ̀ onírúurú pírámíìdì (èyí tí ó jẹ́ pé pírámìdi ńlá ló tóbi jù) onírúurú ibojì kéékèké, tẹ́m̀pìlì lọ́pọ̀lọpọ̀ àti sphinx ńlá.
english: The Giza Plateau, or "Giza Necropolis" in the Egyptian Valley of the Dead contains several pyramids (of which the great pyramid is the largest), several small tombs, several temples, and the great Sphinx.

yoruba: Wọ́n se àgbékalẹ̀ pírámìdì ńlá láti dá Pharaoh Khufu lọ́lá àtàwọn pírámìdì kékéèké, ibòjì àtàwọn ilé tẹ́ḿpìlì ni wọ́n kọ́ láti dá àwọn ìyàwó àti mọ̀lẹ́bí Khufu lọ́wọ́.
english: The great pyramid was created to honor the Pharaoh Khufu, and many of the smaller pyramids, tombs, and temples were built to honor Khufu's wives and family members.

yoruba: Àmì “up bow” jọ bí V nígbàtí àmì “down bow” dàbí staple ti kò ní ìdí.
english: The "up bow" mark looks like a V and the "down bow mark" like a staple or a square missing its bottom side.

yoruba: Òkè túmọ̀ sí wípé kí o bẹ̀rẹ̀ ní etí góńgó kí o sì taari ọrún, àti pé ìsàlẹ̀ túmọ̀ sí pé kí o bẹ̀rẹ̀ nísàlẹ̀ (níbití ọwọ́ rẹ ti di ọrún mún.
english: Up means you should start at the tip and push the bow, and down means you should start at the frog (which is where your hand is holding the bow) and pull the bow.

yoruba: Orun-oke ma n saba dun kelekele, orun-isale ma n dun ni ohun lile to lase.
english: An up-bow usually generates a softer sound, while a down-bow is stronger and more assertive.

yoruba: O ní àǹfànní láti fi gègé kọ àwọn àmì tìrẹ, sùgbọ́n rántí wípé ìlà àtẹ̀jáde tó wà níbẹ̀ wà fún iṣẹ́ orin, nítorínà wọ́n gbọ́dọ̀ jẹ́ bíbọ̀wọ̀ fún.
english: Feel free to pencil in your own marks, but remember the printed bowing marks are there for a musical reason, so they should usually be respected.

yoruba: Ọba búburú Louis XVI, Queen Marie Antoinette àwọn ọmọ wọn kékèké méjì ( Marie Therese, ọmọ ọdún mọ́kànlá àti Louis-Charles ọmọ ọdún mẹ́rin) àti àbúrò ọba obìnrin Madam Elizabeth, ní ọjọ́ kẹfà, oṣù kẹwa ọdún 1789, ni ọ̀gọọrọ̀ àwọn ìyá ọlọ́jà lé jáde pada sí Paris láti Versailles.
english: The terrified King Louis XVI, Queen Marie Antoinette their two young children (11 year old Marie Therese and four year old Louis-Charles) and the King's sister, Madam Elizabeth, on the 6th October 1789 were forced back to Paris from Versailles by a mob of market women.

yoruba: Nínu kẹ̀kẹ́ ẹṣin, wọ́n rìrìn àjò padà sí Paris àwọn ènìyàn sí yí wọn kà , wọ́n han, wọ́n sì ń pariwo ìdérùba Ọba àti Aya ọba.
english: In a carriage, they traveled back to Paris surrounded by a mob of people screaming and shouting threats against the King and Queen.

yoruba: Àwọn èèyàn tínú ń bí tí kan ọba àti olorì nípá láti má a ṣí fèrèsé ohun èlò tó ń gbé wọn lọ sílẹ̀.
english: The mob of people forced the King And Queen to have their carriage windows wide open.

yoruba: Nígbà tóyá, ọ̀kan lára àwọn èrò ju orí olórí ẹ̀ṣọ́ tí wọ́n sekúpa ni Versaille síwájú ayaba tẹ́rù ti ń bà.
english: At one point a member of the mob waved the head of a royal guard killed at Versailles in front of the terrified Queen.

yoruba: Ogun tí àwọn ikọ̀ ogun Amẹ́rikà jà fún àwọn are Filipini ní àwọn ara Filipi fúnra wọn san páta.
english: The war expenditures of U.S. imperialism in the conquest of the Philippines were paid for by the Filipino people themselves.

yoruba: A ro won lati san owo ori fun ijoba amunisin ti U.S. lati se akojopo awon koko ninu eto isuna ati ere lori idokoowo eyi ti a se loruko ijoba Philippine lati ipase awon ile ifowopamo ni Wall Street.
english: They were compelled to pay taxes to the U.S. colonial regime to defray a major part of the expenditures and the interest on bonds floated in the name of the Philippine government through the Wall Street banking houses.

yoruba: Beeni, awon ere pupo ti a rigba lati ilokulo awon ara Filipini fun ojo pipe maa je ere ijoba ijegaba ilu Amerika.
english: Of course, the superprofits derived from the protracted exploitation of the Filipino people would constitute the basic gains of U.S. imperialism.

yoruba: Láti lè jẹ́ kí Templers yé ọ o ní láti mọ ǹkan tó fa ìṣẹ́dá náà.
english: To understand the Templars one must understand the context that prompted the creation of the order.

yoruba: Akoko ti awon isele naa ti sele ni won maa n saba pe ni Akoko Aarin Giga, akoko ninu itan abalaye Yuropi ni senturi 11th, 12th ati 13th (AD 1000-1300).
english: The age where the events took place is commonly referred as the High Middle Ages the period of European history in the 11th, 12th, and 13th centuries (AD 1000–1300).

yoruba: Akoko Aarin Nla lo gbeyin Ibere Akoko Aarin, lo tun saaju Ipari Akoko Aarin, ti o pari ni bi 1500 nilana itan igbalode.
english: The High Middle Ages were preceded by the Early Middle Ages and followed by the Late Middle Ages, which by convention ends around 1500.

yoruba: Ìpinnu tí ìmọ̀-ẹ̀rọ jé asayan ọ̀rọ̀ gbogbogbò fún orísirísi àwọn ìlóyẹ fún ìmúsisé to´ gbòòro, kíkànípá ìmò-ẹ̀rọ tábì pàtàkì ìmò-ẹ̀rọ sí ìwòyẹ tàràtà wípé àwọn àmúyẹ ìwòyẹ tó nísẹ pèlú àwọn òfin ìmọ̀ sayenisi àti ìfarahàn wọn nípa ìmò-ẹ̀rọ gẹ́gẹ́ bí atọ́kùn kádàrá éníyán.
english: Technological determinism is a term that encompasses a wide range of ideas in practice, from technology-push or the technological imperative to a strict sense that human destiny is driven by an underlying logic associated with scientific laws and their manifestation in technology.

yoruba: Ọ̀pọ̀lọpọ̀ àwọn ìtunmọ̀ ìpinu ìmọ̀ ẹ̀rọ pín àbá méjì, pé ìdágbàsóké ìms ẹ̀rọ fún rarẹ̀ fàyègba ọ̀nà tó jù àṣà àti òṣèlù lọ, ìms ẹ̀rọ nà ní “ipa” lóri àwùjọ tí a jogúnbá, jú àwùjọ ìlóniníyè lọ.
english: Most interpretations of technological determinism share two general ideas: that the development of technology itself follows a path largely beyond cultural or political influence, and that technology in turn has "effects" on societies that are inherent, rather than socially conditioned.

yoruba: Fún àpęrę à lè sọ pé ọkọ ló ń sokùnfà àtúnse àwọn ojú ọ̀nà.
english: For example, one might say that the motor car necessarily leads to the development of roads.

yoruba: Síbẹ̀síbẹ̀, ojú pópó jákèjádò orílẹ̀-èdè fún àwọn awakọ ayọ́kẹ́lẹ́ péréte kò le di síse pẹ̀lú bí ètò ọrọ̀ ajẹ́ sẹ rí, nítorínà àwọn ọ̀nà titun fùn ìselọ́pọ́ tó ń jẹyọ láti dín owó ríra ọkọ̀ kù.
english: However, a nationwide road network is not economically viable for just a handful of cars, so new methods of production are developed to reduce the cost of car ownership.

yoruba: Bi opo eyan se ni moto ma n mu ki ijamba oko oju ona po si, iyen naa ma n muu ki aseda awon ona titun ni iwosan ara eyan to ti kolu ijamba.
english: Mass car ownership also leads to a higher incidence of accidents on the roads, which leads to the invention of new techniques in healthcare for repairing damaged bodies.

yoruba: Romantisimi ma n sába dá àṣà láti àwọn akosile awọn akọ̀wé bío Gọẹ́tì, Fíṣtì àti Ṣlegel.
english: Romanticism had a large element of cultural determinism, drawn from writers such as Goethe, Fichte, and Schlegel.

yoruba: Ní ìlànà tí àkójọpọ̀ ẹgbẹ́ oníse, ẹ̀kọ́ nípa ìpìlẹ̀ ayé tún oníkálukú dá, bí ọjọ́ se ń gorí ọjọ́, ìṣe àti àṣà tí ó lọ́ mọ́ àgbéǹde ìpìlẹ̀ ayé, pẹ̀lú bí èyí se wà nísọ̀kan pẹ̀lú ipò àwùjọ èyí tó dára ju àwọn òfi àfipá gbé kalẹ̀.
english: In the context of Romanticism, the geography molded individuals, and over time customs and culture related to that geography arose, and these, being in harmony with the place of the society, were better than arbitrarily imposed laws.

yoruba: Bí a ti ṣe mọ ìlú Paris gẹ́gẹ́ bí ìlú tó ma ń ṣàralóge ní àgbáyé, A mọ Constantinople gẹ́gẹ́ bí ìlú aṣaralógé tí feudal Europe.
english: In the manner that Paris is known as the fashion capital of the contemporary world, Constantinople was regarded as the fashion capital of feudal Europe.

yoruba: Òkìkí rẹ̀ kàń̀ gẹ́gẹ́ bí nǹkan ìjayé tí ó bẹ̀rẹ̀ ni nǹkan bí 400 A.D. tí ó sì gbòde tihtí di nǹkan bí 1100 A.D.
english: Its renown for being an epicenter of luxury began in about 400 A.D. and lasted up until about 1100 A.D.

yoruba: Ipò rẹ̀ dïnkù ní senturi 16th pápá jùlo nítorí pé àwọn Kruseda ti padà pẹ̀lú ẹ̀bùn bíi aṣọ síkì èròjà oúnjẹ́ tí wọ́n níwọ̀n tó pọ̀ ju nkan tó wà ní ọjà Bisantaini.
english: Its status declined during the twelfth century mainly due to the fact that Crusaders had returned bearing gifts such as silks and spices that were valued more than what Byzantine markets offered.

yoruba: Ní àsìkò yí ní gbígbé àkọlé tí olú ìlú oge láti Constantinople sí Paris di sísẹ.
english: It was at this time that the transfer of the title of Fashion Capital from Constantinople to Paris was made.

yoruba: Àrà ìkọ̀wé Gothic lé téńté ní àkókò séńtúrì kẹwàá sí ikọkànlá àti ikẹrìnlá.
english: Gothic style peaked in the period between the 10th - 11th centuries and the 14th century.

yoruba: Ní ìbẹ̀rẹ̀ àṣà Bisantini ni apá ìlà orùn ni ipá púpọ̀ lórí aṣọ.
english: At the beginning dress was heavily influenced by the Byzantine culture in the east.

yoruba: Àmọ́ṣá, látàrí ìlànà ìbáraẹnisọ̀rọ̀ tó falẹ̀, àwọn àrà lẹ́kùn ìwọ̀ oòrùn le wà lẹ́yìn pẹ̀lú bí i ọdún márùndínlọ́gbọ̀n sí ọgbọ̀n ọdún.
english: However, due to the slow communication channels, styles in the west could lag behind by 25 to 30 year.

yoruba: ní ìparí Middle ages western Europe bẹ̀rẹ̀ láti ṣàgbéga àrà wọn, ìkan lára ìdàgbàsókè tó tóbi jùlọ látàrí crusades àwọn ènìyàn bẹ̀rẹ̀ sí ní lo àwọn bọ́tìnnì láti de aṣọ.
english: towards the end of the Middle Ages western Europe began to develop their own style. one of the biggest developments of the time as a result of the crusades people began to use buttons to fasten clothing.

yoruba: Ètò ọ̀gbìn aládàáni jẹ́ ètò ọ́gbìn tí a má ń se láti pèsè oúnjẹ tí yíò fún àìní àgbẹ̀ àti àwọn ará ilé rẹ̀.
english: Subsistence agriculture is agriculture carried out for the production of enough food to meet just the needs of the agriculturalist and his/her family.

yoruba: Kò sí nǹkan tó lágbára nípa ètò ọ̀gbìn ohun jíjẹ, lọ́pọ̀ ìgbà ọ̀gáníìkì, ìlànà lílo irúgbìn nínú àkùrọ̀ kí a wá paápọ̀ mọ́ àwọn ìlànà tí kò ga ni lára púpọ̀, tí yóò sí mú èrè púpọ̀ w.
english: Subsistence agriculture is a simple, often organic, system using saved seed native to the ecoregion combined with crop rotation or other relatively simple techniques to maximize yield.

yoruba: Nínú ìtàn, àwọn àgbẹ̀ ma ń siṣẹ́ àgbẹ̀ alábọ́dé nìkan, èyí náà ni wọ́n sì ń lò láwọn orílẹ̀ tóti gbèrú kan.
english: Historically most farmers were engaged in subsistence agriculture and this is still the case in many developing nations.

yoruba: Àṣà kekeke ma n so àwọn tó ní èrò bákanaa papọ̀ to n ṣe wọ́́n bii pé gbendeke awujo ti kọ̀ wọ́́n sílẹ̀ ó dẹ̀ ma n jẹ́kí wọ́́n ní idaraenimo.
english: Subcultures bring together like-minded individuals who feel neglected by societal standards and allow them to develop a sense of identity.

yoruba: Àwọn ẹka àṣà ní ìyàtọ̀ nípasè ọjọ́ orí,èyà, kílaàsì, agbègbẹ̀ àti/tàbí jíjẹ́ akọ tàbí ab.
english: Subcultures can be distinctive because of the age, ethnicity, class, location, and/or gender of the members.

yoruba: Awon nkan to ma n mu ki asakekeke da yato ni ede, irisi, esin, oselu, ibalopo, agbegbe abi apapo gbogbo won.
english: The qualities that determine a subculture as distinct may be linguistic, aesthetic, religious, political, sexual, geographical, or a combination of factors.

yoruba: Awon ara asa-kekere maa saba fihan pe won je omo elegbe nipase ilo ara ti o dayato ati ti o ni ami, die ninu apeere re je ara aso wiwo, isesi ati agooti.
english: Members of a subculture often signal their membership through a distinctive and symbolic use of style, which includes fashions, mannerisms, and argot.

yoruba: Ìkan lára awọn ìlànà tí a n lò láti fi júwe bí ìdarapọ̀ ṣe ṣe pàtàkì ni kí a fi àpẹrẹ àwọn ọmọ tí ò kópa nínú ìdarapọ̀ mó èyàn nígbàtí wọ́n dàgbà nítorí àidásí, oríburúkú àti iṣilò.
english: One of the most common methods used to illustrate the importance of socialization is to draw upon the few unfortunate cases of children who were, through neglect, misfortune, or wilful abuse, not socialized by adults while they were growing up.

yoruba: Won ma n pe awon omo kan ni “odaju” tabi alaileko. Awon eyan (nigba pupo obi won ni) a ti de awon odaju omo; nigba miin won fi omo naa sile nitori pe obi o gba aarun agbari ati idagbasoke ti omo naa nii.
english: Such children are called "feral" or wild. Some feral children have been confined by people (usually their own parents); in some cases this child abandonment was due to the parents' rejection of a child's severe intellectual or physical impairment.

yoruba: Awo omo to buru le ti ni iriri ijiya omode tabi isele buburu ki won to ja won sile tabi sisalo.
english: Feral children may have experienced severe child abuse or trauma before being abandoned or running away.

yoruba: Wọ́n sọ pé ẹranko ni ó wo àwọn mìíràn dàgbà; àwọn kan gbé inú aginjù fúnra wọn.
english: Others are alleged to have been brought up by animals; some are said to have lived in the wild on their own.

yoruba: Nígbàtí ó bá jẹ́ ẹrànko tí kìí ṣe ènìyàn lo tọ́ ọ, ọmọ-Feral a máa hu irúfẹ́ àwọn ìwà kan (tí ó jẹ́ mọ ìrínisí)ó fẹ́rẹ̀ jẹ́ pé bí gbogbo ìwà tí àwọn ẹranko náà ń hu ló ń hù, bí í bíbèrù ènìyàn tàbí kí wọ́n má bìkítà ènìyà.
english: When completely brought up by non-human animals, the feral child exhibits behaviors (within physical limits) almost entirely like those of the particular care-animal, such as its fear of or indifference to humans.

yoruba: Niwọn bi eto ikẹkọ ti o dalẹ lori iṣẹ akanṣe ba mu ẹkọ rọrun ati ladun, atẹlẹsẹ mu u rọrun si.
english: While project based learning should make learning easier and more interesting, scaffolding goes a step beyond.

yoruba: Ètò ní ṣíṣe n tẹ́lẹ̀ kì í se ọ̀na kíkọ́ ẹ̀kọ́ ṣùgbọ́n ìrànlọ́wọ́ tó ń pèsè àtilẹ́yìn fún olúkúlukú tó ń ní ìrírí ẹ̀kọ́ titunn bí èyí tó ń lo ètò titun lórí kọ̀m̀pútà tàbí bẹ̀rẹ̀ iṣẹ́ àkànṣe tiitun.
english: Scaffolding is not a method of learning but rather an aid that provides support to individuals whom are undergoing a new learning experience such as using a new computer program or beginning a new project.

yoruba: Scaffolds lè jẹ́ àfojúrí ó sì lè jẹ́ gidi, a lè sọ pé olùkọ lè jẹ́ scaffolds ṣùgbọ́n bẹ́ẹ̀ ni ọkùrin kékeré kílípù bébà nínu Microsoft Office.
english: Scaffolds can be both virtual and real, in other words, a teacher is a form of scaffold but so is the little paperclip man in Microsoft Office.

yoruba: Ìlànà ìkọ́ni tó fún àwọn akẹ́ẹ̀kọ́ ní àǹfànní láti lóye kíkún tíwọ́n kó sínú ohun èlò ni láti fi bèèrè ìbéèrè àti ìsàlàyé àwọn ọ̀nà àátọ̀ tí ó lè jẹ́ ìpènijà fáwọn àkẹ́ẹ̀kọ́ láti dá ṣe.
english: Virtual Scaffolds are internalized in the software and are meant to question, prompt, and explain procedures that may have been to challenging for the student to handle alone.

yoruba: Wọ́n fi àwọn ọmọdé sí ilé ìtọ́jú àwọn ọmọdé fún ọ̀pọ̀lọpọ̀ ìdí, bẹ̀ẹ̀rẹ̀ láti ìpatì, sí ìfìyàjẹni àti ìlọ́nilọ́wọ́gbà.
english: Children are placed in Foster Care for a wide variety of reasons that range from neglect, to abuse, and even to extortion.

yoruba: Kò yẹ kí ọmọ kankan dàgbà sí agbẹ̀gbẹ̀ tí kò ní rí ìtọ́jú, ìkẹ́, àti ẹ̀kọ gba sùgbọn o má n sẹlẹ̀.
english: No child should ever have to grow up in an environment that is not nurturing, caring, and educational, but they do.

yoruba: A wòye Ilànà Ibùdó Itọ́jú là ń wòye pé ó jẹ́ ibi ààbò fáwọn ọmọ wọ̀nyìí.
english: We perceive the Foster Care System to be a safety zone for these children.

yoruba: Ó yẹ kí ìlànà ètò ìgbanitọ́ pèsè ilé tó ní ààbò, àwọn olùtọ́jú tí ó nífẹ̀ẹ́, ètò ẹ̀kọ́ tó dúró re àti ètò ìtọ́jú tó se é gbáralé.
english: Our foster care system is supposed to provide safe homes, loving caregivers, stable education, and reliable health care.

yoruba: O ye ki mojuto omolomo pese gbogbo ohun elo pataki ti o to ni ile ti won yo won tele.
english: Foster care is supposed to provide all the necessities that were lacking in the home they were previously taken from.

yoruba: Ìtàkùn ayélujára ṣe àgbájọ ìbáraẹ́nisọ̀rọ̀ gbogbogbò àti ọlọ́danni.
english: The Internet combines elements of both mass and interpersonal communication.

yoruba: Ìṣesí arà ọ̀tọ̀ tí ayélujára ni ó fa ọ̀nà àrà ọ̀tọ̀,ní ti ìlò rẹ̀ àti ọ̀nà ìtẹ́nilọ́rùn.
english: The distinct characteristics of the Internet lead to additional dimensions in terms of the uses and gratifications approach.

yoruba: Fún àpẹrẹ, “Kíkọ́ ẹ́kọ́” àti “jíjáde” ní a mọ̀ sí ìwúrí tó ṣe pàtàki fún ìlò íńtánẹ́ẹ̀tì (James et al., 1995.
english: For example, “learning” and “socialization” are suggested as important motivations for Internet use (James et al., 1995).

yoruba: “Ìlọ́wọ́sí ara ẹni “ àti “ìbásepọ̀ tó tèsíwajú” wà lára ìdanimọ̀ fún àwọn abala ìwúrí titun nípasẹ̀ Eighmey ati McCord (1998) nígbà tí wón ṣe ìwádìí ìhà ohun olumulo ojú opòpó wẹ́ẹ̀bù.
english: “Personal involvement” and “continuing relationships” were also identified as new motivation aspects by Eighmey and McCord (1998) when they investigated audience reactions to websites.

yoruba: Ìlò gbìgba fídíò sílẹ̀ tí gbé dé àwọn àwárí tó ṣe pàtàkì nínu ìsọ̀tumọ̀ micros expression, yíyí ojú, èyí tí kò pẹ́.
english: The use of video recording has led to important discoveries in the interpretation of micro-expressions, facial movements which last a few milliseconds.

yoruba: Ní pàtó a lè se àwárí̀ bóyá ènìyàn ń parọ́ nípa títú àmì ara tó keré jùlọ.
english: In particular, it is claimed that one can detect whether a person is lying by interpreting micro-expressions correctly.

yoruba: Ninu iew re Oro Aare, Olifa Saksi toka si bi awon eya ti o le gbo oro nitori ijamba opolo le sagbeyewo ododo mo dara dara. .
english: Oliver Sacks, in his paper The President's Speech, indicated how people who are unable to understand speech because of brain damage are nevertheless able to assess sincerity accurately.

yoruba: Ó tilẹ̀ tún fi kún un pé òye láti túmọ̀ ìwà ọmọ ènìyàn ni àwọn ẹranko bí ajá ilé náà le ní.
english: He even suggests that such abilities in interpreting human behavior may be shared by animals such as domestic dogs.

yoruba: Iwadi ní senturi 20th ti fi hàn wípé a lẹ̀ dá iyato jenetiki sí odò mẹ́jì: eyi to pamọ́ àti eyi tó han.
english: Twentieth century research has shown that there are two pools of genetic variation: hidden and expressed.

yoruba: Àṣìṣe tí o wáyé ṣe àfikún àti ìyípadà sí ìmọ̀ orírùn, nígbàtí ìyàsọ́tọ̀ sì yọ ọ́ kúrò nínú agbami ìyípadà.
english: Mutation adds new genetic variation, and selection removes it from the pool of expressed variation.

yoruba: Ìpínyà àti àtúnlò ìyàtò síwájú àti sẹ́yìn láàrin igun méjèèjì pẹ̀lú ìran kọ̀ọ̀kan.
english: Segregation and recombination shuffle variation back and forth between the two pools with each generation.

yoruba: Ní gbangba sàfánà, ó ṣòro fún pírámètì tí ęya ara oúnjẹ́ jíję rẹ̀ jọ ti èyàn, láti rí ásìdì amino tí ara rẹ̀ ní lò láti ara ewé.
english: Out on the savanna, it is hard for a primate with a digestive system like that of humans to satisfy its amino-acid requirements from available plant resources.

yoruba: Sibe si, ti o ba ko lati se, o leyin: idiwo idagbasoke, ailounje, ati iku bopeboya.
english: Moreover, failure to do so has serious consequences: growth depression, malnutrition, and ultimately death.

yoruba: Ohun inu ẹwẹ́ tó máà wà nílè jú Iọ je àwọn oúnje ìdàgbàsókè tinú ẹwẹ́ àti ti ìrísí èwà, sùgbọn kọ rọgbọ fún àwọn prímẹ́ẹ̀tì bìí tàwa láti gba sára àfi tí a ba se e.
english: The most readily accessible plant resources would have been the proteins accessible in leaves and legumes, but these are hard for primates like us to digest unless they are cooked.

yoruba: Níyàtọ̀ ó rọgbọ láti gba ẹran (èrà, tambolo ati ẹ̀yin sára, wọ́n tún ma n pèsè ounje idagbasoke púpọ̀ tó ní àwọn asidi amino pàtàkì.
english: In contrast, animal foods (ants, termites, eggs) not only are easily digestible, but they provide high-quantity proteins that contain all the essential amino acids.

yoruba: Láì fi nkankan sílẹ̀, kò yẹ kó jẹ́ ìyàlénu tí àwọn babanla wa wá èsì fún “ìṣòro ounje ìdàgbàsókè” lọ́nà tó fẹ́ jọ bíi ti àwọn ọ̀bọ sàfánà ṣe n ṣe tiwọn.
english: All things considered, we should not be surprised if our own ancestors solved their "protein problem" in somewhat the same way that chimps on the savanna do today.

yoruba: Dídá orun mọ́yàn lójú ní ètò títají nígbàtí o bá ń sun orun tàbí nígbàtí o bá ń tòǹgbé tóbáyá (ìṣẹ́jú mẹwàá sí ọgọ́ta.
english: Sleep interruption is the process of purposefully awakening during your normal sleep period and falling asleep a short time later (10–60 minutes).

yoruba: Eleyi le seese ni bonkele nipa lilo aago itaniji ti ko pariwo lati muni ni ooye ohun ti o n sele laiji patapata.
english: This can be easily done by using a relatively quiet alarm clock to bring you to consciousness without fully waking you.

yoruba: Tí o bá rí ara rẹ pé ò ń yí ọwọ́ ago padà ní ojú àlá rẹ, o le è gbé sí òdi kejì yàrá ní ibi tí ọwọ́ rẹ̀ kò tí ni to o láti pa á.
english: If you find yourself resetting the clock in your sleep, it can be placed on the other side of the room, forcing you to get out of bed to turn it off.

yoruba: Àwọn àṣàyàn mín tó dá lóri bayoritimi ma n nílo mimu nkan olomi (pápá jùlọ omi àbi tí tí a n pè ní diuretiki) ki o tó sùn, o ma n mú kí èyàn maa jítọ̀ dandandan.
english: Other biorhythm-based options involve drinking lots of fluid (particularly water or tea, a known diuretic) prior to sleep, forcing one to get up to urinate.

yoruba: Iye àláfíà àtinúwá tí ẹnìkan ní jẹ́ ìdàkejì si iye ẹ̀dọ̀fún tó wà nínu rẹ.
english: The amount of inner peace a person possesses correlates oppositely to the amount of tension in one’s body and spirit.

yoruba: Bí ẹ̀dọ̀fun ṣe lọlẹ̀ tó, ni bí rere ayé ṣe ṣe ara rẹ̀. Gbogbo ènìyàn ní agbára láti ní aláfíà àti ìtẹ́lọ́rùn.
english: The lower the tension, the more positive the life force present. Every person has the potential to find absolute peace and contentment.

yoruba: Gbogbo eyan lo le de ipo imo ijinle, nkan soso to n dinna aleba yi ni irora wa ati ilodi si w.
english: Everyone can achieve enlightenment. The only thing standing in the way of this goal is our own tension and negativity.

yoruba: Tibetan Buddhism dá lórí kíkọ́ Buddha, ṣùgbọ́n mahayana fàágùn nípasẹ̀ àwọn oríṣi ọ̀na láti Indian Yoga.
english: The Tibetan Buddhism is based on the teachings of Buddha, but were extended by the mahayana path of love and by a lot of techniques from Indian Yoga.

yoruba: Ní ti àlàkalẹ̀, Buddhism ti ilẹ̀ Tibetan rọrùn. Ó ní àṣàrò Kundalini Yoga,àtipa àgbélárugẹ ìfẹ́.
english: In principle the Tibetan Buddhism is very simple. It consists of Kundalini Yoga, meditation and the path of all-embracing love.

yoruba: Pẹ̀lú Kundalini Yoga àgbàrá yìí (àgbàrá ìmòye) máa ń di jìjì dìde nípasẹ̀ àwọn ìdúró Yoga, àwọn ẹré ìdárayá mímí, àwọn èrò àtúnwí àti ìwọran.
english: With Kundalini Yoga the Kundalini energy (enlightenment energy) is awakened through yoga postures, breathing exercises, mantras and visualizations.

yoruba: Ibùdó àṣàrò Tibetan ni Òòṣà Yoga. Látara lílo àfihàn àwọn onírúurú òòṣà, wọ́n ń sàtúnfọ̀ agbára, wọ́n pe àwọn chakras síta, ìtají sì wáyé.
english: The center of Tibetan meditation is the Deity Yoga. Through the visualization of various deities the energy channels are cleaned, the chakras are activated and the enlightenment consciousness is created.

yoruba: Jámánì jẹ ọ̀tá gbogbo ní àsìkò ogun àgbáyé kejì, èyí tó yọrísí ìfọwọ́sowọ́pọ̀ láàrin ilẹ̀ USSR àti USA. Pẹ̀lú òpin sí ogun náà, ifigagbaga ètò, ìlànà àti àṣà ló mú àwọn orílẹ̀-èdè náà túká .
english: Germany was a common enemy in World War 2, leading to cooperation between the USSR and USA. With the end of the war the clashes of system, process and culture led to the countries falling out.

yoruba: Pẹ̀lú ọdún méjì tí ogun náà fi parí, àwọn tó jẹ́ ọ̀rẹ́ tẹ́lẹ̀ ti wá di ọ̀tà, Ogun abẹ́lé náà wá bẹ̀rẹ̀.
english: With two years of the end of the war, the former allies were now enemies and the Cold War began.

yoruba: Ó yẹ kí ó pẹ́ tó ogójì ọdún tó ń bọ̀ àti pé a ó ja ìjà òtítọ́ nípasẹ̀ asojú àwọn ọmọ ogun ní ojú ogun láti ilẹ̀ adúláwọ̀ lọ sí Asia, ní Afghanistan, Cuba àtàwọn ibòmíràn.
english: It was to last for the next 40 years and would be fought for real, by proxy armies, on battlefields from Africa to Asia, in Afghanistan, Cuba and many other places.

yoruba: Bí yíó ba fi di ọjọ́ kẹtàdínlógún oṣù kẹsàn án, 1993, ikọ̀ alábòbò ilẹ̀ Poland àti ìrètí tó wà ni láti sàtúnse ìpàgọ́ àti àtúngbékalẹ̀ pẹ̀lú ilẹ̀ Ròòmù.
english: By September 17, 1939, the Polish defense was already broken, and the only hope was to retreat and reorganise along the Romanian bridgehead.

yoruba: Amóò, àwọn ètò wọ̀nyí kúròláṣà mọ́jú nígbàtí bíi ajagun 800,000 láti Egbe Ajagun Pupa tì Ìgbìmọ̀ Sofieti wọlé, wọ́n díbọ́n Belarusi àti Yukireni léhin tí wọ́n ṣẹ́gun apá ila òórùn Polandi, ní ìlòdì sí Àdéhùn Àláfíà Riga, Àdéhùn Má-Jàkadì Sofieti-Polandi, pẹ̀lú gbogbo àwọn Àdéhùn ìlúsílù, àti t’oníìlú méjì ati t’oníìlú púpọ̀ si.
english: However, these plans were rendered obsolete nearly overnight, when over 800,000 soldiers from the Soviet's Union Red Army entered and created the Belarussian and Ukrainian fronts after invading the eastern regions of Poland in violation of the Riga Peace Treaty, the Soviet-Polish Non-Aggression Pact, and other international treaties, both bilateral and multilateral.

yoruba: Lílo ọkọ̀ ojú omi láti kó ọjà ni ọ̀nà tí ò sì dára jùlọ láti ko ènìyàn tàbí ẹrù tí ó pọ̀ kọjá ní orí òku.
english: Using ships to transport goods is by far the most efficient way to move large amounts of people and goods across oceans.

yoruba: Isṣ´ àwọn ológun ojú omi látilẹ̀ wá ní láti sàrìdájú wípé orílẹ̀èdè rẹ̀ ní agbára láti kó àwọn ènìyàn àti ẹrù, nígbàkan náà má a tọwọ́ bọ agbára àwọn ọ̀tá láti kó àwọn ènìyàn àti ẹeù wọn.
english: The job of navies has traditionally been to ensure that your country maintains the ability to move your people and goods, while at the same time, interfering with your enemy's ability to move his people and goods.

yoruba: Ìkan lára àpẹrẹ́ má le gbàgbé ti ìkéde North Atlantic ti WWII. Àwọn Amẹ́ríkà ń gbìyànjú láti gbé àwọn ọkùnrin àti àwọn ǹkan èlò kọjá Atlantic Ocean láti ran Britain lọ́wọ́.
english: One of the most noteworthy recent examples of this was the North Atlantic campaign of WWII. The Americans were trying to move men and materials across the Atlantic Ocean to help Britain.

yoruba: Ní ìgbà kanáà, ológun German, tó ń lo U-boats, ńgbìyànjú lati dá súnkẹrẹ fà kẹrẹ ọkọ́ dúró.
english: At the same time, the German navy, using mainly U-boats, was trying to stop this traffic.

yoruba: Tí Allies bá kùnà, Ìlú Germany lè borí Britain bí ó ṣe borí àwọn tókù ní Europe.
english: Had the Allies failed, Germany probably would have been able to conquer Britain as it had the rest of Europe.

yoruba: Àwọn ara Ìrano lo kọ́kọ́ bẹ̀rẹ̀ si ni sin ewure ní bíi ọdún 15,0000 sẹ́yìn ní Oke Sagrosi.
english: Goats seem to have been first domesticated roughly 10,000 years ago in the Zagros Mountains of Iran.

yoruba: Awon asa ati eya atijo bere si ni pa won mo lati le ri miliki, irun eran ati awo ara lo ni irorun.
english: Ancient cultures and tribes began to keep them for easy access to milk, hair, meat, and skins.

yoruba: Wọ́n má ń sin àwọn ẹran ọ̀sìn lọ́pọ̀ lọ sí orí òkè tàbí àwọn ibi ìkó ẹran jẹ̀ míràn, táwọn olùsìn tó sábàá ma ń jẹ́ ọmọdé tàbí àgùnbánirọ̀ sì má ń tọ́jú wọn lọ́pọ̀ ìgbà, èyí ló fara pẹ́ olùsọ́àgùtàn. Ìlànà èyí tí wọ́n sì ń lò dí òní.
english: Domestic goats were generally kept in herds that wandered on hills or other grazing areas, often tended by goatherds who were frequently children or adolescents, similar to the more widely known shepherd. These methods of herding are still used today.

yoruba: Wọ́n tí kọ́ Wágọ́nwè ní Ìlu Gẹ̀ẹ́sì láti sẹ́ntúrí 16th.
english: Wagonways were built in England as early as the 16th Century.

yoruba: Lóòótọ́ àwọn ohun tí wọ́n fi ń wọ́ kẹ̀kẹ́ akẹ́rù ní àwọn pákó pẹlẹbẹ pẹlẹbẹ, wọ́n má ń lo àwọn ẹṣin tó ń wọ́wọn láti lè sáré dáadáa, le fa ẹrù tó pọ̀ láwọn ọ̀nọ̀ tí kò tọ́ dáadáa.
english: Although wagonways merely consisted of parallel planks of wood, they allowed horses pulling them to achieve greater speeds and pull larger loads than on the slightly more rough roads of the day.

yoruba: Wọ́n tètè sàfihàn àwọn ìgbọ̀nwọ́ ojú ọ̀nà irin láti sàrídájú pé àwọn ipa wà déédé. Ó sàfihàn wípé àwọn ipa yìí yóó múná dóko bíwọ́n bá ní ọ̀pá irin lókè.
english: Crossties were introduced fairly early to hold the tracks in place. Gradually, however, it was realised that tracks would be more efficient if they had a stip of iron on the top.

yoruba: Eléyìí di ìse, sùgbọ́n irin mún kí àwọn pákó ẹsẹ̀ ọkọ̀ jẹ.
english: This became common practice, but the iron caused more wear on the wooden wheels of the wagons.

yoruba: Àmọ́ ṣá wọ́n fi wíìli onírin dípo wíìlì onípákó. Ní ọdún 1767 wọ́n gbe réèli onírín láti òkè délẹ̀ àkọ́kó irú rẹ̣̀ jáde.
english: Eventually, wooden wheels were replaced by iron wheels. In 1767, the first full-iron rails were introduced.

yoruba: Ọnà ìrìnà àkọ́kọ́ ni fífi ẹsẹ̀ rìn, ọmọ ẹ̀nìyàn bẹ̀rẹ̀ sí ní nàró rín ní bí mílíọ́nù mẹjì ọdún ṣẹ́yìn pẹ̀lú ìgbẹ́ìnde Homo Erectus (tí ó túnmọ̀ sí ọkùnrin tó ń nàró rìn).
english: The first known transportation was walking, humans began walking upright two million years ago with the emergence of Homo Erectus (meaning upright man).

yoruba: Àwọn bàbá bàbá wọn Ostralopitekusi ò dúró rìn.
english: Their predecessors, the Australopithecus did not walk upright as habitually.

yoruba: Ìmọ̀já àwọn aláfẹsẹ̀tẹ̀ onípele méjì ni wọ́n rí nínú àwọn ẹ̀rí àbáláyé Australopithecus ní bíi mílíọ̀nù 4.2 sí 3.9 ọdún sẹ́hìn, ẹ̀wẹ̀ ó ṣeéṣe kí Sahelanthropus ti rìn pẹ̀lú ẹsẹ̀ méjì ní ọdún mílíọ̀nù méje sẹ́hìn.
english: Bipedal specializations are found in Australopithecus fossils from 4.2-3.9 million years ago, although Sahelanthropus may have walked on two legs as early as seven million years ago.

yoruba: A lè bẹ̀rẹ̀ sih ní gbeh ìgbé ayé to faramọ́ra, bẹ́ẹ̀ sì ni a tún lè jẹ́ ajàjàgbara kí a lè mú àdínkù bá ìyà tí o le.
english: We can start living more friendly to the environment, we can join to the environmental movement, and we can even be activists in order to reduce the future suffering in some degree.

yoruba: Èyí dàbi wíwo àwọn àmà àìsàn ni ọ̀pọ̀ ìgbà. Sùgbón, tí a kọ ba fẹ ojútuu ránpẹ, a nílò làti wá ìdí àwọn ìṣòro náà, ki´ a si yanjú wọn.
english: This is just like symptomatic treatment in many cases. However, if we do not only want a temporary solution, then we should find the root of the problems, and we should deactivate them.

yoruba: Ó hàn kedere wípé ayé ti yípadà gidigidi pèlú ìlọsíwájú ìmọ̀-èrọ àti sáyẹ̀nsì láti ọwó ẹ̀dá ènìyàn, àti wípé ìṣòro ti di púpọ̀ nípasè pípọ̀ làpọ̀jù àti ìgbé ayé àsẹjù.
english: It is obvious enough that the world has changed much because of humankind's scientific and technological advancements, and problems have become greater because of overpopulation and mankind's extravagant lifestyle.

yoruba: Lẹ́yìn tí wọ́n bá yálò ní ọjọ́ Agemo 4 wọn fi atọ́kùnwò rẹ̀ ti Àrẹ Kongresi Johanu Hankoki àti akọ̀we Ṣalsi Tomsini fọwọ́sí ránṣẹ́ sí ilé ìtẹ̀jáde Johanu Dunlapi tí ò jìnà.
english: After its adoption by Congress on July 4, a handwritten draft signed by the President of Congress John Hancock and the Secretary Charles Thomson was then sent a few blocks away to the printing shop of John Dunlap.

yoruba: Bíi ẹ̀dà̀ àádọ́jọ sí igba ni wọ́n se mọ́jú tí a ńpè ní “Dunlap broadsides”.
english: Through the night between 150 and 200 copies were made, now known as "Dunlap broadsides".

yoruba: John Niksoni lo ka iwe naa nigba akoko ti won maa ka ni gbanga ni ogba Hoolu Ominira ni Agemo 8.
english: The first public reading of the document was by John Nixon in the yard of Independence Hall on July 8.

yoruba: Wọ́n fi ìkan ránṣẹ́ sí George Washington ní ọjọ́ lẹfà oṣù kẹje, tó kàá fún àwọn ikọ̀ rẹ ní New York ní ọja kẹssàn oṣù kẹje. Ẹ̀dà kan dé London ní ọja kẹwàá oṣù kẹjọ.
english: One was sent to George Washington on July 6, who had it read to his troops in New York on July 9. A copy reached London on August 10.

yoruba: Ìwọ̀n mẹ́ẹ̀dógbọ̀n ìwé fẹ̀rẹ̀gẹ̀dẹ̀ ti Dunlap èyí tí a mọ̀ wípé ó jẹ́ ẹ̀dà àkọọ́lẹ̀ tó dàgbà jù tó sì wà. Àpilẹkọ̀ ẹ̀dà náà gan-an kò sí mọ́.
english: The 25 Dunlap broadsides still known to exist are the oldest surviving copies of the document. The original handwritten copy has not survived.

yoruba: Púpọ̀ nínú àwọn paleontologist lónìí gbàgba pè ẹgbẹ́ dinosaur kan yè ó sì wá ní ayé di òní. A pè wọ́n ní ẹyẹ.
english: Many paleontologists today believe that one group of dinosaurs survived and is alive today. We call them birds.

yoruba: Opo eyan lo kin ri won gege bi dainoso tori pe won ni ye won de le fo.
english: Many people don't think about them as dinosaurs because they have feathers and can fly.

yoruba: Sùgbọ́n ọ̀pọ̀lọpọ̀ nǹkan lówà nípa ẹyẹ tí ó sì dàbí ti dinosaur.
english: But there are a lot of things about birds that still look like a dinosaur.

yoruba: Wọ́n ní ẹsẹ̀ tó ní pele àti èékán, wọ́n ń yé ẹyin àti pé wọ́n ń rìn pẹ̀lú ẹsẹ̀ ẹ̀yìn wọn méjì bíi ti T-REX.
english: They have feet with scales and claws, they lay eggs, and they walk on their two back legs like a T-Rex.

yoruba: Gbogbo àwọn ẹ̀rọ ayára bí àṣá tí wọ́n ń lò lónìí ló dá lórí ìlọ́pọ̀ àwọn ọ̀rọ̀ tí wọ́n ti kóòdù nílànà òǹkà .
english: Virtually all computers in use today are based on the manipulation of information which is coded in the form of binary numbers.

yoruba: Nọ́ḿbà bínárì lè ní ọ̀kan nínú iye méjì ìyẹn oódo àti oókan, àwọn iye wọ̀nyìí ni wọ́n ń pè ní binary digits - tàbí bits, láti le fi se jágbajàgba ẹ̀rọ ayárabíàṣá.
english: A binary number can have only one of two values, i.e. 0 or 1, and these numbers are referred to as binary digits - or bits, to use computer jargon.

yoruba: Aarun italoro inu le ma farahan lesekese. Awon ami aisan bii eebi ma saba waye torina won o le se iwadii aarun naa lesekese.
english: Internal poisoning may not be immediately apparent. Symptoms, such as vomiting are sufficiently general that an immediate diagnosis cannot be made.

yoruba: Ohun tí ó dára jù láti sàfihàn májèle lábẹ́lẹ́ ni bí ike òògùn olóró tàbí ohun inú ilé tí ó le sekúpani bá wà.
english: The best indication of internal poisoning may be the presence of an open container of medication or toxic household chemicals.

yoruba: Se àyẹ̀wò lébẹ̀lì náà fún ìtọ́nisọ́nà ìrànlọ́wọ́ àkọ́kọ́ fún májèlé ọ̀hún ní pàtó.
english: Check the label for specific first aid instructions for that specific poison.

yoruba: ọrọ̀ yìí “bug” ní àwọn onimọ kòkòrò máa ń lò, láti pe èyíkéyìí àkójọpọ̀ àwọn kòkòrò.
english: The term bug is used by entomologists in a formal sense for this group of insects.

yoruba: Ọ̀rọ̀ yìí ni wọ́n mún láti ìdámọ́ àtijọ́ pẹ̀lú ìdun, èyí tó jẹ́ kòkòrò tó tètè ma ń di èyí tó ń lẹ̀ mọ́ni lára.
english: This term derives from ancient familiarity with Bed-bugs, which are insects highly adapted to parasitize humans.

yoruba: Àwọn oríṣi ìdun méjèèjì jẹ́ ẹ̀ranko tí kò lè kúrò ní ibi ìbí rẹ̀, tí ibi tí wọ́n pàgọ́ sí ti mọ́ wọn lár.
english: Both Assassin-bugs and Bed-bugs are nidicolous, adapted to living in nest or housing of their host.

yoruba: Káàkiri orílẹ̀èdè America, ìṣẹ̀lẹ̀ àìsàn ọpọlọ àti ògóró ẹ̀yìn (MS) tí ó tó irinwó ẹgbẹ̀rún, èyí tí ó mún un di àìsàn tó nííse pẹ̀lú ọpọlọ láàrin àwọn ọ̀dọ́ àti àwọn tí ó wà ní agbedeméjì ọjọ́ orí tí ó pọ̀jù lọ ní ìwádìí ti fìdi rẹ̀ múnlẹ̀.
english: Across the United States of America, there are approximately 400,000 known cases of Multiple Sclerosis (MS), leaving it as the leading neurological disease in younger and middle aged adults.

yoruba: MS je aarun ti o ma n se akoba fun aringbungbun eto ainifokanbale ti o niise pelu opolo, ogoro eyin ati opa ofoolo.
english: MS is a disease that affects the central nervous system, which is made up of the brain, the spinal cord and the optic nerve.

yoruba: Ìwádí ti gbé jáde pé obìnrin ní ìlọ́po méjì ma ń sábà ní MS ju okùnrin lọ.
english: Research has found that females are two times more likely to have MS then males.

yoruba: Àwọn tọkọ-taya le pinu pe títọ́ ọmọ kò jẹ àwọn, tàbí ọmọ náà gan lógún.
english: A couple may decide it is not in their best interest, or in the interest of their child, to raise a baby.

yoruba: Àwọn tọkọtaya le yàn láti se ìlanà àti fọmọ wọn fún alágbàtọ..
english: These couples may choose to make an adoption plan for their baby.

yoruba: Ní gbígba ọmọ tọ́, awọn òbí tí ó bí ọmọ náà gangan yóò fagi lé ẹ̀tọ́ tí wọ́n ní gẹ́gẹ́ bí òbí, kí tọkọ-taya mìíràn le è ṣe òbí ọmọ náà.
english: In an adoption, the birth parents terminate their parental rights so that another couple may parent the child.

yoruba: Àlébá sáyẹ́nsì ní láti ṣàwáàrí bí ayé ṣe n ṣiṣẹ́ nípasẹ̀ ìlànà sáyẹ́nsì. Ìlànà yí ló ma n darí àwọn ìwádi sáyẹ́nsì.
english: Science’s main goal is to figure out the way the world works through the scientific method. This method in fact guides most scientific research.

yoruba: Kò dá nìkan wà, ìṣèdánwò, ṣíṣe ìdánwó jẹ́ ìdárawò tí a lò láti pa ìkan tàbí méjì rk nínu àwọn àlàyé ìdáwọ́lé, nípa bibèrè ìbéèrè,ṣíṣe àwọn àkíyèsí tún ṣe ìtọ́sọ́nà ìwádìí.
english: It isn’t alone though, experimentation, and an experiment is a test that is used to eliminate one or more of the possible hypotheses, asking questions, and making observations also guide scientific research.

yoruba: Áfojùsún àwọn alámòdájú àti onímòyẹ ní àwọn ìwé àbáláyé, àti ní pàtàkì, ti Bíbélì àti èdè Latini.
english: Naturalists and philosophers focused on classical texts and, in particular, on the Bible in Latin.

yoruba: Àwọn oìwoye tí wọ́n gbà lórí ìmọ̀ ìjìnlẹ̀ sáyẹ̀nsì àti ìmọ̀ ọpọlọ ni ìwòye Aristotle.
english: Accepted were Aristotle's views on all matters of science, including psychology.

yoruba: Bi imọ awọn Giriki ṣe n dinku, apa iwọ orun ri wipe awọn ko ni nkan ṣe pẹlu ọgbọn imọran ati imọ sayensi ti o fi idi mulẹ ninu Giriki.
english: As knowledge of Greek declined, the West found itself cut off from its Greek philosophical and scientific roots.

yoruba: Ọ̀pọ̀ àwọn ìyọpọ̀ tó má ń wáyé nínú ìsègùn eegun àti isesí ló má ń dálé àwọn ìjẹyọ inú ara àti ìpèse látata àwọn aago biọ́lọ́jì.
english: Many observed rhythms in physiology and behavior often crucially depend on the presence of endogenous cycles and their production through biological clocks.

yoruba: Ìlù tí a lù ní ìgbà kànkan, tí kìń ṣe ìdáhùn sí ìṣe ìgbà kànkan, ní a ti kọsílẹ̀ fún àwọn ènìyàn, pẹ̀lú àwọn kòkòrò àrùn, ewéko, àti àwọn ẹranko.
english: Periodic rhythms, which are not simply responses to external periodic cues, have been documented for most living beings, including bacteria, fungi, plants, and animals.

yoruba: Àwọn ago oníyè máà n sisè fúnrafúnra ẹni nípasè àyíká iná tí o n sisé fún àsìkò ìsisẹ ara eni làísí àfikín láti ìta.
english: Biological clocks are self sustaining oscillators which will continue a period of free-running cycling even in the absence of external cues.

yoruba: Idanwo Esi ati Saasi je ikan ninu awon aba wipe DNA je nkan jenetiki, to siwaju ju.
english: The Hershey and Chase experiment was one of the leading suggestions that DNA was a genetic material.

yoruba: Hershey àti Chase lokòkòrò tàbi àtirú kòkòrò míràn, láti fi ìdí DNA wọn múlẹ̀̀ sínu bacterium.
english: Hershey and Chase used phages, or viruses, to implant their own DNA into a bacterium.

yoruba: Wọ́n se àyẹ̀wò méjì tó sàfihàn bóyá DNA tó wà nínú kòkòrò pẹ̀lú radioactive phosphorus tàbí amáradàgbà inú kòkòrò ara pẹ̀lú radioactive sulfur.
english: They did two experiments marking either the DNA in the phage with a radioactive phosphorus or the protein of the phage with radioactive sulfur.

yoruba: Àwọn ìyípadà lẹ ní àwọn oríṣi àbéjádẹ pèlú dídálórí irú àyípadà tó ba jè, ìse pàtàkì irú nkan àjogúnbá oní ìyẹ̀ tó ní ipa lẹ lórí àti bóyá àwọn seeli tó ní ipa lẹ lórí jẹ àwọn seeli kékẹré ọlọpọ́.
english: Mutations can have a variety of different effects depending on the type of mutation, the significance of the piece of genetic material affected and whether the cells affected are germ-line cells.

yoruba: Awon mutasoni to wa ninu seeli ila-kokoro nikan ni a le koran awon omo inu wa, awon mutasoni to wa ni ibo miin le fa iku-seeli abi arun akan.
english: Only mutations in germ-line cells can be passed on to children, while mutations elsewhere can cause cell-death or cancer.

yoruba: Ìrìnàjò afẹ́ a-jẹmọ́-ìdẹ́dàá máa ń mú kí ìfẹ́ àwọn ènìyàn sí lílọ sí àwọn ibí tí ìṣẹ̀dá pọ̀si wà, kí wọ́n le gbádùn afẹ́fẹ́, ìpalọ́lọ́ to fi mọ́ ewékò àti àwọn ẹrànko ìgbẹ.
english: Nature-based tourism attracts people interested in visiting natural areas for the purpose of enjoying the scenery, including plant and animal wildlife.

yoruba: Apeere aawon ohun sise nibe ni ise ode, eja pipa, aworan yiya, wiwo eye ati bibewo awon ogba idaraya ati kika alaye nipa awon agbegbe eranko ati ewe.
english: Examples of on-site activities include hunting, fishing, photography, bird watching, and visiting parks and studying information about the ecosystem.

yoruba: Àpẹẹrẹ síṣe àbèwọ, yíya awòrán, àti kíkọ́ nípa àwọn ìnànkí ní Borneo.
english: An example is visiting, photographing, and learning about organgatuangs in Borneo.

yoruba: Ni araaro, awon eniyan ma n fi ilu kereje sile pelu oko lati lo si ibi ise, won a si ma pade awon osise yoku ti won n lo se ise nilu ti awon ti kuro.
english: Every morning, people leave small country towns in cars to go their workplace and are passed by others whose work destination is the place they have just left.

yoruba: Nínú ọkọ̀ akérò aláràọ̀tọ̀, gbogbo ènìyàn nì ó lọ́pọ̀ àti ìgbárùkùtì, ìlànà ìrìnlọrìnbọ̀ tó dá l´rí àwọn ọkọ̀ aláàdáni.
english: In this dynamic transport shuttle everyone is somehow connected with, and supporting, a transport system based on private cars.

yoruba: Ìmọ̀ ìjìnlẹ̀ sáyẹ̀nsì ti se àfihàn báyìí wípé ọrọ̀ ajé kábọ̀nù tí mún àyípadà bá ibùgbé láti ọ̀kan nínú àwọn ìpínlè rẹ̀ tó dúróo re èyí tó ti ń sàtìlẹyìn fún ìdàgbàsókè ọmọnìyàn láti bí i mílíọ̀nù méjì ọdún sẹ́yìn.
english: Science now indicates that this massive carbon economy has dislodged the biosphere from one of its stable states that has supported human evolution for the past two million years.

yoruba: Gbogbo àwọn èèyàn ló kópá láwùjọ tí wọ́n tún lo ìlànà ìrìnkèrindò. Ọ̀pọ̀lọpọ̀ àwọn èèyàn ló ń sàríwísí ìlànà ìrìnlọrìnbọ̀.
english: Everyone participates in society and uses transportation systems. Almost everyone complains about transportation systems.

yoruba: Láwọn orílẹ̀èdè tó ti gògè, kò wọ́pọ̀ láti gbọ́ pé wọ́n ń se àríwísí nípa ìpéye omi tàbí pé afára já lulẹ̀.
english: In developed countries you seldom hear similar levels of complaints about water quality or bridges falling down.

yoruba: Kílódé tó jẹ́ pé esun pipo ma n kan fún étò ìrìn àjò, kílódé to jẹ́ wípé wọ ma n pòfo lójojumo? Àb àwọn enjinia o mọ̀Ṣẹ́ ni àbi nkan miin pataki wa níbẹ̀?
english: Why do transportation systems engender such complaints, why do they fail on a daily basis? Are transportation engineers just incompetent? Or is something more fundamental going on?

yoruba: Lílọ-bíbọ̀ ìrìnnà ní ìwádìí ìrìnkèrindò àwọn awakọ kọ̀ọ̀kan àti ipò òkàn kan sí òmíràn ní agbẹdẹ méjì pélù ìbásẹpọ̀ tí wọn ní sí ara wọn.
english: Traffic Flow is the study of the movement of individual drivers and vehicles between two points and the interactions they make with one another.

yoruba: Ó seniláànú wípé, kíkọ nípa lílọ bíbọ̀ ọkọ̀ nira nítori ìhùwàsí awakọ̀ kò se é sọ sílẹ̀ pẹ̀lú ìdájú ìdá ọgọ́rùn ún.
english: Unfortunately, studying traffic flow is difficult because driver behavior cannot be predicted with one-hundred percent certainty.

yoruba: Ó dára wípé àwọn awakọ̀ ma ń ṣọ́ ìwà wù láàrin ìwọ̀n tó ní ìbámú ; nítorí ìdí èyí, ojú pópó ma ń wà ní ìbámu tí ó tọ́ tí a lefi ìṣirò rọ́pò.
english: Fortunately, drivers tend to behave within a reasonably consistent range; thus, traffic streams tend to have some reasonable consistency and can be roughly represented mathematically.

yoruba: Láti tubọ̀ ṣe àfihàn suńkẹrẹ-fàkẹrẹ, wọ́n ṣe àgbékalẹ̀ ìbáṣepọ̀ láàáriń (1) ìsàn (2) ìlóòrin (3) ìsáre.
english: To better represent traffic flow, relationships have been established between the three main characteristics: (1) flow, (2) density, and (3) velocity.

yoruba: Àwọn ìbásòpọ̀ wọ́nyí ma ń gbìyànjú fún ètò ṣíṣe, àrà àkànṣe àrà, àti ìlò ohun èlò ojú ọ̀nà.
english: These relationships help in planning, design, and operations of roadway facilities.

yoruba: Kòkòrò jẹ́ ẹranko àkọ́kọ́ láti gba atẹ́gun. Ipa fífò wọn rànwọ́nlọ́wọ́ láti dojú ìjà kọ àwọn ọ̀tà wọn kí wọ́n sì wá óúnjẹ àti láti bá arawọn sùn dáradára.
english: Insects were the first animals to take to the air. Their ability to fly helped them evade enemies more easily and find food and mates more efficiently.

yoruba: Ọ̀pọ̀lọpọ̀ àwọn kòkòrò ní àǹfàní láti ká ìyẹ́ apá won mọ́ ara.
english: Most insects have the advantage of being able to fold their wings back along the body.

yoruba: Eyi ma n fun won ni asayan pupo ibi ti won le ti sa pamo fun eranko to fe pa won je.
english: This gives them a wider range of small places to hide from predators.

yoruba: Lónìí, kòkòrò kan ṣoṣo, tí kò lè ká ìyẹ́ rẹ̀ papọ̀ ni kòkòrò tí a mọ̀ sí dragoni àti Mayflie.
english: Today, the only insects that cannot fold back their wings are dragon flies and mayflies.

yoruba: Egberun odun seyin, okunrin ti a n pe ni Aristakusu wipe Ona Orun man yi orun ka.
english: Thousands of years ago, a man called Aristarchus said that the Solar System moved around the Sun.

yoruba: Àwọn ẹ̀nìyàn kan rò pé ó jàre, ṣùgbọ́n ọ̀pọ̀ ènìyàn gbàgbọ́ nínú òdì kejì; pé ní ṣe niSolar System n yi yípo ayé, tí ó fi mọ́ òòrùn (ati àwọn ìràwọ̀ mìíràn.
english: Some people thought he was right but many people believed the opposite; that the Solar System moved around the Earth, including the Sun (and even the other stars).

yoruba: Ẹ̀yí mú ọpọlọ wá, torí wípé kò dà bí ẹni wípé pé ayé ń yí, àbí ó dàábẹ̀?
english: This seems sensible, because the Earth doesn't feel as if it's moving, does it?

yoruba: Odò Amazon jé odè to gùn jù tósì tóbi jù lórílè áyè. O gba ìlọ́po mẹjọ omi tí odò kejì tó tóbijù lọ gbà.
english: The Amazon River is the second longest and the biggest river on Earth. It carries more than 8 times as much water as the second biggest river.

yoruba: Amasoni ni odò tó fẹ̀ jùlọ ní ile ayé, nígbàmín ó ma n fẹ̀ tò maili mẹ́fà.
english: The Amazon is also the widest river on Earth, at times six miles wide.

yoruba: Ìdá ogún omi tó dà sílẹ̀ nínú àwọn odò ayé sínú òkun wá láti Amazon.
english: A full 20 percent of the water that pours out of the planet's rivers into the oceans comes from the Amazon.

yoruba: Odò Amazon gangan jẹ́ ẹgbẹ̀rún mẹ́fà ó lé ọ̀rìn lé lọ́ọ̀dúnrún ó lè méje kìlómítà (ẹgbẹ̀rún mẹ́rin ó dín ogún máìlì). Ó gba omi láti ẹgbẹ̀lẹ́gbẹ̀ odò kéèké.
english: The main Amazon River is 6,387 km (3,980 miles). It collects water from thousands of smaller rivers.

yoruba: Lóòótọ̀ àwọn ilé pírámìdì toní òkúta wà títí di òpin ìjọba àkúwórí. píràmìdì Gasa kò jura wọn lọ níye àti ní ìdára ìjìnlẹ̀ kíkọ́ wọn.
english: Although pyramid-building in stone continued until the end of the Old Kingdom, the pyramids of Giza were never surpassed in their size and the technical excellence of their construction.

yoruba: Ijoba titun Ijipti Atijo kun fun iyalenu fun ohun arabara a=ti asaaju won ti o to bii egberun odun nigba naa.
english: New Kingdom ancient Egyptians marvelled at their predecessors monuments, which were then well over a thousand year old.

yoruba: Àwọn èrò ìlú Vatican tó bí i ẹgbẹ̀rin. ó jẹ́ ìlú olómìnira kékeré kan lágbàláayé àti orílèèdè tó ní èrò tó kéré jù.
english: Vatican City's population is around 800. It is the smallest independent country in the world and the country with the lowest population.

yoruba: Ìlú Vatican lo ilẹ̀ Italy nínú ìlànà òfin àti ìbáraẹnisọ̀rọ̀ aláṣẹ.
english: Vatican City uses Italian in its legislation and official communications.

yoruba: Ede Italo ni ede ti gbogbo awon to n sise ni ilu naa ma so, ti ede Latin si je eyi ti won n lo ni ile ijosin.
english: Italian is also the everyday language used by most of those who work in the state while Latin is often used in religious ceremonies.

yoruba: Gbogbo awon olugbe Ilu Fatikani je elesin Katoliki Roomu.
english: All citizens of Vatican City are Roman Catholic.

yoruba: Àwọn èyàn ti mọ̀ nípa àwọn kemika pepe bí wúrà, fàdákà àti kọ́pa àtijọ́, torípé a lè rí wọn ní ìṣẹ̀dá ní ipò àdáyéba wọn, kò dẹ̀ le jù láti wú wọn jáda láti inú ilẹ̀ pẹ̀lú irinṣẹ́ àtilẹ̀bá.
english: People have known about basic chemical elements such as gold, silver, and copper from antiquity, as these can all be discovered in nature in native form and are relatively simple to mine with primitive tools.

yoruba: Arísítótù, fìlọ́sọ́fà, dárò pé ẹ̀kun gbogbo nkan láyé yìí ni ìkan tàbí ọ̀pọ̀ nínu àwọn nkan ohun èlò mẹ́rin. Àwọn ni ilẹ̀, omi, afẹ́fẹ́, iná.
english: Aristotle, a philosopher, theorised that everything is made up of a mixture of one or more of four elements. They were earth, water, air, and fire.

yoruba: Èyí dàbí ìpínlẹ̀ mẹ́rin tó se pàtàkì (ní ìlànà kanáà): Líle, olómi, gáàsì, àti pílásíma, lóòótọ́, ó tún se àyípadà tíọ́rì sí ohun èlò tuntun sóhun tí wọ́n ń rí.
english: This was more like the four states of matter (in the same order): solid, liquid, gas, and plasma, though he also theorised that they change into new substances to form what we see.

yoruba: Àwọn àdàlú nípìlè jẹ́ àpapò àlùrọ méjì tàbí jù bẹ lọ. Láì gbàgbé wípé ọ̀pọ̀lọpọ̀ ìdá lówà lórí àtẹ sáà.
english: Alloys are basically a mixture of two or more metals. Don't forget that there are many elements on the periodic table.

yoruba: Awọn ohun elo bii kasiọmu ati potasiọmu jẹ gẹgẹ bi irin, bẹẹ ni irin bii wura ati fadaka naa wa.
english: Elements like calcium and potassium are considered metals. Of course, there are also metals like silver and gold.

yoruba: O le ni idapọ iriin to ni awọn nkan ti kii ṣe irin diẹ ninu bii kabọni.
english: You can also have alloys that include small amounts of non-metallic elements like carbon.

yoruba: Gbogbo ǹkan ilé àyé ní ó kéré, Gbogbo ǹkn kékeré ní ó wá láti atoms.
english: Everything in the Universe is made of matter. All matter is made of tiny particles called atoms.

yoruba: Atomi je oun iyalenu to je pe trilioni won le wonu ami opin gbolohun yi.
english: Atoms are so incredibly tiny that trillions of them could fit into the period at the end of this sentence.

yoruba: Tori naa pensu naa je ore to dara si eyan pupo nigbati o jade.
english: Thus, the pencil was a good friend to many people when it came out.

yoruba: O je ibanuje pe ona titun lati fikowe ti jade, pensu ti jabo si ipo kekere ati ilo kekere.
english: Sadly, as newer methods of writing have emerged, the pencil has been relegated to lesser status and uses.

yoruba: Awọn ènìyàn tí ń tẹ àtẹ̀jíṣẹ́ lòrí ẹ̀rọ kọ̀ǹpútà wọn, wọn kò nílò láti sunmọ́ ohun mímún mọ.
english: People now write messages on computer screens, never having to come close to a sharpener.

yoruba: Èèyàn a kàn máa rokàn pé kí ni yóò gbẹ̀yìn dùrù nígbàtí kò bá si nǹkan kan.
